title: First Day of Classes for 8-Week Session II
2026 Legacy Speakers Series “Retelling Our Story of Aging: Reimagining the Future through Community, Research, and Action”
description: Join the UNC School of Social Work for our fifth annual Legacy Speakers Series. This year’s event, “Retelling Our Story of Aging: Reimagining the Future through Community, Research, and Action,” invites participants to explore a strengths-based, age-positive view of aging and what it will take to support thriving later in life. Spend the day with scholars, community leaders, and storytellers with lived experience as we examine the landscape of older adults in the United States and North Carolina and consider how communities and systems can respond more effectively. The day will be framed by keynote remarks from Kathy Greenlee, who will offer a national perspective on aging and highlight emerging opportunities to strengthen aging supports through collaboration across communities, research, and systems. Lived-experience storytelling and panel discussions will further explore research-informed practices, policy and system factors, and practical approaches for building more inclusive, age-friendly communities. Don’t miss this engaging event focused on shifting narratives about aging, strengthening community supports, and identifying actions we can take — individually and collectively — to shape a future where people can thrive as they age. You can register for the event here.

title: Harriet Jacobs in the Attic
description:
Join us for an immersive audiovisual experience as we illuminate Harriet Jacobs, a fugitive North Carolina enslaved person who hid in a tiny attic garret for seven years. While hiding she planned her escape, fled to the North, and eventually became a key figure in the abolitionist movement.

In celebration of Black History Month, we invite you to explore a to-scale recreation of Harriet’s attic space and listen to readings from her book, Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl, which was published in 1861.

title: Introduction to Survey Development
description:
This in-person course introduces learners to the foundations of survey development for primary data collection. Students will learn fundamental practices in planning, designing, and conducting data collection using survey research best practices. Core topics will cover:
• sampling and outreach
• questionnaire development (writing questions, response options, and question ordering)
• visual design considerations of electronic and paper surveys
• approaches for testing, and monitoring
Participants will have the opportunity to draft and review survey questions, explore resources for existing survey questions, and review the strengths and limitations of various data collection modes. Registration and fee (refundable for students) are required.

title: Legends & Landmarks: Paintings and Drawings by James C. Horton
description: In commemoration of America's 250th, the Bellamy Mansion Museum is hosting an art exhibition by local artist James C. Visitors can view the exhibit free of charge any time the museum is open (M-Sun. 10 am-4 pm). Please stop by the Carriage ​House Visitor Center to gain admission. Art is available for purchase and an Artist's Reception will be held on February 27th (6-8pm). Visit bellamymansion.org/exhibits for more information or email info@bellamymansion.org if you have questions.

title: The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt
description: The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt showcases how the biblical story of heroic Queen Esther, depicted by Rembrandt and his contemporaries in art and popular culture, expressed ideas of political and religious freedom during the Dutch Golden Age. Charting this history through objects and artworks, The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt provides an original perspective on one of the most studied periods of Dutch prosperity.

description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: Supporting Employee Well-Being: What You Can Do As a Manager
description:
Organizations are investing in benefits to improve employee well-being and taking on initiatives to foster a culture of well-being in the workplace. However, many employees still feel that their job is an obstacle to their well-being rather than a help. What can you do as a manager to shift that perception? Join Learning and Organizational Development for a session that will provide practical ways to show employees that you and your organization support their well-being.

This workshop is part of L&OD's LEAD Events, an initiative that provides employees with opportunities to learn or refresh skills that can help them grow their careers. LEAD Events are a component of L&OD's Leadership Education and Development, or LEAD, program.

title: A Series of Questions: Team Trivia at 321 Coffee
description:

Whether you're a casual quizzer or a seasoned trivia champ, this lunchtime series is the perfect way to test your knowledge and connect with co-workers and friends.

NC State University librarians will serve as hosts of the competition, and trivia master extraordinaire Dave Provost is our question maker.

The winning teams will take home a seasonal 3D printed trophy and a free coffee drink, but the real prize might be the friends we make along the way.

title: March 5 - AI, ML and Computer Vision Meetup
description:
Raleigh AI, Machine Learning and Computer Vision Meetup
Join our virtual meetup to hear talks from experts on cutting-edge topics across AI, ML, and computer vision.

**Date and Location**

Mar 5, 2026
9 - 11 AM Pacific
Online. **[Register for the Zoom!](https://voxel51.com/events/ai-ml-and-computer-vision-meetup-march-5-2026)**

**MOSPA: Human Motion Generation Driven by Spatial Audio**

Enabling virtual humans to dynamically and realistically respond to diverse auditory stimuli remains a key challenge in character animation, demanding the integration of perceptual modeling and motion synthesis. Despite its significance, this task remains largely unexplored. Most previous works have primarily focused on mapping modalities like speech, audio, and music to generate human motion. As of yet, these models typically overlook the impact of spatial features encoded in spatial audio signals on human motion.

To bridge this gap and enable high-quality modeling of human movements in response to spatial audio, we introduce the first comprehensive Spatial Audio-Driven Human Motion (SAM) dataset, which contains diverse and high-quality spatial audio and motion data. For benchmarking, we develop a simple yet effective diffusion-based generative framework for human MOtion generation driven by SPatial Audio, termed MOSPA, which faithfully captures the relationship between body motion and spatial audio through an effective fusion mechanism. Once trained, MOSPA can generate diverse, realistic human motions conditioned on varying spatial audio inputs. We perform a thorough investigation of the proposed dataset and conduct extensive experiments for benchmarking, where our method achieves state-of-the-art performance on this task.

*About the Speaker*

[Zhiyang (Frank) Dou](https://www.linkedin.com/in/zhiyang-frank-dou-0259111b3/) is a Ph.D. student at MIT CSAIL, advised by Prof. Wojciech Matusik. I work with the Computational Design and Fabrication Group and the Computer Graphics Group.

**Securing the Autonomous Future: Navigating the Intersection of Agentic AI, Connected Devices, and Cyber Resilience**

With billions of devices now in our infrastructure and emerging as autonomous agents (AI), we face a very real question: How can we create intelligent systems that are both secure and trusted? This talk will explore the intersection of agentic AI and IoT and demonstrate how the same AI systems can provide robust defense mechanisms. At its core, however, this is a challenge about trusting people with technology, ensuring their safety, and providing accountability. Therefore, creating a new way of thinking is required, one in which security is built in, and where autonomous action has oversight; and, ultimately, innovation leads to greater human well-being.

*About the Speaker*

[Samaresh Kumar Singh](https://www.linkedin.com/in/samaresh-singh-9772ba23/) is an engineering principal at HP Inc. with more than 21 years of experience in designing and implementing large-scale distributed systems, cloud native platform systems, and edge AI / ML systems. His expertise includes agentic AI systems, GenAI / LLMs, Edge AI, federated and privacy preserving learning, and secure hybrid cloud / edge computing.

**Plugins as Products: Bringing Visual AI Research into Real-World Workflows with FiftyOne**

Visual AI research often introduces new datasets, models, and analysis methods, but integrating these advances into everyday workflows can be challenging. FiftyOne is a data-centric platform designed to help teams explore, evaluate, and improve visual AI, and its plugin ecosystem is how the platform scales beyond the core. In this talk, we explore the FiftyOne plugin ecosystem from both perspectives: how users apply plugins to accelerate data-centric workflows, and how researchers and engineers can package their work as plugins to make it easier to share, reproduce, and build upon. Through practical examples, we show how plugins turn research artifacts into reusable components that integrate naturally into real-world visual AI workflows.

*About the Speaker*

[Adonai Vera](https://www.linkedin.com/in/adonai-vera/) \- Machine Learning Engineer & DevRel at Voxel51\. With over 7 years of experience building computer vision and machine learning models using TensorFlow\, Docker\, and OpenCV\.

**Transforming Business with Agentic AI**

Agentic AI is reshaping business operations by employing autonomous systems that learn, adapt, and optimize processes independently of human input. This session examines the essential differences between traditional AI agents and Agentic AI, emphasizing their significance for project professionals overseeing digital transformation initiatives. Real-world examples from eCommerce, insurance, and healthcare illustrate how autonomous AI achieves measurable outcomes across industries. The session addresses practical orchestration patterns in which specialized AI agents collaborate to resolve complex business challenges and enhance operational efficiency. Attendees will receive a practical framework for identifying high-impact use cases, developing infrastructure, establishing governance, and scaling Agentic AI within their organizations.

*About the Speaker*

[Joyjit Roy](https://www.linkedin.com/in/royjoyjit/) is a senior technology and program management leader with over 21 years of experience delivering enterprise digital transformation, cloud modernization, and applied AI programs across insurance, financial services, and global eCommerce.
source: Meetup: Raleigh AI, ML & Computer Vision

title: Urban Heterogeneity Measurements, Forecasts and Uses
description: The Geospatial Forum will host a lecture by Christa Brelsford, a research scientist at the Los Alamos National Laboratory. Brelsford will present research exploring the causes, consequences and determinants of the urban built environment and urban heterogeneity. She will describe a handful of methods for quantifying urban heterogeneity; present an example of a multi-objective optimization framework for describing risk along hydrologic, economic and social dimensions; and then demonstrate an approach for quantifying trade-offs between different objectives when designing observation networks of the system.

title: From Data Governance to Semantic Intelligence
description:
Data Science Raleigh
As AI adoption accelerates, many enterprises are hitting the same wall: traditional approaches to data governance, metadata management, and cataloging were not designed to support AI and AI agents operating at scale. Fragmented tools, siloed semantics, and inconsistent definitions make it difficult for both people and AI systems to trust and act on data.

In this webinar, industry expert Mike Ferguson and Collate co-founder and CEO Suresh Srinivas explore how leading organizations are evolving from tool-centric data catalogs and governance toward semantic intelligence, a unified approach that brings governance, context, and meaning together to support analytics, automation, and AI at scale.

Collate co-founder and CTO Harsha Chintalapani will also provide a brief demonstration showing how semantic intelligence is applied in practice, including how data teams design and build AI agents on governed enterprise data.

**What You'll Learn:**

1️⃣ Why data discovery, governance, lineage, and data quality and observability must work together to support AI-driven use cases

2️⃣ How fragmented semantic layers and tool-specific context limit trust, scale, and reuse across analytics and AI

3️⃣What semantic intelligence means in practice and how it extends beyond traditional data catalogs

4️⃣How shared meaning enables secure, governed access to data for both people and AI agents

5️⃣How semantic intelligence supports the delivery of trusted, consumable data products with meaning, quality signals, and governance that are ready for analytics and AI agents

6️⃣What it takes to make data truly AI-ready without sacrificing control, compliance, or consistency

Designed for data leaders, enterprise architects, and governance teams, this session explains why existing catalogs and governance tools remain useful—but are no longer sufficient on their own—and how enterprises can operationalize shared meaning to support analytics, data engineering, and AI initiatives at scale.

**[Register here](https://hubs.ly/Q042D1NJ0)**

title: Ballet With Oliver Beres
location: Wellness and Recreation Center and Carmichael Gym, 2307
description:
Ballet teacher Oliver Beres will lead a class that will focus on the Vaganova technique with a blend of Balanchine style. The class will work on placement, technique and turnout, with a strong emphasis on musicality. The session will begin with intermediate barre work, starting at plies and finishing with grand battements. Center exercises and across-the-floor combinations that will incorporate a mix of adagio, turning combinations and jumping will also be part of the session.

Béres was born in Hungary and began his dance training in 1996 at age 11 when he was accepted into the Hungarian National Dance Academy. During his education, Béres was one of the four students chosen to perform with the Hungarian National Ballet. He was an apprentice in the company from age 14 until he graduated from the school in 2005. During his nine years of education, he had the privilege of training under many great teachers, including Zoltan Solymosi, who was a principal dancer with the Royal Ballet. Beres has performed in several countries in Europe, including Romania, Slovakia, Poland, Czech Republic, Austria and Italy. After graduation, he was offered a full scholarship with the North Carolina Dance Theatre in Charlotte, which brought him to the United States. He has danced with the Hungarian National Ballet, Slovak State Opera House, New Jersey Ballet and Carolina Ballet. He has performed several soloist and principal roles, including Firebird, Swan Lake, Don Quixote and many more. He retired as a soloist with Carolina Ballet in 2017 after dancing with the company for nine years. Béres is currently a full-time ballet teacher.

title: Andrews Launch Accelerator: Launch Sessions
description:
The Andrews Launch Accelerator's Launch Sessions are designed to help ventures articulate, validate and communicate their value to key programs and stakeholders. The schedule for the Launch Sessions is below. All sessions will be held virtually.

Session 1 (Feb. 26): Join us for an introduction to the Andrews Launch Accelerator, the Launch Sessions and the first core principle: customer discovery.

Session 2 ( March 5): This session will explore the often-overlooked fundamentals of customer discovery.  It will focus heavily on understanding your customers' frustrations, dissatisfactions and unfulfilled desires. The session will also delve into collecting evidence of these things through unbiased discovery work.

Session 3 (March 12): You'll learn about the methods entrepreneurs use to rapidly validate demand for what they are working on without spending a lot of money and time.

Session 4 (March 26): With just a few days left before the Andrews Launch Accelerator applications are due on March 31, this session will be an open-floor Q&A on anything related to the Andrews Launch Accelerator, including applications.

title: Right to Be's Bystander Intervention to Support the Disability Community in Public Spaces
description:
REGISTER HERE: https://righttobe.org/events/bystander-intervention-to-support-the-disability-community-in-public-spaces-6/




The 75-minute, interactive training will teach you the 5Ds of bystander intervention methodology. We’ll start by talking about the types of disrespect that people with disabilities or those perceived to be with disabilities are facing right now — from microaggressions to violence — using a tool we call the “spectrum of disrespect.” You will learn what to look for and the positive impact that bystander intervention has on individuals and communities. We’ll talk through five strategies for intervention: distract, delegate, document, delay, and direct; and how to prioritize your own safety while intervening.


We’ll have time at the end for practice, and you’ll leave feeling more confident intervening the next time you see ableism or disability harassment online or in person.

title: Film Screening With Afloat Filmmaker and NC State Alum Tae Park
description:
What if you could erase all your pain? Would you do it? Afloat, a film that follows Owen, a young man burdened by shame, fear and trauma, who returns home and takes an experimental pill meant to erase emotional pain. What he hopes will heal him only makes things worse. Written and directed by Tae Park, a North Carolina filmmaker and NC State alum, and with cast and crew from the Triangle’s creative scene, Afloat explores the quiet, invisible battles of healing. 

The film's runtime is 26 minutes. It will be followed by a Q&A with Park.

title: Thursday Community Board Game night at Moondog Meadery
description:
Durham Geeks
In conjunction with Moon Dog Meadery, we continue to have our Thursday Night Gaming Night from 7 until they kick us out. It's a bit smaller group than the large Monday night extravaganza, and there are a lot of drop in and independent groups here as well. Restaurants are open on Thursdays, so you can Dash you food right inside, or bring it with you!

Moondog has an extensive and ever-changing collection of games that are usually loaned out for a fee, but on Thursdays (just like on Mondays), they are all free to play. Usually we can find folks to fill in your table or teach you the basics of the games. I've been hosting it for a few months now, and we have had a great time with students returning and bringing in new colleagues, as well as the faithful ones who attended over the summer.

Dogs and outside food both allowed and encouraged. Always free! There is award-winning mead on tap and bottled, as well as an ever-changing collection of beer, cider, THC-infused water, colas and, yes, just plain old water to keep you hydrated on your adventure.

Any questions? Contact us here at Durham Geeks!

description: A general meeting of the UNC-Chapel Hill Employee Forum. - ONYEN protected. Online meeting RSVP is limited to UNC Employees only. External guests please email Matt Banks for Zoom meeting information by 8 a.m. on meeting date at employeeforum@unc.edu.

title: Harriet Jacobs in the Attic
description:
Join us for an immersive audiovisual experience as we illuminate Harriet Jacobs, a fugitive North Carolina enslaved person who hid in a tiny attic garret for seven years. While hiding she planned her escape, fled to the North, and eventually became a key figure in the abolitionist movement.

In celebration of Black History Month, we invite you to explore a to-scale recreation of Harriet’s attic space and listen to readings from her book, Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl, which was published in 1861.

title: Women of Worth Conference
location: The Frank Porter Graham Student Union
description:
The Women of Worth Conference returns in 2026, offering space for learning, dialogue, and connection across campus and community.

This year’s conference brings together all students, faculty, staff and community members for dynamic speakers, interactive workshops, and meaningful networking.

title: AI Literacy Workshop II
description:
Are you ready to level up your artificial intelligence skills ethically, creatively and practically?

The College of Humanities and Social Sciences invites students from all majors and backgrounds who want to better understand generative AI and how it's used in communication, strategy and real-world contexts to attend its AI Literacy Workshop. If you complete the workshop, you'll receive a certificate of completion.

Registration is required. The first 30 registrants will receive a free $50 learning kit and a complimentary lunch. Register today.

title: AI Assisted Surveys
description:
This course will be held online only over 3 days. Attendance is required as it will not be recorded.

This 6-hour course provides training on how AI can assist a survey project from beginning to the end.

We will focus on each step of the research process (Callegaro and Yang, 2025)

PlanningExecutionActivationIn the planning phase there are two sub phases
1) Ideate and define

AI can be an idea helper and assist on generating research ideas starting from previously knowledgeA literature review can definitely be assisted by AI and there are specific tools available for that2) Design and plan

The design stage is where AI can assist in producing design brief, analysis plan and map the concepts that are going to be measured using the questionnaireThe execution phase consists of
3) Data wizard

Here AI has been used to help drafting and pretesting the questionnaire, help writing survey material such as survey invitation and confidentiality assurances.4) Analysis copilot

Once the data collection is under way, AI can assist in automating, and writing statistical code to analyze the dataset (stat code assistant). It can also be used as  theme discovery, for coding of open ended answers and data visualization.The activation phase consists of:
5) Insight partner

Help summarize findings, generate reports, help review the final deliverables such as a research report or a complete academic paper6) Storytelling aid

Develop presentation materials, create multi-media artifacts, generated blog post for different platforms, generate images based on content from the researchOrganizational Structure of the Course 
The course will be taught in a workshop style where the lectures will be using presentation slides as a roadmap. After each concept has been introduced and discussed, the participants will be invited to work on brief hands on exercises with different LLMs such as Gemini, Claude, Copilot, and ChatGPT

Participants will be strongly encouraged to ask questions during lectures, especially on how the content relates to their own research.

Learning objectives
By the end of the course, you will:

Learn how to prompt LLMs for survey research help and assistanceLearn how to evaluate the quality of LLMs outputsStrengths and challenges of different LLMs toolsWhere LLMs are not necessaryEthical usage of LLMs/AI ToolsRecent debate of using LLMs to produce research and write academic papersRegistration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: Bennett Place School Day
description:
Bennett Place hosts several historic sites and museums for a history-packed School Day program. Students rotate through several hands-on stations focusing not just on the Civil War, but many aspects of North Carolina's long, rich history.

$2 per student payable on the morning of the program.

Advanced registration recommended - space limited. Please use the linked website to register.

title: The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt
description: The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt showcases how the biblical story of heroic Queen Esther, depicted by Rembrandt and his contemporaries in art and popular culture, expressed ideas of political and religious freedom during the Dutch Golden Age. Charting this history through objects and artworks, The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt provides an original perspective on one of the most studied periods of Dutch prosperity.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: Games and Learning Webinar
description: Games can be an excellent way to promote student engagement. Attend a workshop hosted by the Graduate School to learn how to apply game design principles in learning environments and how to implement different examples of the principles in your classroom.

title: Paying off Debt While Building Wealth
description:
In difficult economic times, many people are struggling to just keep up with monthly bills, let alone make progress on debt repayment. What suffers most is contributing to savings and investing goals. This session will focus on developing financial plans and strategies to address the dual goals of having a plan for paying off debt, while also contributing to building personal wealth.
This session covers the basics (or essentials) of each area and describes practical techniques that can help you to formulate your own plan to manage your personal finances to organize a sensible debt repayment plan without sacrificing other financial goals.

 

Workshop Objectives:

-Recognize the importance of a practical debt repayment strategy
-Understand the importance of setting and tracking short- and long-term financial goals
-Learn how to set milestones to address debt repayment and savings
-Be able to prioritize most effective use of disposable income
-Differentiate between saving and investing and when each makes sense

 

Click on Carolina Talent registration link and select the “Request” button to automatically enroll.

title: Artemis II Launch Party
location: North Carolina Museum of Natural Sciences
description:
Join us as we cheer on Artemis II, NASA’s first crewed Moon Mission in more than 50 years. NASA astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover and Christina Koch, as well as Jeremy Hansen from the Canadian Space Agency, will become the first people to return to the Moon since Apollo 17. Celebrate North Carolina’s own astronaut, Christina Koch, as she becomes the first woman to orbit the Moon.

Registration is required. Space is limited. Launch date and time are subject to change by NASA. Current launch windows are between 8PM–1AM on select dates through April. Register now to secure your spot and receive watch party details and go/no-gos via email.

title: Durant birding -  Registration required
location: Durant Nature Preserve, 8305 Camp Durant Rd, Raleigh, NC 27614, USA
description:
Early Bird at Durant Nature Preserve (registration required off Meetup)
Saturday, MARCH 7, 2026, 8 am - 9:30 am
Register Off-site only: Requires a login and password:
Login FIRST at https://ncraleighweb.myvscloud.com/webtrac/web/splash.html
THEN register for this free event at:
https://ncraleighweb.myvscloud.com/webtrac/web/iteminfo.html?Module=AR&FMID=441383870
Or click on Search -  Keyword Search and type in "EARLY BIRD"

Grab your binoculars and meet us at beautiful Durant Nature Preserve’s SOUTH entrance for a bird-watching hike led by Wake Audubon naturalist, Lynn Erla Beegle. Spend your morning watching and learning about birds that prefer forests and thickets edges on this approximately 1.5 mile hike. For beginning and advanced birders. Bring your binoculars, or borrow a pair of ours. Pre-registration is required. Limited to City of Raleigh residents.

Meet in the SOUTH parking lot at Durant Nature Preserve at 3237 Spottswood Street near Campbell Lodge. Note there is also a north parking lot on Camp Durant Road, but, you cannot drive from one lot to the other through the park .

description: The annual Career Crawl hosted by the Graduate Association of Public Administrators will take place from 9 a.m. to 1 p.m. on Saturday, March 7, in the Caldwell Hall lounge. This interactive event will feature alumni networking opportunities, resume workshops and free professional headshots — all designed to help students prepare for internships, job searches and future career paths. Refreshments will be available.

title: Illuminate the Song
description: In this hands-on University Theatre workshop, you'll learn how to use the fundamentals of lighting design to create and program a light show for a five- to 10-minute-long song. The music will be provided. Your task will be to make the light show come to life through movement, color and the texture of the lighting rig.

title: LGBTQ+ Beginner Bird
location: Annie Louise Wilkerson, MD Nature Preserve Park, 5229 Awls Haven Dr, Raleigh, NC 27614, USA
description:
*Complete this form to register. *This free workshop requires registration OFF Meetup.

LGBTQ+  Beginner Birders Program with Wake Audubon, Field Inclusive, Inc. and Raleigh Parks.

Date: Saturday, March 7, 2026

Time: 9:00* a.m. – 12:15 p.m. (*Please arrive 15 minutes early to reach the Education Center from parking and check-in; arrive 8:40 a.m. if also borrowing binoculars),

Location: Annie Louise Wilkerson, MD Nature Preserve Park, 5229 Awls Haven Dr., Raleigh, NC 27614

Hosting this event expressly for the LGBTQ+ community are workshop sponsors Wake Audubon and Field Inclusive, Inc. in partnership with City of Raleigh Parks and the LGBT Center of Raleigh. Collectively we’re striving for everyone to feel welcome, safe and included in the natural sciences and outdoors.

‘Birding’ – or the observation of birds in their natural habitats as a hobby – has become one of the most popular outdoor activities. If you are taking a new interest in birds and looking for help getting started, come develop your skills together with others at our Beginning Birding workshop.

This free public program teaches the basics of bird identification; how to use binoculars, guides and apps; types of bird safety and ways to bird safely; and the importance of inclusion and allyship in birding and the outdoors. We’ll spend time outside putting your learning to quick use.

Our route outside will consist of a mix of paved, gravel, and grassy surfaces with slight gradients. All-gendered restrooms will be available.

No binoculars? No problem! We have plenty of loaners to spare.

Note: Participation may be capped due to space restrictions, at which time notice of a wait list will be added to the top of the registration form. Please continue to register to join the waitlist. Should we receive cancellations, we’ll right away invite those on waitlist to confirm in the order registrations were received.

Reach Wake Audubon board director and Equity, Diversity, Inclusion and Belonging co-chair Rick LaRose at rlarose.was@gmail.com with any questions.

We look forward to building your beginner birding skills together with you on March 7!

title: Bountiful Containers: The Art of Small Space Foodscaping
description:
Get ready to maximize every inch and savor the joys of cultivating your favorite plants in compact spaces. Dive into the secrets of designing and nurturing container foodscapes that deliver year-round beauty and abundant harvests. From sun-kissed herbs to luscious vegetables, author and horticulturist Brie Arthur has got you covered.

Discover the recipe for success as Arthur spills the beans on ideal soil blends, optimal fertilization and watering practices that will nurture your plants to perfection. Unleash your creativity with ingenious planting combinations that celebrate both form and function with a special container planting session after Arthur's talk. You'll design your own foodscaping container to take home to your special sweetheart or to feed your heart with some tasty winter vegetables.

You must register to attend the lecture. Registration includes Arthur's lecture and all supplies needed to plant a container of winter vegetables to take home.

title: Signs of Fascism Visibility Event at Peace & Justice Plaza
location: 179 E Franklin St, Chapel Hill, NC, 27514
description: Please join Engaged Defenders for Democracy for a Signs of Fascism event. We will meet at Peace & Justice Plaza in Chapel Hill (179 E Franklin Street) to raise awareness about elements of fascism that we are seeing in our country today. We will hold signs written with signs of fascism spaced out about 25 feet apart along Franklin Street. This event is being held in collaboration the Community Church of Chapel Hill's Day of Service. Please choose one or two shifts. We are asking folks to commit to holding a sign for 45 minutes (you can sign up for both shifts), arriving at 9:30 am to get set up and holding your sign from 9:45-10:30 am, or arriving at 10:15 am and holding a sign from 10:30-11:15 am. Before or after your shift, we will have handouts to pass out to pedestrians with information about how people can combat the fascism we see through specific actions. Check in at Peace & Justice Plaza to get your sign and instructions. You will also receive an email with more detailed instructions closer to the event.  Children are welcome but must be supervised by an adult at all times. Limited street parking available downtown - paid parking decks are available. More information here: https://parkonthehill.com/public-parking/ Carpooling or public transportation encouraged. Please join our Substack: https://substack.com/profile/115484101-engaged-defenders-4-democracy For more ED4D Mobilize Events: https://www.mobilize.us/engagedefendersfordemocracy/ https://www.mobilize.us/engagedefendersfordemocracy/event/902116/

title: A Town is Born. When Are We US?
description: A Town is Born. When Are We US? Story of why and where the town of Bath was founded, became the North Carolina's first colonial port of entry, held first library, and lay on the first Post Road. We will look at its role in the region, its local history that feeds into the story of independence seven decades later, and Founders of Bath relate how this area was selected, and their roles in its development.

title: Alamance County America 250 Celebration
description:
Alamance County America 250 Celebration       March 7 10-4

Save the Date! The Alamance County America 250 NC Celebration will take place at Alamance Battleground on Saturday, March 7th.
Join us for an inspiring day featuring engaging lectures and living history demonstrations, all in celebration of the upcoming 250th anniversary of America’s independence.
More details to come—stay tuned!

title: Boomtown Bash
description:
Step back in time to the bustling 1850s with Reed Gold Mine’s new immersive living history event, "Boomtown Bash: Life at a Carolina Gold Mine." Focused on the heyday of North Carolina’s gold mining history, this event offers a captivating look into the lives of the miners, immigrants, and women who sought adventure and riches.

The outdoor Panning Area also be open for the event (weather permitting)! After learning about gold mining in the 19th century, try your hand at panning in our troughs and see if you have what it takes to strike it rich yourself. Panning tickets are $5.00 each, plus tax, for anyone ages 8 and older. Tickets are sold for set session times on a first come, first served basis in our Visitor Center.

Visitors of all ages will enjoy an unforgettable experience at “Boomtown Bash” by being transported back in time to the 1850s and reliving the spirit of adventure and pursuit of wealth that defined North Carolina’s Piedmont region until the dawn of the Civil War. Don’t miss your chance to learn more about America’s first Golden State and discover the forgotten history of North Carolina’s gold mining heritage.

“Boomtown Bash” will be held from 10:00 am to 4:00 pm on Saturday, March 7th.

Event admission is $5.00 for anyone aged 5 and up, payable on the event day. We accept cash and cards, excluding American Express. Children under 5 are free. Registration is not accepted.

title: Kinston's Civil War Experience: Homefront, Sailors, and Soldiers
description:
🤔🏛️ Curious about Kinston during the Civil War? 

Are you curious about the businesses that operated in downtown Kinston during the Civil War? Did you know Kinston was home to a Free Black community during this pivotal time? Ever wondered what daily life was like for women and children while men were away at war?

🪖💭 Were any battles fought in Kinston?
⚓ Do you know where the CSS Neuse ironclad was outfitted—and who served aboard her?
📜 What were the two largest Civil War battles fought in North Carolina?

If these questions spark your interest, join us for a day of exploration into Lenoir County’s history during the 1860s!

Throughout the museum, our knowledgeable staff and volunteers will bring history to life with living history demonstrations, interactive discussions, and helpful resources—all designed to answer your questions and deepen your understanding of life in North Carolina during the Civil War.

🎖️👩‍👩‍👦‍👦⚓
Perfect for families, history enthusiasts, and curious minds of all ages—this event offers a meaningful and memorable way to connect with the past.

Explore, learn, and experience history where it happened. We can’t wait to welcome you!

🎟️ Admission Rates:

$5 – Adults$4 – Senior Citizens, Active Duty Military, and Veterans$3 – StudentsFree – Children ages three and under📧 Questions?
Email Rachel at rachel.kennedy@dncr.nc.gov or call 252-526-9600, ext. 221 or 222.

title: "Her Heart is Slowly Breaking:" a Women's History Tour of Stagville - 2026
description:
Explore the resistance, resilience, love, and labor of enslaved women on one of the largest plantations in North Carolina.

This March, visit Historic Stagville for a special guided tour of the Bennehan House and grounds featuring the stories of enslaved women from Stagville. This tour highlights the lives of Phoebe Bennehan, Anna Bell, Emma Turner Henderson, and more. Their personal stories, reconstructed with careful research, illuminate the experiences of countless women who endured bondage in North Carolina. 

This tour will be offered each Saturday in March, at 11 am, in recognition of Women's History Month.

This tour is intended for adult visitors. It includes content about violence, sexual violence, and family separation. Tours will last about 1 hour. The tour will include walking and standing, both indoors and outdoors, including walking over grass, gravel, and uneven surfaces.

Space is limited. Register now to reserve your space! Tickets are $10 per person plus tax, payable by card when you check-in for the tour on site.

title: First Saturday Birding at Brumley
location: Brumley North Nature Preserve, 3620 Old State Highway 10, Chapel Hill, NC, 27514, United States
description:
Come join us for our First Saturday birding hike at our Brumley North Nature Preserve!
Did you know that Brumley North is the #2 Hotspot for birding in the state? Our Brumley Birding hike will be led by co-members of both TLC and the New Hope Bird Alliance. We will meander on an easy 1.5 mile hike over about 2 hours with many stops along the way. Please wear good walking shoes and layers, bring a water bottle, and binoculars if you have them. We suggest that you leave your furry friends at home for this hike as they may scare away wildlife viewing opportunities. This is a free event, but preregistration is required.

description:
The Durham Farmers’ Market is a community of farmers, growers and crafters. We are strongly committed to local products: every item you see at the market was produced by the people selling it, and it all comes from no more than 70 miles away. 
The Durham Farmers’ Market open Saturdays mornings for local produce, flowers, crafts and more! The Wednesday Market is open from 3-6pm Wednesdays, Mid-April through Mid-October 
If you’d like more information about this week’s vendor list, SNAP benefits and map, visit durhamfarmersmarket.com 

We are happy to offer Double Bucks to our SNAP clients. This means is a SNAP client has $10, they receive $20 in tokens to spend at the winter market.

Estamos muy contentos de ofrecer Double Bucks a nuestros clientes de SNAP. Esto significa que si un cliente de SNAP gasta $ 10, recibirá $ 20 en tokens para gastar en productos frescos.


Additionally, we offer a cash match program for other qualifying folks. To receive this benefit, simply bring cash to the info table. Then, we’ll double the amount with tokens. For example, $10 in cash equals $20 in tokens.

También ofrecemos un programa de contrapartida en efectivo para nuestros beneficiarios de SNAP que no tienen beneficios durante el mes, clientes de WIC y miembros de la comunidad que viven en viviendas públicas. Para recibir este beneficio, simplemente traiga efectivo a la tabla de información y duplicaremos la cantidad con tokens. Por ejemplo, $ 10 en efectivo equivalen a $ 20 en fichas.

title: Crafting Botanicals with Paper: Trilliums
location: North Carolina Botanical Garden, 100 Old Mason Farm Road, Chapel Hill, NC, 27517, United States
description:
Register Here

with Cynthia Woodsong, Artist 
Date: Saturday, March 7, 2026
Time: 10:00 a.m. – 2:30 p.m.
Location: North Carolina Botanical Garden
Fee: $57 ($52 Members) 
  
In celebration of spring ephemerals, this paper botanical workshop focuses on trilliums, which emerge in March and typically bloom through April. Trillium catesbaei (rosy wakerobin) and Trillium discolor (pale yellow trillium) will be featured.  Participants will use crepe paper to make a life-size replica of two trillium plants while learning about the botanical characteristics of this genus. Participants of previous workshops will learn a new technique for rendering leaves. 
No previous experience with making paper botanicals is needed, but the activity requires the use of scissors and good finger dexterity. 
Materials will be provided to complete the blooming stems, and tools will be available for use during the workshop. There will be a 30-minute break for lunch (bring your own). 
  
Register Here

title: Poles and Plants
location: Johnston Mill Nature Preserve, 2713 Mt. Sinai Rd., Chapel Hill, North Carolina, 27514, United States
description:
Join us for an educational hike about plants and hiking poles!
During this hike you’ll join TLC Trail Guide, Nancy, to learn how and when to set the length of your poles, the most efficient way to grip your poles, and how to decide what features you want in your hiking poles.  Along the way, TLC Trail Guide, Dale, will point out local tree and plant species you will see throughout the preserve. This is a great hike to build on both your hiking pole and plant ID skills!

This will be a moderate 2-3 mile hike over variable terrain, stopping periodically for educational moments. Another big thank you to Great Outdoor Provision Company and LEKI for letting us borrow poles for you to try, but if you have a pair already, please bring them! This is a free event, but preregistration is required.

 



 
If registration for this event is full, please fill out our waitlist form here, but March 6th at noon. 
 

Current Mask Policy: TLC seeks to build a supportive, mask-friendly environment in all collaborations with staff and the public. Please wear a mask if you are not vaccinated, or if that makes you feel most comfortable. We also encourage those eligible to be vaccinated to get their Covid-19 vaccine and booster shot. If you are experiencing any Covid-related symptoms, please stay home and attend a program another day. Our top priority is to create a safe environment for our community. title: Drafts of the Bull: Durham Craft Beer Tour
description:
Chapel Hill And Durham Fun with Friends (CHAD) 30s 40s 50s+
**NOTE:** You will need to register for the event on the Flavors of the Bull website. We are holding the date just for our group. Please go to [www.flavorsofthebull.com,](http://www.flavorsofthebull.com,/) click the "Book now" link in the upper right corner and then search for March 7th.

**Use promo code Meetup10 to save 10% off the cost of the tour**.

Join us for a craft beer tour in downtown Durham! Discover the heart of Durham’s craft beer culture with an immersive brewery tour that blends rich flavor and local history. Whether you’re a seasoned beer lover or simply curious, this tour offers an authentic taste of the city—full of character, craftsmanship, and that unmistakable Durham charm. Cheers to an unforgettable experience!

The tour begins at [Clouds Brewing](https://click.c.alumni.umich.edu/?qs=2496befc4dc0a0c62bf4352772a97ad930134646a5ed4de9c969af9933bf41ffa0e8316d877e42d862e08c96c84d3643908dfc34dcb896da), where they’ve been brewing since 2014 focusing on German-inspired, American-made bold brews and pub fare. From there, we’ll head to [Crank Arm Brewing](https://click.c.alumni.umich.edu/?qs=2496befc4dc0a0c64e906c9c0b5a717e2464ad075c6e17a0e62cccc6af0967f391c3e80975c513078d5bbfb417a040b26bac936a9c2d10a4), a place that’s been focused on bikes and beer since 2013. The next stop is [Flying Bull Beer Company](https://click.c.alumni.umich.edu/?qs=2496befc4dc0a0c6601bd6092f5dc59158eb234652fe945b3c6d0e86479881465ef975cb5351d44914f954fda6725c53f6d3961e2d475b13), which is focused on building community here in Durham through warm hospitality, great food, and even better beer. The last stop is [Atomic Clock Brewing Company](https://click.c.alumni.umich.edu/?qs=2496befc4dc0a0c64eaaf0b2b92897f2c1b273b3e03480ef9cde87d54ff71d2cd57a36580d5ebbc384249c14dcaaa927f37a1f27f5882ff0), known for innovative brews and a focus on perfection.

This tour is offered through Flavors of the Bull, a brand-new food and drink tour company in Durham (and started by a Michigan alum). Tours are $75/per person and include flights at each stop, food tastings throughout the tour, and local Durham history provided as we walk to each stop. Tours are limited to 15 people.

NOTE: You will need to register for the event on the Flavors of the Bull website. We are holding the date just for our group. Please go to [www.flavorsofthebull.com,](http://www.flavorsofthebull.com,/) click the "Book now" link in the upper right corner and then search for March 7th.

Use promo code Meetup10 to to save 10% off the cost of the tour.

Their 10 a.m. monthly basic maintenance class is a great way to learn the essentials of bike care and tune-ups. Each class is shaped by the needs of those who show up with no two being the same.

description: In commemoration of America's 250th, the Bellamy Mansion Museum is hosting an art exhibition by local artist James C. Horton, which includes over 40 original paintings and drawings that highlight historic scenes and places from around the region and country that help tell America's story. Visitors can view the exhibit free of charge any time the museum is open (M-Sun. 10 am-4 pm). Please stop by the Carriage ​House Visitor Center to gain admission. title: The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt
description: The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt showcases how the biblical story of heroic Queen Esther, depicted by Rembrandt and his contemporaries in art and popular culture, expressed ideas of political and religious freedom during the Dutch Golden Age. Charting this history through objects and artworks, The Book of Esther in the Age of Rembrandt provides an original perspective on one of the most studied periods of Dutch prosperity.

title: Exhibit Reception: The Whimsical Naturalist
description:
Meet the artist and enjoy light refreshments at the exhibit reception for The Whimsical Naturalist at the North Carolina Botanical Garden on Sunday, March 8, from 1-3 p.m.!

 

In this exhibit of original watercolors, North Carolina artist Katie Crawford celebrates the rich biodiversity of the East Coast, telling the story of interconnectedness between species, ecosystems, and communities. Through portraits of native plants and animals, she uses anthropomorphism to shift perspectives and inspire visitors to consider how we are connected to our environment and each other.

 

The reception takes place in and around the DeBerry Gallery, in our main education center, and is open to all.

 

Reminder: Sunday, March 8 is the first day of Daylight Savings Time!

title: Phone Bank with Ground Truth
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47683000/1:c425c4ac6501a985c2d94dac086619377da5afe7ed7ad6b8244f69d38a7be3df/

Join Swing Left for a Ground Truth phone bank—designed to fix what’s broken in Democratic organizing. We’re launching an innovative new program that will revolutionize how we connect with voters over the phone. 

Our goal: to help Democrats rebuild trust, win tough races, and flip the House. We're bringing people together to build the community of real people, having real conversations with real people. During Ground Truth phone banks, we will talk to as many people as possible and focus on listening. 

At this phone bank, we’ll:

- Call ALL types of voters -- not just those who vote like us.

- Have real conversations that LISTEN deeply to voters’ concerns.

- Gather insights to share with campaigns, so they can be responsive to voters’ needs and smarter with their messaging, targeting, and outreach.

Don’t worry if it’s your first time—training, materials, and support are all provided. Just come prepared with your phone and laptop or computer!

We recommend using earbuds or using speakerphone to free up your hands as well! 

––

About the program:

This isn’t traditional phone banking—it’s a whole new way of talking to voters. Together, we’ll gather authentic insights from communities, use cutting-edge tools to capture what we hear, and share it back with campaigns to strengthen their efforts.

Why we’re starting early: We launched a year ahead of the midterms to build power before campaigns get off the ground.

How we’ll go big: Calling every voter, not just the ones we normally rely on, so every conversation builds a more complete picture of the electorate.

How we’ll go deep: Practicing empathetic listening and real conversations that uncover voters’ “Ground Truth,” not just checking boxes on a script.

Paid for by Swing Left, Swingleft.org, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

How to prepare:
Thanks for signing up to join the Ground Truth phone banking program! Together, we can transform how we talk with voters, rebuild trust, and swing the most competitive races.

There will be a training at the beginning of the phone bank, so don't worry if this is your first time.  

Please be sure to have your laptop/ computer charged and ready to go!

See you there!

https://www.mobilize.us/swingleft/event/872281/

title: Harriet Jacobs in the Attic
description:
Join us for an immersive audiovisual experience as we illuminate Harriet Jacobs, a fugitive North Carolina enslaved person who hid in a tiny attic garret for seven years. While hiding she planned her escape, fled to the North, and eventually became a key figure in the abolitionist movement.

In celebration of Black History Month, we invite you to explore a to-scale recreation of Harriet’s attic space and listen to readings from her book, Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl, which was published in 1861.

title: Mental Health Seminar: The Healing Power of Presence - Mindfulness for Stress and Life Satisfaction
description: What does it really mean to be present, and why does it matter for both our own well-being and the health of our communities? This seminar explores how mindfulness and presence can support mental and emotional health in everyday life. Through practical examples, guided reflection, and accessible mindfulness practices, participants will learn how cultivating awareness can reduce stress, improve emotional regulation, and increase overall life satisfaction. Beyond individual benefits, the session will examine how presence fosters empathy, stronger relationships, and more thoughtful engagement with others. Participants will leave with simple, usable tools for integrating mindfulness into daily routines and for building healthier, more connected communities through intentional awareness. This seminar is open to everyone, whether you are new to mindfulness or looking to deepen your existing practice.

title: Book Talk with Matt Kaplan
location: The Frank Porter Graham Student Union, Auditorium
description:
Broken Beakers and Brilliant Minds: Fixing The Machinery of Science 

  

From the energy crisis and feeding eight billion people to defeating cancer and coping with climate change, humanity faces some rather large challenges today. To tackle them, we need science running like a well-oiled machine. Unfortunately, it has more often resembled a clunky old engine: loud, temperamental and prone to breaking down at the worst possible moment. 

  

It is tempting to blame recent funding cuts for science’s current woes, but focusing only on money rather misses the point. Many of science’s biggest problems have been around for years, sometimes centuries. Scientists have long been criticized, sidelined or attacked for having ideas that were too new, too inconvenient or simply too correct for the people in power at the time. Even truly unscientific behaviors, like character assassination and fraud, are not modern inventions… though they do seem to have enjoyed a recent renaissance. 

  

The good news is that longstanding problems are not the same as unsolvable ones. Join Matt Kaplan, science correspondent at The Economist and author of I Told You So! Scientists Who Were Ridiculed, Exiled and Imprisoned… For Being Right, for a spirited and insightful talk on the history of these endemic issues and what we can do to finally consign them to the scientific scrap heap.

title: SPEL Talk: The Human Right To Protest
description: Join the Society for Politics, Economics and the Law as Allison Renteln, a professor of political science and anthropology at the University of Southern California, presents a lecture on the right to protest.

title: [Deep Canvass Institute] Skills Session 3: Identifying & Processing Underlying Conflict
description:
How to join:
You will be asked to complete a brief Zoom registration at the time you log-in for the event.

In conversations, we spend a lot of time on the surface, arguing about facts and opinions we’ve heard online , but never really understand the underlying emotional conflicts that drive our beliefs and the beliefs of others. Join us to learn more about identifying core underlying conflicts, and how to approach processing those conflicts by focusing on the things we’ve actually experienced rather than what we’ve heard on the news.

*This session will be offered twice - please choose the date/time that works best for you.*

title: Solving Sustainability Live Podcast: Future of Protein
location: Partners I – NC State University, Raleigh Founded (Suite 1650)
description:
Solving Sustainability is a live interdisciplinary podcast that seeks to raise awareness about macro-level sustainability challenges and innovations while fostering meaningful discussion between students, researchers, entrepreneurs and policymakers.

During the episode, the podcast will seek to engage NC State community members — students alongside interested faculty and staff — who have experience within sustainability but may not have a deep understanding of environmental justice. The podcast is not just an entertaining way to seek solutions but also a call to action and a way to connect with others.

This event will take place in the Raleigh Founded Centennial Cafe and hors d'oeuvres will be available for all attendees.

title: Ackland Film Forum: "Boyhood" (Richard Linklater, 2014)
location: The Lumina Theater, 620 Market Street, Chapel Hill, 27516, United States
description:
Filmed over twelve years with the same cast members who conspicuously age from segment to segment, Boyhood offers one of cinema’s most literal and radical explorations of coming of age. Following Mason (Ellar Coltrane) from early childhood through his first days of college, the film refuses traditional narrative climaxes in favor of accumulation: small moments, shifting family dynamics, and incremental changes in perception as time itself figures as the “main character.” Linklater’s method allows us to experience maturation as something lived, uneven, and quietly profound. 
The film will be introduced by Rick Warner, Bowman and Gordon Gray Distinguished Term Professor and Director of Film Studies. 
  
This film will be screened at 7:30 p.m. at The Lumina Theater (620 Market St, Chapel Hill, NC 27516). 
Tickets are free for all audience members, and registration is required via The Lumina’s webpage. Please click here to reserve your free ticket. UNC-Chapel Hill students can receive CLE credit for attending by scanning the QR code at the event.

The Ackland Film Forum is co-organized by the Ackland Art Museum and UNC Film Studies, housed in the UNC-Chapel Hill Department of English and Comparative Literature.

title: Civic Hacking for Affordable Housing
description:
Code with the Carolinas - Triangle
Join volunteers at Code with the Carolinas as we help the [National Zoning Atlas](https://www.zoningatlas.org/) (NZA) team include North Carolina and South Carolina in their nationwide zoning atlas. "The National Zoning Atlas aims to depict key aspects of zoning codes in an online, user-friendly map." The Atlas aims to provide data for better solutions in transportation, environmental, and especially affordable housing policy.

Affordable housing proponents have long recognized zoning policies as barriers to affordable housing. To improve zoning policies, we first need to better understand the current state of zoning across the United States. Code with the Carolinas is helping this cause by joining the National Zoning Atlas to gather zoning data for North and South Carolina.

This is a multi-dimensional project grounded in data science with an end-goal of contributing to a nationally influential dataset. Our current work session activities focus on internet search and integrating data from multiple sources. The project as a whole has occasional opportunities for U/X and outreach and opportunities to learn about machine learning and natural language processing through the work of the NZA team. Volunteering on this project will provide excellent exposure to the foundations of civic data science, with a focus on GIS data.

Even if you're new to this project and/or civic tech volunteering, you're welcome to join the meeting. Visitors are always welcome and no technical skills are needed. You can learn more about the project on our [Slack Workspace](https://join.slack.com/t/codeforthecarolinas/shared_invite/zt-1kxuwu05x-3KxOpkOYjAuN5yuOAH8ROg) in the #project-zoning-atlas channel.

Meetings start with brief introductions. New volunteers are offered a personal orientation, often in a breakout room, while experienced volunteers get right to work. By the end of the session, both groups of volunteers are working together. We also contribute to the project through asynchronous work and informal work sessions.

Meetings are on the Jitsi platform and the link is provided before each meeting. If you will be joining on mobile, install the Jitsi app in advance [https://jitsi.org/downloads/](https://jitsi.org/downloads/) .

Learn more at [codewiththecarolinas.org](codeforthecarolinas.org) or reach out to info@codewiththecarolinas.org

All activities of Code with the Carolinas, a project of [Social Good Fund](https://www.socialgoodfund.org/), follow our [Code of Conduct](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/0d32d5f905f8fd49d5e80395f8fd47f2b59ce3ef8b335f72/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTkx) and are subject to this [Volunteer Waiver of Liability](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/06dd62200e6b28426b137e0e6b2848fb901487014f72cd16/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTE2).

title: Weekly Game Night!
description:
Durham Geeks
We meet at Moon Dog Meadery under Joe Van Gogh on Broad Street every Monday night. We have loads of games (some belong to MoonDog, some are brought by attendees), a fun and welcoming group of folks (always eager to have more, however!), and there are always folks eager to teach games to those who haven't played certain titles.

Moondog has a growing collection of canned and bottled brews, ciders, and colas in addition to their in-house-brewed meads. There are also some snacks like chips and popcorn. Sometimes, folks bring in other treats to share. If you do, please mark them as containing any common allergens.

Outside food (delivered, brought in, or homemade) is welcomed and encouraged. Outside drinks are not. Since the venue does not prepare food, **dogs are allowed and welcomed.** (Dogs are very friendly and welcoming, too, but they mostly roam around to collect ear scritches and love. If you're not comfortable with dogs around, let us know, and we'll try to accommodate.)

Bring games, friends, and a welcoming spirit. We're excited to have you with us!

title: Ackland Film Forum: "Boyhood" (Richard Linklater, 2014)
location: The Lumina Theater, 620 Market Street, Chapel Hill, 27516, United States
description:
Filmed over twelve years with the same cast members who conspicuously age from segment to segment, Boyhood offers one of cinema’s most literal and radical explorations of coming of age. Following Mason (Ellar Coltrane) from early childhood through his first days of college, the film refuses traditional narrative climaxes in favor of accumulation: small moments, shifting family dynamics, and incremental changes in perception as time itself figures as the “main character.” Linklater’s method allows us to experience maturation as something lived, uneven, and quietly profound. 
The film will be introduced by Rick Warner, Bowman and Gordon Gray Distinguished Term Professor and Director of Film Studies. 
  
This film will be screened at 7:30 p.m. at The Lumina Theater (620 Market St, Chapel Hill, NC 27516). 
Tickets are free for all audience members, and registration is required via The Lumina’s webpage. Please click here to reserve your free ticket. UNC-Chapel Hill students can receive CLE credit for attending by scanning the QR code at the event. 
  
About the Series: 
Join the Ackland Film Forum this spring for Growing Up, Looking Back, a series that accompanies the Ackland exhibition Bill Bamberger: Boys Will Be Men. This semester’s series highlights the filmgoing experience by using three local movie theaters as its venues. 
This film series is dedicated to the memory of Allison Portnow Lathrop, former Head of Public Programs at the Ackland Art Museum. For more than a decade, Allison was an indispensable champion of film culture on campus and in Chapel Hill, most notably through her visionary stewardship of the Ackland Film Forum. She fostered a spirit of generous collaboration among faculty, students, and artists. Her belief in the power of cinema lives on in the conversations and shared experiences she helped create. 
The Ackland Film Forum is co-organized by the Ackland Art Museum and UNC Film Studies, housed in the UNC-Chapel Hill Department of English and Comparative Literature.

2026 UNC Cleantech Summit
description:
The 12th annual UNC Cleantech Summit will take place at the UNC Friday Conference Center on March 9 – 10, 2026. The two-day event will bring together professionals in business, government, and academia from across the Southeast and around the world for discussion, workshops, mentoring, and networking to foster leadership and growth in the Southeast’s cleantech economy. The Summit convenes more than 1,000 attendees and is the largest event of its kind by a university in the United States. Co-hosted by the UNC Institue for the Environment and the Ackerman Center for Excellence in Sustainability at the Kenan-Flagler Business School. Registration opens Fall 2025.

 

Register here: https://web.cvent.com/event/dc0e55ed-323b-4ce9-bb78-8699d3e904f3/summary?RefId=UNC%20Website

title: Harriet Jacobs in the Attic
description:
Join us for an immersive audiovisual experience as we illuminate Harriet Jacobs, a fugitive North Carolina enslaved person who hid in a tiny attic garret for seven years. While hiding she planned her escape, fled to the North, and eventually became a key figure in the abolitionist movement.

In celebration of Black History Month, we invite you to explore a to-scale recreation of Harriet’s attic space and listen to readings from her book, Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl, which was published in 1861.

title: Lake Raleigh Woods Hike
description:
Please join us on a monthly basis for a walk through the Lake Raleigh Woods, one of NC State's hidden gems and Hallowed Spaces. The hike is about 1.5 miles. Below are a few things to remember for the hike:

Make sure to park in the northwest corner of the Park Alumni Center parking lot a little before 9 a.m. for a brief safety conversation. Parking is free for everyone. Wear closed-toed shoes and bring a water bottle.Check the weather forecast and wear weather-appropriate clothing on the hike.Restrooms are available in the Alumni Center. There are no facilities on the trail.The hike will last approximately 90 minutes but could be a little shorter depending on the group's pace. Space is limited for each hike. Be sure to register.

title: Election Polling: Design, Analysis, and Communication
description: As the 2026 election cycle approaches, polls will once again dominate headlines and shape public discussion. This half-day course provides a practical foundation in the design, analysis, and communication of election polls. Topics include questionnaire design, the advantages and limitations of different survey modes, weighting techniques to improve representativeness, strategies for likely voter screens, and approaches to assess, interpret, and communicate poll results accurately. Registration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: Legends & Landmarks: Paintings and Drawings by James C. Horton
description: In commemoration of America's 250th, the Bellamy Mansion Museum is hosting an art exhibition by local artist James C. Horton, which includes over 40 original paintings and drawings that highlight historic scenes and places from around the region and country that help tell America's story. Visitors can view the exhibit free of charge any time the museum is open (M-Sun. 10 am-4 pm). Please stop by the Carriage ​House Visitor Center to gain admission. Art is available for purchase and an Artist's Reception will be held on February 27th (6-8pm). Visit bellamymansion.org/exhibits for more information or email info@bellamymansion.org if you have questions.

title: Legends and Landmarks
description: A 1 person art exhibition with many paintings of historical subjects and national and local landmarks.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: Social Media Made Simple: Tools, Timing, and Templates
description: Social media doesn't have to feel like a second full-time job. Join us for Social Media Made Simple, led by the always dynamic Kim Kaid of Kaid Marketing. Kim brings the perfect mix of strategy, creativity, and real-world practicality. (and our attendees consistently rave about her sessions) In this interactive workshop, you'll learn: Which platforms actually make sense for your business (hint: not all of them) How to create eye-catching graphics using simple, customizable templates What to post, when to post, and how to stay consistent without burning out Kim will break it all down in a way that's current, clear, and immediatey actionable. You'll leave with practical tools, renewed confidence, and a realistic plan that makes social media work for your business... not the other way around. If you've ever thought "I know I should be doing more online, but I don't know where to start," this class is for you.

title: Faculty and Staff Walking Club
description: Join your colleagues for a lovely walk down to the NC State greenway. We'll meet outside the Starbucks at Talley Student Union and walk down the greenway to Pullen Park and back. The route is approximately 1.4 miles. All are welcome to participate.

description: Take a break from your day and enjoy a free yoga class on Centennial Campus. Classes are held every weekday, except when the university is closed, from 12:15 to 1:05 p.m. The classes are open to everyone, and registration is not required. Please bring your own mat.

title: Creating Your Own Digital Exhibit
description:
Want to tell a story using images, videos, documents, or other media from your research project? You can create and host a digital exhibit fore free using CollectionBuilder! This workshop will cover how to set up your own digital exhibit using CollectionBuilder and Google Sheets. Attendees will learn how to add images, PDFs, videos, and more to their website. CollectionBuilder makes it easy to set up your own digital exhibit with minimal technical expertise.

 

This workshop is offered by the University Libraries and will be led by librarian Rolando Rodriguez and CALA Shannon Henry. 

 

This workshop is being offered remotely via Zoom only. Registrants will receive a Zoom link; those on the waitlist will not receive a Zoom link unless they are bumped up to a registered spot. After the workshop, registrants and those on the waitlist will receive a link to the workshop recording. You can register on the University Libraries calendar: https://calendar.lib.unc.edu/event/16023378

Meet at the fort entrance. Pets are not allowed inside the fort.

title: In-Person Workshop: How To Write an Abstract
description: The Office of Undergraduate Research can help students prepare their abstracts for oral research and poster presentations at its in-person abstract workshops. Please attend one of our workshops so we can help you get ready to showcase your hard work on and off campus. We’re also happy to help you one-on-one and point you towards additional opportunities to present your work.

title: Right to Be's Stand Up Against Street Harassment
description:
REGISTER HERE: https://righttobe.org/events/stand-up-against-street-harassment-44/

How can we take action if we don’t know what to do? Right To Be is offering this free, one-hour, interactive training to train people on how to safely intervene when you witness street harassment — or experience it yourself. During this training you’ll learn a clear, adaptable, and expert-approved set of tools that have been proven to reduce the prevalence of street harassment: Right To Be’s 5Ds of bystander intervention.

We’ll start by deepening our understanding of street harassment and its impact. Then, we’ll talk through five strategies for intervention: distract, delegate, document, delay, and direct; and how to prioritize your own safety while intervening. We’ll also talk about what to do if you’re worried the violence will turn on you and how to avoid escalating the situation. Then, we’ll talk about what to do if you experience harassment and give you tools to safely get your power back.

We’ll have time at the end for practice. Exit polls show 98.8% of attendees leave the training feeling like there is at least one thing they can do the next time they witness harassment.

title: Crafting an Oral Research Presentation
description: The Office of Undergraduate Research can help students craft oral research presentations. Join us at one of our oral research presentation workshops to help you get ready to showcase your hard work on and off campus.

title: Graduate Oral Defense 101
description: Learn how to master the final stage of the thesis/dissertation process. The Graduate School will offer an in-person session for graduate students to review public speaking strategies and practice their speaking skills so they can approach their oral defense with confidence.

title: State of AI : Context Engineering with Microsoft and Anthropic
description:
Modern Web (Triangle)
Context is becoming a defining layer of modern AI systems and is shaping how models reason, respond, and integrate into real products. This event explores how context engineering is influencing the current state of AI and the way teams are thinking about building more capable and reliable systems.

[Join us for a conversation](https://luma.com/79w48jgi) with experts from Microsoft and Anthropic as we examine emerging approaches to structuring context, lessons from real-world AI development, and the considerations guiding what comes next.

Featuring insights from
[Harald Kirschner](https://www.linkedin.com/in/digitarald/), Principal Product Manager for GitHub Copilot and VS Code at Microsoft
[Ado Kukic](https://www.linkedin.com/in/adocomplete/), Community Manager at Anthropic
[Brandon Mathis](https://www.linkedin.com/in/mathisbrandon/), Engineering Lead, This Dot Labs
[Tracy Lee](https://www.linkedin.com/in/tracyslee/), CEO, This Dot Labs

[If you are interested in attending, please RSVP using this link!](https://luma.com/iho7wvqp)

title: Public School Strong Organizing Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46979984/1:8a82c476b1f77694f639474ce602665b2e94401cf3d9fcadb88b6bd1bb557fc1/

How to join:
Hi there, 

Thanks for signing up for our Public School Strong Orientation & Organizing Meeting!  

Be sure to register on zoom in advance for this meeting:
https://raceforward-org.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIvcuiurz4pE9BGsfJi-mBBX_lhl2bPG7Ti 

After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting.

**We are thrilled to welcome David Backer, author of As Public As Possible: Radical Finance for America’s Public Schools, as our guest speaker at January’s meeting.** 

Join us for the next Public Schools Strong organizing meeting on Thursday, January 22nd, where parents, family members, educators, and supporters from across the nation gather each month to gain practical tools to promote, protect, and strengthen our public schools. 

*If you have already attended a Public School Strong Orientation, you'll join a breakout room with current members for connection, reflection and training. If you're new, we've got you covered too!*

**Do you want honest, equitable and fully-funded public schools?** Acting locally is key.

### Join public school families, educators and supporters from across the country to learn how to:
1. Form a Public School Strong team in your school district
2. Engage your community to get school boards and district leaders to speak up for equitable and fully funded public schools
3. Connect your local advocacy to others for state and national impact

For more info, please visit publicschoolstrong.org

### Current Public School Strong Members

There is strength in staying connected. After completing Public School Strong (PSS) Orientation, this is your consistent space to reconnect with your peers across the country to:

1. Access helpful tools and practice relevant skills,
2. Connect the dots between what is happening locally and nationally,
3. Learn from mistakes and progress made along the way

Bring a friend!

**Self Directed Orientation Now Available!**

If these live training times don't work for you, you can complete your orientation at your own pace:

1) Create Account in NEA Portal (open to non-NEA members too): 

neapartnera.learnupon.com/users/sign_up

2) Select the PSS orientation by clicking on this link:

bit.ly/SelfDirectedPSS

How to prepare:
### Hi there! Thanks for signing up for a 'Public School Strong Organizing Meeting!' (for new folks we'll also have a new member orientation)

This event is virtual, will provide updates on the federal attacks on our public schools and the immigrant community, actions you can take right now and the tools to support you in doing son. 

For new folks, we'll provide an introduction to our Public School Strong campaign. Check out our organizing platform using this link to find or create a local team: 

- publicschoolstrong.org

More on this powerful tool at your Orientation...
