title: Durham Farmers' Market
location: Durham Central Park, 501 Foster Street, Durham, NC, 27701, United States
description:
The Durham Farmers’ Market is a community of farmers, growers and crafters. We are strongly committed to local products: every item you see at the market was produced by the people selling it, and it all comes from no more than 70 miles away. 
The Durham Farmers’ Market open Saturdays mornings for local produce, flowers, crafts and more! The Wednesday Market is open from 3-6pm Wednesdays, Mid-April through Mid-October 
If you’d like more information about this week’s vendor list, SNAP benefits and map, visit durhamfarmersmarket.com 

We are happy to offer Double Bucks to our SNAP clients. This means is a SNAP client has $10, they receive $20 in tokens to spend at the winter market.

Estamos muy contentos de ofrecer Double Bucks a nuestros clientes de SNAP. Esto significa que si un cliente de SNAP gasta $ 10, recibirá $ 20 en tokens para gastar en productos frescos.


Additionally, we offer a cash match program for other qualifying folks. To receive this benefit, simply bring cash to the info table. Then, we’ll double the amount with tokens. For example, $10 in cash equals $20 in tokens.

También ofrecemos un programa de contrapartida en efectivo para nuestros beneficiarios de SNAP que no tienen beneficios durante el mes, clientes de WIC y miembros de la comunidad que viven en viviendas públicas. Para recibir este beneficio, simplemente traiga efectivo a la tabla de información y duplicaremos la cantidad con tokens. Por ejemplo, $ 10 en efectivo equivalen a $ 20 en fichas.

title: Phone Bank with Ground Truth
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47683006/1:1d859d5838a314cd00eb29f52a62182ad856e0786f7b2c83e9dedb14ec5b21c5/

Join Swing Left for a Ground Truth phone bank—designed to fix what’s broken in Democratic organizing. We’re launching an innovative new program that will revolutionize how we connect with voters over the phone. 

Our goal: to help Democrats rebuild trust, win tough races, and flip the House. We're bringing people together to build the community of real people, having real conversations with real people. During Ground Truth phone banks, we will talk to as many people as possible and focus on listening. 

At this phone bank, we’ll:

- Call ALL types of voters -- not just those who vote like us.

- Have real conversations that LISTEN deeply to voters’ concerns.

- Gather insights to share with campaigns, so they can be responsive to voters’ needs and smarter with their messaging, targeting, and outreach.

Don’t worry if it’s your first time—training, materials, and support are all provided. Just come prepared with your phone and laptop or computer!

We recommend using earbuds or using speakerphone to free up your hands as well! 

––

About the program:

This isn’t traditional phone banking—it’s a whole new way of talking to voters. Together, we’ll gather authentic insights from communities, use cutting-edge tools to capture what we hear, and share it back with campaigns to strengthen their efforts.

Why we’re starting early: We launched a year ahead of the midterms to build power before campaigns get off the ground.

How we’ll go big: Calling every voter, not just the ones we normally rely on, so every conversation builds a more complete picture of the electorate.

How we’ll go deep: Practicing empathetic listening and real conversations that uncover voters’ “Ground Truth,” not just checking boxes on a script.

Paid for by Swing Left, Swingleft.org, not authorized by any candidate or candidate’s committee.

How to prepare:
Thanks for signing up to join the Ground Truth phone banking program! Together, we can transform how we talk with voters, rebuild trust, and swing the most competitive races.

There will be a training at the beginning of the phone bank, so don't worry if this is your first time.  

Please be sure to have your laptop/ computer charged and ready to go!

See you there!

https://www.mobilize.us/swingleft/event/872281/

title: Get Wild: Pollinator Power!
location: Williamson Farm and Nature Preserve, 4429 Old Country Mial Rd, Raleigh, 27610, United States
description:
Join TLC for a family friendly pollination exploration!
Pollinators run the world! As the first blooms of spring arrive, so do their pollinators. Meet us at Williamson Nature Preserve's pollinator garden to learn about the special role of butterflies, bees, and more. Explore why pollinators matter with kid-focused nature games and learn to see the world through their eyes with a pollinator scavenger hunt! 

Get Wild! Hikes and activities are most appropriate for ages 5 years and up. Wear layers, bring a water bottle, and wear shoes that can get muddy. Please register all members of the family attending, including children and adults. This is a free event, but pre-registration is required:


If the event is full, please email Meera Butalia at mbutalia@triangleland.org to be added to the waitlist.

Current Mask Policy: TLC seeks to build a supportive, mask-friendly environment in all collaborations with staff and the public. Please wear a mask if you are not vaccinated, or if that makes you feel most comfortable. We also encourage those eligible to be vaccinated to get their Covid-19 vaccine and booster shot. If you are experiencing any Covid-related symptoms, please stay home and attend a program another day. Our top priority is to create a safe environment for our community. Thank you for your understanding.

Inclement weather policy: In the event of inclement weather, you will be contacted by email for a call on cancellation or postponement.

title: Spring Break Camp (Grades 1st-3rd)
location: North Carolina Botanical Garden, 100 Old Mason Farm Road, Chapel Hill, NC, 27517, United States
description:

Grades: 1st – 3rd
Date: Monday, March 30 – April 2 (no camp on Friday)
Time: 9:00 AM – 3:00 PM *Drop-off: 8:45-9 a.m.; Pick-up: 3-3:15 p.m.
Location: North Carolina Botanical Garden
Fee: $290 ($262 Household & above Members) 
School’s out for spring break? Send your nature lover to a week full of hands-on outdoor discovery celebrating the change of season.  
Join us for an adventure through the four elements—earth, water, air, and fire—and discover how each one helps our native plants and animals thrive! Campers will dig into the soil to uncover life underground, explore the magic of water and how it shapes habitats, discover flying seeds, and learn how fire helps forests grow anew. Each day includes hands-on science activities, nature hikes, creative crafts, and storytelling that connects these powerful elements to the living world around us.

title: The Art and Science of Plant Care
description:
Caring for plants is both an art and a science — part intuition, part know-how. Every gardener, from beginner to expert, faces the same essential questions: When should I prune? How much should I water? What’s the best way to plant, fertilize or repot? In this class, Bryce Lane will bring decades of horticultural expertise to demystify these everyday tasks, showing you not just what to do but why it works.

Over eight weeks, you’ll explore the fundamental practices that keep gardens thriving, learning how to perform each one with confidence and purpose. You’ll uncover the science behind good technique — understanding how soil, water, nutrients and plant biology interact — while also developing the practical judgment that comes only with experience.

Each session will focus on one key aspect of plant care, building a complete toolkit for growing healthier, more resilient plants in any garden setting.

title: NC Reads - Daughters of Green Mountain Book Event
description:
Join North Carolina Humanities on March 30, 2026 at 6:15 pm, for its second North Carolina Reads book club discussion of 2026. This discussion will examine themes and topics from the North Carolina Reads book selection “Daughters of Green Mountain Gap.” This discussion features author Teri M. Brown in a moderated conversation. Please note: you do not have to read the featured book(s) to attend a North Carolina Reads book club discussion. North Carolina Reads is North Carolina Humanities’ award-winning, virtual, statewide book club. 
From February – June 2026, NC Humanities will host free, virtual, monthly book club discussion events where participants will hear from guest speakers, including book authors and topic experts. Libraries, community groups, and individuals across North Carolina are encouraged to read along with NC Humanities, attend NC Reads book club discussion events, and then host their own local book discussions to further conversation, camaraderie, and community. NC Reads events will be held on the following dates, all events start at 6:15 PM: 

February 25, 2026 – Morningside: The 1979 Greensboro Massacre and the Struggle for an American City’s Soul by Aran Shetterly
March 30, 2026 – Daughters of Green Mountain Gap by Teri M. Brown
April 28, 2026 – The Devil’s Done Come Back: New Ghost Tales from North Carolina edited by Ed Southern
May 27, 2026 –The Caretaker by Ron Rash
June 23, 2026 – Night Magic: Adventures Among Glowworms, Moon Gardens, and Other Marvels of the Dark by Leigh Ann Henion

title: Plug & Play: Live AI Workflows in Action
description:
Data Science Raleigh
Forget static demos and slide decks. Plug & Play Sessions are all about showing, not telling. In this hands-on webinar series, you'll see tech leaders walk through their AI product in real time, highlighting exactly how it works in the wild: setup, workflows, integrations, all in just 30 minutes.

Built on the momentum of our AI Demo Day format, Plug & Play goes deeper. Presenters slow down and dig in: demonstrating usability, showing off speed to value, and guiding attendees through common real-world scenarios. It’s a rare opportunity to see how tools behave under actual conditions, not just polished slides.

Expect 2–3 hours of back-to-back, engaging walkthroughs. Attendees are encouraged to click along live, ask questions, and walk away with more than awareness—usable knowledge.

**Key Takeaways:**

1️⃣ **Real Product Experience:** See full workflows in motion—not just the highlights.

2️⃣ **Usability in Focus:** Evaluate ease of use, integration paths, and how quickly value is delivered.

3️⃣ **Interactive Learning:** Follow along live and get answers as you go.

4️⃣ **Depth Over Demos:** A deeper understanding of how AI tools actually fit into your stack.

**[Register here](https://hubs.ly/Q042c9tg0)**

This sub-group focuses on Science Fiction and Fantasy, so if you have anything that falls under that category — be it short story, poem, novel-in-progress or screenplay — then come! We'd love to hear from you.

title: NC Science Trails Scavenger Hunt
description: Birds and bicycles take center stage at Lake Norman State Park in Troutman. Located in Iredell County 40 miles north of Charlotte, Lake Norman State Park boasts the region's popular mountain biking trail system, the Itusi Trail, offering nearly 31 miles of single-track trail. The namesake lake is the largest manmade lake within the state, and the park covers about 17 miles of its northern shoreline. Park Lake, set off from the larger Lake Norman by a dam, offers quiet fishing spots and paddling. Heron rookeries on two islands shelter over 25 great blue heron nests. Join us at Lake Norman State Park for a fun Science Festival scavenger hunt during the month of April to learn about science and nature at its finest!

description:

The spring wildflower season is challenging to predict, but we will see plenty! Plan to wear weather-appropriate, comfortable clothes and good walking shoes.

Location: Meet at Penny’s Bend. Driving directions will be included in a program reminder email sent the week before the program. Parking is free and carpooling is encouraged.   
Accessibility: This will be a small group program that takes place outdoors and traveling on unpaved trails with some uneven surfaces and moderate inclines.

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980072/1:a97fcb1f9d2eefa76bde5c456a072080e8a3bb87aaf10d142e007ee52af0d182/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Laughter, humor and play to reduce stress
description:
Professional comedians shouldn’t be the only people to use smiling, laughter, humor and play as part of their daily routine. These skills are valuable for everyone. A good smile has long been a key tool for anyone who needs to influence others. Laughter is now shown to improve our pain tolerance. Humor and play can be the building blocks of problem-solving. Together they can improve our happiness and effectiveness.

 

Workshop Objectives
At the end of this workshop, you will be able to:
Understand the benefits of smiling for:
• Improving your mood
• Keeping a positive focus through an easy exercise
• Building confidence and receptiveness among others
Use laughter to:
• Improve cardiovascular health by practicing voluntary laughter
• Enjoy the social dynamic of group behavior
• Reduce stress
Use humor and play to:
• Have fun by building simple jokes
• Improve creativity by keeping editing separate from the creative process
• Build relationships by saying, “Yes, and…”

title: A Series of Questions: Team Trivia at 321 Coffee
description:
Bring your lunch, some teammates (or opponents) and your collective brainpower to 321 Coffee shop twice a month to compete in team trivia. Solo participants are also encouraged to compete. Whether you're a casual quizzer or a seasoned trivia champ, this lunchtime series is the perfect way to test your knowledge and connect with co-workers and friends.

NC State University librarians will serve as hosts of the competition, and trivia master extraordinaire Dave Provost is our question maker.

The winning teams will take home a seasonal 3D printed trophy and a free coffee drink, but the real prize might be the friends we make along the way.

title: Leadership in High Pressure Situations
description:
Under pressure, stress pulls leaders out of their roles and into managing mode, reacting to problems, controlling tasks, and just trying to get through the day. This session explores what happens in the brain during high-pressure moments and why that makes it harder to lead with clarity. We will look at how pressure pushes leaders toward managing instead of leading, and why that matters for your team’s growth and resilience. Most importantly, you will leave with a simple, practical tool to help you slow down, refocus, and lead in a way that aligns with your values, even when the stakes are high.

Workshop Objectives:

• Explain how stress impacts the brain and leadership behavior
• Identify your default leadership lane under pressure
• Shift from managing the crisis to leading through it

title: April 2 - AI, ML and Computer Vision Meetup
description:
Raleigh AI, Machine Learning and Computer Vision Meetup
Join our virtual Meetup to hear talks from experts on cutting-edge topics across AI, ML, and computer vision.

**Date, Time and Location**

Apr 2, 2026
9 - 11 AM Pacific
Online. **[Register for the Zoom!](https://voxel51.com/events/ai-ml-and-computer-vision-meetup-april-2-2026)**

**Async Agents in Production: Failure Modes and Fixes**

As models improve, we are starting to build long-running, asynchronous agents such as deep research agents and browser agents that can execute multi-step workflows autonomously. These systems unlock new use cases, but they fail in ways that short-lived agents do not.

The longer an agent runs, the more early mistakes compound, and the more token usage grows through extended reasoning, retries, and tool calls. Patterns that work for request-response agents often break down, leading to unreliable behaviour and unpredictable costs.

This talk is aimed at use case developers, with secondary relevance for platform engineers. It covers the most common failure modes in async agents and practical design patterns for reducing error compounding and keeping token costs bounded in production.

*About the Speaker*

[Meryem Arik](https://www.linkedin.com/in/meryemarik/) is the co-founder and CEO of Doubleword, where she works on large-scale LLM inference and production AI systems. She studied theoretical physics and philosophy at the University of Oxford. Meryem is a frequent conference speaker, including a TEDx speaker and a four-time highly rated speaker at QCon conferences. She was named to the Forbes 30 Under 30 list for her work in AI infrastructure.

**Visual AI at the Edge: Beyond the Model**

Edge-based visual AI promises low latency, privacy, and real-time decision-making, but many projects struggle to move beyond successful demos. This talk explores what deploying visual AI at the edge really involves, shifting the focus from models to complete, operational systems. We will discuss common pitfalls teams encounter when moving from lab to field. Attendees will leave with a practical mental model for approaching edge vision projects more effectively.

*About the Speaker*

[David Moser](https://www.linkedin.com/in/david-moser-dm/) is an AI/Computer Vision expert and Founding Engineer with a strong track record of building and deploying safety-critical visual AI systems in real-world environments. As Co-Founder of Orella Vision, he is building Visual AI for Autonomy on the Edge - going from data and models to production-grade edge deployments.

**Sanitizing Evaluation Datasets: From Detection to Correction**

We generally accept that gold standard evaluation sets contain label noise, yet we rarely fix them because the engineering friction is too high. This talk presents a workflow to operationalize ground-truth auditing. We will demonstrate how to bridge the gap between algorithmic error detection and manual rectification. Specifically, we will show how to inspect discordant ground truth labels and correct them directly in-situ. The goal is to move to a fully trusted end-to-end evaluation pipeline.

*About the Speaker*

[Nick Lotz](https://www.linkedin.com/in/nicholaslotz) is an engineer on the Voxel51 community team. With a background in open source infrastructure and a passion for developer enablement, Nick focuses on helping teams understand their tools and how to use them to ship faster.

**Building enterprise agentic systems that scale**

Building AI agents that work in demos is easy, building true assistants that make people genuinely productive takes a different set of patterns. This talk shares lessons from a multi-agent system at Cisco used by 2,000+ sellers daily, where we moved past "chat with your data" to encoding business workflows into true agentic systems people actually rely on to get work done.

We'll cover multi-agent orchestration patterns for complex workflows, the personalization and productivity features that drive adoption, and the enterprise foundations that helped us earn user trust at scale. You'll leave with an architecture and set of patterns that have been battle tested at enterprise scale.

*About the Speaker*

[Aman Sardana](https://www.linkedin.com/in/amansardana/) is a Senior Engineering Architect at Cisco, I lead the design and deployment of enterprise AI systems that blend LLMs, data infrastructure, and customer experience to solve high‑stakes, real-world problems at scale. I’m also an open-source contributor and active mentor in the AI community, helping teams move from AI experimentation to reliable agentic applications in production.
source: Meetup: Raleigh AI, ML & Computer Vision

description:

Join the Graduate School for a workshop that will offer strategies for distilling the most important elements of your teaching philosophy and expressing it in a clear, succinct narrative. We will especially think about how it can be used to highlight your strengths when applying for academic teaching positions.

title: Concise Communication
description: Do people say that your communication style is too wordy? If so, join the Graduate School for Concise Communication, formerly Concise Writing. This interactive workshop will address the challenge of building clear, concise sentences that represent your science to diverse stakeholders. Using Richard Lanham’s famous “Paramedic Method” and other approaches, participants will leave the session with strategies for user-friendly sentences in reports and other areas of workplace communication.

description:
Come explore the serene waters of the Beaver Dam area of Falls Lake on a guided paddling adventure. This unique reservoir offers a tranquil paddling experience away from motorized boat traffic, making it ideal for beginners and wildlife enthusiasts alike. Paddle the lake and explore small coves while being surrounded by a forested landscape that serves as a rookery for herons, ospreys and bald eagles.

Prior paddling experience isn't required to participate, but participants must be able to swim and will undergo a swim test during a pre-trip meeting. Transportation, equipment and instruction are included in the trip.

title: Cultivating Courage by Covering the Corners
location: Corners all over the Triangle and beyond!, Durham, NC, 27705
description: Join us on the first Sunday of the month as we do a coordinated demonstration on multiple corners (Feb 1 is just for colleges and has its own link). All events are from 1pm-2:30pm as we host “Cultivating Courage by Covering Corners,” a peaceful visibility event designed to build courage and unity in support of democracy. Please sign up for your corner on SignUp Genius: Small groups will gather on consecutive corners along a key stretch of road, holding positive, pro-democracy signs. We will cover corners in areas where ED4D does typically do not demonstrate: Creedmoor, Holly Springs, and XXX. All of these areas are purple. The purpose is to build motivation for folks to join us. By standing together on multiple corners simultaneously, we show our community that courage is contagious—that even small acts of presence can inspire others to stand up for shared values. Each participant plays an essential role: being visible, being brave, and encouraging others to do the same. The goal is to create a ripple of courage that spreads across our community and beyond. We will have one common sign at each corner. People will need to bring their own signs. Please also consider bring an American flag along with one of several other flags (rainbow flag, Palestine flag, Mexican flag, Ukraine flag), to make it clear we are not MAGA. We have rainbow flags for each corner. Please make signs that say, “Stand up, you are not alone!” “Resist!” “Courage is contagious!” "Call your elected officials" "Vote" "We the People Have Power!" "Start your own demonstration!!" Please join ED4D's Substack: https://substack.com/profile/115484101-engaged-durhamites-4-democracy For more ED4D Mobilize Events: https://www.mobilize.us/engageddurhamitesfordemocracy/ https://www.mobilize.us/engagedefendersfordemocracy/event/867070/

title: Botany by Plant Family: The Key to See
location: Onsite at Duke Gardens – directions to follow
description:
Learning to notice plant family patterns can greatly aid in demystifying the “green wall” of plant species around us. Nearly 350,000 species of flowering plants are known to global science, and these species have been grouped into more than 400 flowering plant families. By learning the top 30 plant families growing around you, you will begin to recognize plants everywhere you go. After an indoor presentation on the major plant families common to the Southeastern U.S., ethnobotanist marc williams will reinforce your plant identification skills on a walk in the Gardens to observe family patterns in leaf, flower and fruit types. Discuss edibility, medicinality, craft, wildlife support and landscape beauty as you gain a more holistic understanding of the major plants comprising Southern U.S. flora and their potential ecological and ethnobotanical applications. 
Fee: $32; 20% discount for Duke Gardens members with discount code. 
Parking: Fees apply. 
Location: Onsite at Duke Gardens. Meeting location will be included in a program reminder email sent before the program. 
Accessibility: This will be a small group program that takes place partially seated indoors and partially outdoors traveling on a combination of paved and unpaved paths with some uneven surfaces and moderate inclines.

location: NC Department of Natural and Cultural Resources 109 E. Jones St, Raleigh, NC 27601, M45
description:
NC Cardinal Governance Committee

NC Cardinal is a consortium of North Carolina Public Libraries with the goal of sharing resources and expanding access through the use of a single online catalog. Members libraries share an Integrated Library System called Evergreen and participate in resource sharing of library materials across the state.

title: The Art and Science of Plant Care
description:
Caring for plants is both an art and a science — part intuition, part know-how. Every gardener, from beginner to expert, faces the same essential questions: When should I prune? How much should I water? What’s the best way to plant, fertilize or repot? In this class, Bryce Lane will bring decades of horticultural expertise to demystify these everyday tasks, showing you not just what to do but why it works.

Over eight weeks, you’ll explore the fundamental practices that keep gardens thriving, learning how to perform each one with confidence and purpose. You’ll uncover the science behind good technique — understanding how soil, water, nutrients and plant biology interact — while also developing the practical judgment that comes only with experience.

Each session will focus on one key aspect of plant care, building a complete toolkit for growing healthier, more resilient plants in any garden setting.

title: Civic Hacking for Affordable Housing
description:
Code with the Carolinas - Triangle
Join volunteers at Code with the Carolinas as we help the [National Zoning Atlas](https://www.zoningatlas.org/) (NZA) team include North Carolina and South Carolina in their nationwide zoning atlas. "The National Zoning Atlas aims to depict key aspects of zoning codes in an online, user-friendly map." The Atlas aims to provide data for better solutions in transportation, environmental, and especially affordable housing policy.

Affordable housing proponents have long recognized zoning policies as barriers to affordable housing. To improve zoning policies, we first need to better understand the current state of zoning across the United States. Code with the Carolinas is helping this cause by joining the National Zoning Atlas to gather zoning data for North and South Carolina.

This is a multi-dimensional project grounded in data science with an end-goal of contributing to a nationally influential dataset. Our current work session activities focus on internet search and integrating data from multiple sources. The project as a whole has occasional opportunities for U/X and outreach and opportunities to learn about machine learning and natural language processing through the work of the NZA team. Volunteering on this project will provide excellent exposure to the foundations of civic data science, with a focus on GIS data.

Even if you're new to this project and/or civic tech volunteering, you're welcome to join the meeting. Visitors are always welcome and no technical skills are needed. You can learn more about the project on our [Slack Workspace](https://join.slack.com/t/codeforthecarolinas/shared_invite/zt-1kxuwu05x-3KxOpkOYjAuN5yuOAH8ROg) in the #project-zoning-atlas channel.

Meetings start with brief introductions. New volunteers are offered a personal orientation, often in a breakout room, while experienced volunteers get right to work. By the end of the session, both groups of volunteers are working together. We also contribute to the project through asynchronous work and informal work sessions.

Meetings are on the Jitsi platform and the link is provided before each meeting. If you will be joining on mobile, install the Jitsi app in advance [https://jitsi.org/downloads/](https://jitsi.org/downloads/) .

Learn more at [codewiththecarolinas.org](codeforthecarolinas.org) or reach out to info@codewiththecarolinas.org

All activities of Code with the Carolinas, a project of [Social Good Fund](https://www.socialgoodfund.org/), follow our [Code of Conduct](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/0d32d5f905f8fd49d5e80395f8fd47f2b59ce3ef8b335f72/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTkx) and are subject to this [Volunteer Waiver of Liability](https://share.mailbox.org/ajax/share/06dd62200e6b28426b137e0e6b2848fb901487014f72cd16/1/8/Nzg/NzgvMTE2).

title: Project Learning Tree - Together for Birds
description:
Join other K-5 classroom and nonformal educators as we explore Project Learning Tree activities focused on birds.

Together for Birds is PLT’s Newest Activity Collection!

Birds are a wonderful introduction to the natural world and happen to be just outside your door! Whether in the city or a forest, a variety of birds are usually within easy eyeshot or earshot. The activities are designed for educators to use with students in grades K-2, with variations for grades 3-5. The collection is filled with enhanced bird-specific content such as new enrichment experiences, recommended reading, forest facts, accompanying posters, and charts. Every participant will leave with a guide of this collection.

This workshop counts toward NC Environmental Education Certification (Criteria I) and consists of six contact hours that may count toward CEUs for teachers.

 

Location: Carolina Beach State Park 
Workshop Cost: $25
The PLT activity guides are provided free of charge to participants through support from the NC Forestry.
For additional workshop information, call 910-772-0546.

title: *IN-PERSON* SF/F Group
description:
Durham Writers Group
We will be at the People's Solidarity Hub at 1809 Chapel Hill Rd, Durham. This venue does not serve food or drink, but feel free to bring your own. There is a kitchenette for our use, but be sure to clean up afterwards. The space might be a little cozy; please consider masking or staying home if you have a respiratory ailment.

This sub-group focuses on Speculative Fiction, including Science Fiction, Fantasy and Horror. If you have anything that falls under that category — be it short story, poem, novel-in-progress or screenplay — then come! We'd love to hear from you.

Our format allows each reader 15 minutes to present their work aloud, followed by approximately 20 minutes of feedback from the other attendees. To ensure fairness, each attendee is limited to 1 minute of Initial Feedback time. During that period, the author remains silent. Next, we have a 5 minute Free-For-All discussion about the work -- then the author may speak. (We usually get through three readers.)

Though not required, in order to receive more in-depth feed-back, readers may post their work in advance on our Google Group:
https://groups.google.com/d/forum/durham-sff-writers-group

We ask that participants keep their comments constructive and respectful, focusing on what works or doesn't work in the piece.

We also ask that readers provide a warning, both on the Google Group and during the meeting, if their story features content that some readers might find upsetting. This includes descriptions of violence or sexual content.

Readers are:

Backup Readers are:

title: Being Adaptive in an Ever-Changing World
description:
“Because we’ve always done it like that,” may not be the best answer to, “Why do we do the things we do?” In our ever-changing world, we are seeing shifts in our markets and economies, our cultural norms, our behaviors, and ourselves. Now is the time to thrive by learning how to be adaptive and to be consistently able to change yourself to accommodate and maximize the benefits of change.

 

Workshop Objectives
By the end of this workshop, you will:
• Differentiate between being adaptive and similar concepts
• Recognize the importance of being adaptive in work and life
• Examine traits of highly adaptive people
• Outline action steps to take to improve adaptive skills

title: Modular Design and Automated Testing with R
description:
Research is relying on increasingly complex code. However, most academics have not received formal training in software development. This course will introduce participants to some of the best practices of professional software development. Participants will learn to structure their Python code into reusable functions and store those functions as separate scripts and/or packages. Participants will learn to write automated tests for their functions, to help confirm that results are as expected and to ensure that future changes to code do not cause unexpected changes to results.

We will also discuss how to create reproducible “environments” that record package versions in use to help avoid package version incompatibilities and ensure that results can be replicated even months or years after the original analysis.

Modular design is best paired with a version control system. We will discuss how modular design practices integrate with Git and Github, and how to set up “continuous integration” so that automated tests are run each time code is changed.

The course will primarily consist of a hands-on exercise to restructure a single long analysis file into functions and automated tests.

“Version Control with Git and Github” course is a prerequisite for this course.

Registration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: April 8 - Getting Started with FiftyOne
description:
Raleigh AI, Machine Learning and Computer Vision Meetup
This workshop provides a technical foundation for managing large scale computer vision datasets. You will learn to curate, visualize, and evaluate models using the [open source FiftyOne app](https://docs.voxel51.com/).

**Date, Time and Location**

Apr 8, 2026
10 AM PST - 11 AM Pacific
Online. **[Register for the Zoom!](https://voxel51.com/events/getting-started-with-fiftyone-april-8-2026)**

The session covers data ingestion, embedding visualization, and model failure analysis. You will build workflows to identify dataset bias, find annotation errors, and select informative samples for training. Attendees leave with a framework for data centric AI for research and production pipelines, prioritizing data quality over pure model iteration.

What you'll learn

* **Structure unstructured data.** Map data and metadata into a queryable schema for images, videos, and point clouds.
* **Query datasets with the FiftyOne SDK**. Create complex views based on model predictions, labels, and custom tags. Use the FiftyOne to filter data based on logical conditions and confidence scores.
* **Visualize high dimensional embeddings**. Project features into lower dimensions to find clusters of similar samples. Identify data gaps and outliers using FiftyOne Brain.
* **Automate data curation.** Implement algorithmic measures to select diverse subsets for training. Reduce labeling costs by prioritizing high entropy samples.
* **Debug model performance.** Run evaluation routines to generate confusion matrices and precision recall curves. Visualize false positives and false negatives directly in the App to understand model failures.
* **Customize FiftyOne**. Build custom dashboards and interactive panels. Create specialized views for domain specific tasks.

**Prerequisites:**

* Working knowledge of Python and machine learning and/or computer vision fundamentals.
* All attendees will get access to the tutorials and code examples used in the workshop.
source: Meetup: Raleigh AI, ML & Computer Vision

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980073/1:b23ed0dc05ca3fd7fcacbd2b359ed2351dccabc3f8b0c37b9abd0b9655118a94/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Aquatic WILD
description:
Aquatic Wild is a program offered through the NC Wildlife Resources Commission. The North Carolina Aquarium at Fort Fisher and NC Sea Grant are facilitating this workshop.

The Aquatic WILD workshop is for formal and non-formal educators interested in teaching people about aquatic wildlife and ecology. Aquatic WILD materials build upon developments in wildlife conservation needs as well as advances in instructional methodology in Pre-K through 12th grade education.  

Participants in this 6-hour workshop receive the Aquatic WILD K-12 Curriculum & Activity Guide. The guide contains field investigations, In Step With STEM activity extensions, connections to wildlife careers, new activities on fish conservation and angling, and an increased focus on outdoor activities. Project Wild materials are provided free of charge by the NC Wildlife Resources Commission. 

The workshop at the Carolina Beach State Park will have a marine focus. Participants are eligible for Criteria I credit of NC OEE Environmental Education Certification and school teachers receive CEU credit. Homework option available.  

 

Location: Carolina Beach State Park

Cost: $25 per person 

 For additional workshop information, call 910-772-0546.

title: Social Media as Qualitative Data: A Design Framework
description:
Conversations that take place online – from learning management systems to Signal chats to Discord groups to Reddit support groups and Instagram influencing – can be valuable forms of evidence for qualitative researchers. In this workshop, participants will create their own research designs to investigate a phenomenon of interest, as evidenced through social media data, in a systematic and rigorous way. The Paulus and Wise research design decision framework will scaffold learners in how to identify an object of interest; articulate their philosophical and theoretical assumptions about how language functions online; create focused and relevant research questions; ensure methodological alignment across aspects of the study design; resolve ethical dilemmas around treating social media as data; extract and transform social media into a coherent dataset; select appropriate technologies for managing and analyzing data; perform a systematic narrative, discourse and/or thematic analysis; and establish the quality of the findings. Course material will be drawn from Looking for Learning, Insight and Transformation in Online Talk (Paulus & Wise, Routledge, 2019).

Registration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

title: Right to Be's Bystander Intervention to Stop Online Harassment: How to Be an Ally When You Witness Abuse Online
description:
REGISTER HERE: https://righttobe.org/events/bystander-intervention-to-stop-online-harassment-how-to-be-an-ally-when-you-witness-abuse-online-7/ 




PEN America and Right to Be have partnered to provide training on how to intervene safely when you see a journalist attacked online. This training is also brought to you in partnership with the NYC Commission on Gender Equity, the Mayor’s Office of Equity and Racial Justice, and the NYC Commission on Gender Equity.


In this free, one-hour, interactive training, we’ll give you the tools you need to intervene safely and effectively in online abuse using Right To Be’s 5Ds of bystander intervention. First, we’ll talk through what online harassment is and how to identify abusive tactics. We’ll discuss the severe impact online abuse can have – on well being, livelihoods, and free expression – and how it’s drowning out voices that urgently need to be heard in public discourse. Then, we’ll share concrete strategies for how to distract, delegate, document, delay, or directly intervene in a way that is safe, effective, and centered on the needs of the person being targeted.

We’ll have time to practice so you can leave feeling more confident intervening the next time you see someone being abused online. We hope you’ll join us to learn how we can all band together to do your part to protect press freedom and defend the media from attacks on their independence, including online abuse and threats.

title: April 9 - Workshop: Build a Visual Agent that can Navigate GUIs like Humans
description:
Raleigh AI, Machine Learning and Computer Vision Meetup
This hands-on workshop provides a comprehensive introduction to building and evaluating visual agents for GUI automation using modern tools and techniques.

**Date, Time and Location**

April 9, 2026 at 9 AM Pacific
Online. **[Register for the Zoom](https://voxel51.com/events/visual-agents-what-it-takes-to-build-an-agent-that-can-navigate-guis-like-humans-april-9-2026)**

Visual agents that can understand and interact with graphical user interfaces represent a transformative frontier in AI automation. These systems combine computer vision, natural language understanding, and spatial reasoning to enable machines to navigate complex interfaces—from web applications to desktop software—just as humans do. However, building robust GUI agents requires careful attention to dataset curation, model evaluation, and iterative improvement workflows.

Participants will learn how to leverage [FiftyOne](https://github.com/voxel51/fiftyone), an open-source toolkit for dataset curation and computer vision workflows, to build production-ready GUI agent systems.

What You'll Learn:

* **Dataset Creation & Management:** How to structure, annotate, and load GUI interaction datasets using the COCO4GUI standardized format
* **Data Exploration & Analysis:** Using FiftyOne's interactive interface to visualize datasets, analyze action distributions, and understand annotation patterns
* **Multimodal Embeddings:** Computing embeddings for screenshots and UI element patches to enable similarity search and retrieval
* **Model Inference:** Running state-of-the-art models like Microsoft's GUI-Actor to predict interaction points from natural language instructions
* **Performance Evaluation:** Measuring model accuracy using standard metrics and normalized click distance to assess localization precision
* **Failure Analysis:** Investigating model failures through attention maps, error pattern analysis, and systematic debugging workflows
* **Data-Driven Improvement:** Tagging samples based on error types (attention misalignment vs. localization errors) to prioritize fine-tuning efforts
* **Synthetic Data Generation:** Using FiftyOne plugins to augment training data with synthetic task descriptions and variations

*About the Speaker*

[Harpreet Sahota](https://www.linkedin.com/in/harpreetsahota204/) is a hacker-in-residence and machine learning engineer with a passion for deep learning and generative AI. He’s got a deep interest in RAG, Agents, and Multimodal AI.
source: Meetup: Raleigh AI, ML & Computer Vision

title: Become a Lake James State Park Junior Ranger!
location: Lake James State Park- Paddy's Creek Visitor Center
description:
Calling all young explorers, nature lovers, and future park protectors! Join the Lake James State Park Rangers for an exciting Junior Ranger Program designed to help kids earn their official Junior Ranger Badge.

Kick things off by meeting our friendly Park Rangers, who will guide you through the Junior Ranger journey and share what it’s really like to work as a ranger- protecting wildlife, education, firefighting, keeping people safe, and helping visitors enjoy the park. Then the adventure is yours! Set out to explore Lake James State Park at your own pace while completing activities in the Junior Ranger Booklet along the way.

Once you’ve earned enough acorns (points), head back to the Visitor Center to take the Junior Ranger Oath and be officially sworn in as Lake James State Park’s newest Junior Ranger—badge included!

This program is fun, educational, and perfect for curious minds of all ages. Come learn, explore, and discover why Lake James State Park is such a special place!

title: Modular Design and Automated Testing with R
description:
Research is relying on increasingly complex code. However, most academics have not received formal training in software development. This course will introduce participants to some of the best practices of professional software development. Participants will learn to structure their Python code into reusable functions and store those functions as separate scripts and/or packages. Participants will learn to write automated tests for their functions, to help confirm that results are as expected and to ensure that future changes to code do not cause unexpected changes to results.

We will also discuss how to create reproducible “environments” that record package versions in use to help avoid package version incompatibilities and ensure that results can be replicated even months or years after the original analysis.

Modular design is best paired with a version control system. We will discuss how modular design practices integrate with Git and Github, and how to set up “continuous integration” so that automated tests are run each time code is changed.

The course will primarily consist of a hands-on exercise to restructure a single long analysis file into functions and automated tests.

“Version Control with Git and Github” course is a prerequisite for this course.

Registration and fee (refundable for students) are required for Zoom link access.

description:
NC Public Librarian Certification Commission Meeting

The North Carolina Public Librarian Certification Commission was formed in 1936 to set minimum standards for public librarians to protect and maintain public library resources, to assure professional management and administration of library services, to meet the personnel requirements for State Aid administered by the State Library of NC, and to guarantee the best possible public library service to all North Carolinians. Commission meetings are held quarterly to review applications and discuss Certification policies and procedures. 

For information about the NC Public Librarian Certification Commission, please visit https://slnc.info/PLCC.

title: Let’s Craft! American Flag Fans in Little Red Schoolhouse
description: Hand fans were used during the American Revolution to cool off and show support to the new country. Join us in Little Red Schoolhouse and make your own fan! Our Sensory Friendly environment (dim lights, calm room, limited people) will be from 10:00am to 12:00pm. FREE. All ages welcome. Drop-in.

title: Prelude to Revolution: Halifax Resolves Days
description: Celebrate the 250th anniversary of the Halifax Resolves, the first official action by an entire colony recommending independence from Great Britain.  On Friday and Saturday, enjoy two days of living-history activities, historic weapons demonstrations, military drills, and interact with characters from Halifax’s past. Guest speakers will also give presentations on various topics. On Sunday, the living-history activities will continue.  In the afternoon, the NC Society Sons of the American Revolution will host their annual wreath-laying ceremony, and the Historical Halifax Restoration Association will host the annual Halifax Day ceremony, followed by a reception.  Free.

title: Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution
description:
KMHM is excited to present Women at the Edge of War: Carolina Piedmont's Heroines of the American Revolution. This exhibit highlights the daily courage and civic work of women in the Carolinas, whose efforts were essential to Revolutionary resistance and survival on the southern frontier.

The exhibit will run from Feb. 17, 2026 to May 30, 2026. The museum is open Tues-Sat, 10-4. Admission is free. Check out kingsmountainmuseum.org or call 704-739-1019 for more information.

title: UNC Science Expo 2026
description: Join us for a celebration of science learning! The UNC Science Expo is the signature event of the NC Science Festival, administered by Morehead Planetarium and Science Center. More than 100 booths staffed by UNC-Chapel Hill students, researchers, practitioners, and community volunteers fill the quads surrounding Morehead to offer hands-on activities, games, and science demonstrations to community members of all ages.

title: Tour: A Closer Look at "Bill Bamberger: Boys Will Be Men"
description:
Join Ackland educators for a lively half-hour look at a small handful of artworks. These engaging conversational tours offer a closer look at and new perspectives on works on view at the Museum. Today’s tour looks at Bill Bamberger: Boys Will Be Men. 
This is a free, walk-in event – no RSVP required. 
This event is part of UNC–Chapel Hill’s Campus Life Experience (CLE) program. Students who attend can scan the QR code provided at the event to receive CLE credit.

title: Wild Ideas: You Are A Force For Nature
location: NC Museum of Art, 2110 Blue Ridge Rd, Raleigh, NC, 27607, United States
description:
Save the date! Wild Ideas April 2026
TLC invites you to join us at the NC Museum of Art for our annual Wild Ideas event! This year, community partners and TLC staff will host a series of family-friendly activities that intersect art with the environment and celebrate how we can all be forces for nature. 
Visit the art museum park from 10:00am-2:00pm on April 11th to meet local organizations doing amazing work to provide opportunities to enjoy the outdoors and have a positive impact on local natural habitats. The Museum of Art will also host a morning volunteer workday to support their creek watershed restoration project!  More details and a schedule of events will be posted on our Wild Ideas website soon. This is a free event, but preregistration is required. 

Learn more here!

title: Bird Watch, Chapel Hill Public Library
description: Bird Watch takes place on the covered patio at the back of the Chapel Hill Public Library.  New Hope Bird Alliance volunteers assist library staff with helping visitors use binoculars to identify birds coming to the library's feeders and sharing the count with Project Feederwatch, a nation-wide citizen science project.  We also have games for kids and families.

title: Our American Story
location: Western Carolina University,  Coulter Recital Hall
description: Join us in this celebration that tells the American story through music. This concert will feature a wide array of artists offering their unique story on what America means to them. The concert is free and open to the public.

title: Celebrating America's Playlist: The 250-Year Soundtrack of Freedom and Peace
description:
For 250 years, America has expressed its ideals of peace and freedom through music—and now, those songs are coming together in one powerful night. Celebrating America’s Playlist is a live concert that marks the nation’s Semiquincentennial with a soundtrack that spans from 1776 to today.

Set at the Fletcher Opera Theater in Raleigh, this event brings local choirs, soloists, and small groups to the stage—backed by a stellar live band and led by a visionary music director. Each performance is paired with short narration that connects the music to defining moments in U.S. history and to the enduring spirit of liberty that has shaped—and been shaped by—each generation.

Presented by Crabtree Rotary and your local Rotary clubs, this is more than a concert—it’s a celebration that lets the story of our nation sing through the voices of our community.

title: Culture Mill Eclipse
location: Joan H. Gillings ArtSpace at CURRENT
description:
Culture Mill’s Eclipse, which first debuted in 2022, returns to Chapel Hill this season, blurring boundaries and exploring race, place, and Southern identity through movement and community ritual.  

 

Tickets are available starting at $11. Enjoy a variety of season-long discounts, available exclusively for Carolina Performing Arts’ series performances. 

 

$11 Student Tickets 

UNC students + ALL K-college students statewide can purchase $11 tickets for every CPA season show. 

 

Choose Your Price Tickets 

Five percent of tickets are released 24 hours before each show at pay-what-you-can pricing. 

 

Discounts for UNC Employees + More 

Explore our ticketing FAQ to discover discounts for UNC employees, first responders, and more! 

Buy Tickets & Learn More: https://carolinaperformingarts.org/events/culture-mill/

title: The Importance of Being Earnest by Oscar Wilde
description:
The Importance of Being Earnest, a Trivial Comedy for Serious People, is Oscar Wilde’s comedic play, which satirizes the conventions and manners of the late 19th century. Best friends Jack and Algernon each adopt fictional identities to escape social obligations. The plot unfolds with mistaken identities, romantic entanglements, clever wordplay and humorous social commentary.

The play's director is Danica Jenelle Jackson.

title: Bike and Camp Overnight Ride
description: Come with us as we explore historic greenway trails in North Carolina by bicycle.  Outdoor Adventures will transport your camping gear while you take the scenic route to our overnight destination. This entry-level cycling adventure has no major hills and relatively low mileage over two days. Participants will need to know how to ride a bike.

title: Bike Maintenance Class and Group Ride
description:
Every second Sunday, get your biking fix with the Greenway Gear Collective's Bike Maintenance Class and Group Ride. Their 10 a.m. monthly basic maintenance class is a great way to learn the essentials of bike care and tune-ups. Each class is shaped by the needs of those who show up with no two being the same.

Then at noon, you can join a themed group ride starting and ending at The Bike Library container on the shores of Lake Raleigh. Bring your own bike or rent one of theirs. Feel free to show up for the class, the group ride or both.

While both of these events are drop-in accessible and encouraged, signing up in advance for the class or pre-filling out the waiver for the ride is always greatly appreciated.

title: Legends & Landmarks: Paintings and Drawings by James C. Horton
description: In commemoration of America's 250th, the Bellamy Mansion Museum is hosting an art exhibition by local artist James C. Horton, which includes over 40 original paintings and drawings that highlight historic scenes and places from around the region and country that help tell America's story. Visitors can view the exhibit free of charge any time the museum is open (M-Sun. 10 am-4 pm). Please stop by the Carriage ​House Visitor Center to gain admission. Art is available for purchase and an Artist's Reception will be held on February 27th (6-8pm). Visit bellamymansion.org/exhibits for more information or email info@bellamymansion.org if you have questions.

Outdoor Flow Yogis
**S﻿unday Yoga** classes take place monthly at [Trophy Brewing](https://www.trophybrewing.com/brewery-taproom)'s Maywood location. Join us inside the brewery or out on the lawn for a 60-minute yoga flow for all experience levels. Beginners welcome! Bring your mat and any props you like to use.

Registration includes one drink ticket, so stick around after class to enjoy a Trophy pint or non-alcoholic beverage and relax with the crew—great way to start your Sunday FUNday.

Cost: $22 cash/venmo at the door or reserve online [here](https://trophysundayyoga2026.eventbrite.com/).

title: The Art and Science of Plant Care
description:
Caring for plants is both an art and a science — part intuition, part know-how. Every gardener, from beginner to expert, faces the same essential questions: When should I prune? How much should I water? What’s the best way to plant, fertilize or repot? In this class, Bryce Lane will bring decades of horticultural expertise to demystify these everyday tasks, showing you not just what to do but why it works.

Over eight weeks, you’ll explore the fundamental practices that keep gardens thriving, learning how to perform each one with confidence and purpose. You’ll uncover the science behind good technique — understanding how soil, water, nutrients and plant biology interact — while also developing the practical judgment that comes only with experience.

Each session will focus on one key aspect of plant care, building a complete toolkit for growing healthier, more resilient plants in any garden setting.

title: User-Friendly Writing
description: Join the Graduate School for a two-part, in-person workshop that will address the challenges of conveying your research story in a persuasive way for technical and nontechnical stakeholders. The workshop will focus on various writing structures across STEM and humanities fields, including the organization and interpretation of results. These will include technical and nontechnical reports, results and discussion sections and similar types of writing projects.

title: Black Voters Matter Texting Tuesdays
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48337759/1:11a1d2f1e090f57ad061ac58a21703e8a27df07b24dafffaf81eab4a7e991588/

How to join:
You will need at least a basic Zoom account to register.

Join us on Zoom at 6pm ET/5pm CT every week as we gather to drive change through digital organizing. Our texting campaigns will give you the opportunity to engage residents across the U.S. while empowering them to make a positive impact in their communities. Last year, we sent over 19 million texts educating residents on local elections, environmental justice, police reform, health care, and so much more! During the event we will provide training, discuss our latest texting campaign, and we will take action.

A basic Zoom account is required to register.

How to prepare:
Complete your registration here via Zoom: https://bit.ly/BVMTextingTuesday.   

You will need a basic Zoom account to register. A basic Zoom account is free. 

Fill out the volunteer agreement here - https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=4XYntqm7yEqm7h6bNND82BTiriDtw_dLvWG_BUODEPBUMkgyQ1ZQTENCVVBWNTVNQ0pDWVZaNFFLRC4u

title: Global Film Series: On Becoming a Guinea Fowl
location: James B. Hunt Jr. Library, Auditorium
description:
Join the Global Film Series, a collaboration between the NC State University Libraries and NC State’s Office of Global Engagement, for a screening of On Becoming a Guinea Fowl.

On an empty road in the middle of the night, Shula stumbles across the body of her uncle. As funeral proceedings begin, Shula and her cousins bring to light the buried secrets of their middle-class Zambian family. 

The movie runtime is 1 hour, 39 minutes. The film is in English and Bemba with English subtitles. 

Dr. Haddy Njie, associate teaching professor in International Studies at NC State, will introduce the film. 

The Global Film Series is a collaboration between the NC State University Libraries and NC State’s Office of Global Engagement. This screening is in partnership with the NC State Department of International Studies and Africana Studies.

title: Public School Strong Organizing Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46979985/1:df6d648ae6178962cd9fa118925e7246da03f5a1cff98b9de823dc83faeafbe4/

How to join:
Hi there, 

Thanks for signing up for our Public School Strong Orientation & Organizing Meeting!  

Be sure to register on zoom in advance for this meeting:
https://raceforward-org.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIvcuiurz4pE9BGsfJi-mBBX_lhl2bPG7Ti 

After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting.

**We are thrilled to welcome David Backer, author of As Public As Possible: Radical Finance for America’s Public Schools, as our guest speaker at January’s meeting.** 

Join us for the next Public Schools Strong organizing meeting on Thursday, January 22nd, where parents, family members, educators, and supporters from across the nation gather each month to gain practical tools to promote, protect, and strengthen our public schools. 

*If you have already attended a Public School Strong Orientation, you'll join a breakout room with current members for connection, reflection and training. If you're new, we've got you covered too!*

**Do you want honest, equitable and fully-funded public schools?** Acting locally is key.

### Join public school families, educators and supporters from across the country to learn how to:
1. Form a Public School Strong team in your school district
2. Engage your community to get school boards and district leaders to speak up for equitable and fully funded public schools
3. Connect your local advocacy to others for state and national impact

For more info, please visit publicschoolstrong.org

### Current Public School Strong Members

There is strength in staying connected. After completing Public School Strong (PSS) Orientation, this is your consistent space to reconnect with your peers across the country to:

1. Access helpful tools and practice relevant skills,
2. Connect the dots between what is happening locally and nationally,
3. Learn from mistakes and progress made along the way

Bring a friend!

**Self Directed Orientation Now Available!**

If these live training times don't work for you, you can complete your orientation at your own pace:

1) Create Account in NEA Portal (open to non-NEA members too): 

neapartnera.learnupon.com/users/sign_up

2) Select the PSS orientation by clicking on this link:

bit.ly/SelfDirectedPSS

How to prepare:
### Hi there! Thanks for signing up for a 'Public School Strong Organizing Meeting!' (for new folks we'll also have a new member orientation)

This event is virtual, will provide updates on the federal attacks on our public schools and the immigrant community, actions you can take right now and the tools to support you in doing son. 

For new folks, we'll provide an introduction to our Public School Strong campaign. Check out our organizing platform using this link to find or create a local team: 

- publicschoolstrong.org

More on this powerful tool at your Orientation...

https://www.mobilize.us/raceforward/event/596321/

title: Taller Mensual de MamásConPoder
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367860/1:f8618d5aa26bbf8fd6bfe93a7b40aefa4cc9ec3f4582ad5f9c00dd016abd23c6/

How to join:
Únete a la Reunión en este link: https://momsrising.zoom.us/j/84073901424

Te invitamos a participar en nuestro Taller de Salud Mental este miercoles 29 de Octubre a las 5:30 Hora del Pacifico, impartido por la **Dra. Sandra Martínez**, psicóloga y psicoterapeuta con más de 20 años de experiencia brindando apoyo emocional con un enfoque empático, profesional y humano. Taller de Salud Mental, 

**Este evento es gratuito y lo único que se necesita para entrar es tener una cuenta de Zoom. **

Este espacio está diseñado especialmente para la comunidad latina en EE. UU., con el propósito de ofrecer recursos prácticos y estrategias útiles para mejorar nuestra calidad de vida y afrontar con fortaleza estos tiempos de cambio e incertidumbre.

Durante el taller, hablaremos sobre temas esenciales como:

 - Bienestar emocional: la importancia de cuidar nuestra salud mental.

 - Manejo del estrés, miedo y ansiedad: herramientas y técnicas para recuperar el equilibrio.

 - Creación de espacios seguros: cómo cultivar la salud desde nosotros mismos.

 - Acompañamiento a nuestros hijos y comunidad: cómo, como madres y líderes, podemos apoyar, resistir y manejar el miedo.

 - Educación y apoyo en crisis: cómo acompañar de manera efectiva a quienes están pasando por momentos difíciles.

Además, contaremos con una sesión de preguntas y respuestas con la Dra. Sandra Martínez, donde podrás resolver tus dudas y compartir tus experiencias.

**Regístrate ahora para recibir notificaciones y recordatorios sobre el próximo encuentro.** 

¡Tu bienestar emocional también importa!

**NOTA IMPORTANTE: Tomamos muy en serio tu privacidad y toda tu información compartida por aquí o por cualquier otro medio con MamásConPoder está protegida y no se comparte con nadie más.**

HORA DEL EVENTO de acuerdo a tu zona horaria:

- 5:30 p.m. Hora del Pacifico
- 5:30 p.m. Hora de la Montaña
- 7:30 p.m. Hora del Centro
- 8:30 p.m. Hora del Este

Evento organizado por MamásConPoder para la comunidad.

------------

How to prepare:

En este Taller de Salud Mental  para padres de McP platicaremos sobre los temas que actualmente nos preocupan a la comunidad latina en EE. title: *VIRTUAL* SF/F Group
description:
Durham Writers Group
**Zoom Info Below Description**

This sub-group focuses on Science Fiction and Fantasy, so if you have anything that falls under that category — be it short story, poem, novel-in-progress or screenplay — then come! We'd love to hear from you.

Our format allows each reader 15 minutes to present their work... followed by approximately 20 minutes of feedback from the other attendees. To ensure a fair opportunity to respond, each attendee is limited to 1 minute of feedback time. (We usually get through three readers.)

If a reader elects to post their work in advance (NOT a requirement!), members will be able to access that work at our Google Group:
https://groups.google.com/d/forum/durham-sff-writers-group

We ask all members to provide a warning, both on the Google Group and during the meeting, if their story features content that some readers might find upsetting. This includes descriptions of sexual violence.

title: Languishing, Flourishing, and Your Mental Health
description:
Over the past few years, employees have been asked to do more, do their work differently and do better in new and challenging ways, all while managing family life and personal responsibilities that seem never-ending. The feeling of languishing or “will this ever end?” abounds throughout the workforce and can have a huge impact on your mental health. Working on our mental health need not be reserved just for those who are in crisis or who are faced with an official diagnosis: Mental health matters for everyone. This interactive session provides more details and helpful ideas on how to move from languishing through work and life to flourishing.

 

Workshop Objectives
This training will help you:
• Understand the concepts of languishing and flourishing
• Reflect on your current mental wellness status
• Describe three concrete approaches to move from languishing to flourishing
• Identify and action plan to implement within the next 30 days to move toward flourishing

title: From AI to ROI: Turning Pilots Into Scalable Impact
description:
Data Science Raleigh
Every enterprise has its share of AI proof-of-concepts—many promising, few enduring. This webinar goes beyond the hype to explore what it actually takes to move from experimental AI pilots to sustained, enterprise-wide value. We’ll dig into the operational, organizational, and architectural levers that help AI investments move out of the sandbox and into the business mainstream.

Through expert discussion and real-world examples, we’ll explore the friction points that stall scaling efforts—and what forward-thinking leaders are doing to overcome them. If you're leading or influencing AI adoption in your organization, this session is about turning progress into performance.

**Key Takeaways:**

1️⃣ **Real Product Experience:** See full workflows in motion—not just the highlights.

2️⃣ **Operational Readiness:** What’s required in terms of data infrastructure, governance, and cross-functional collaboration to support scaled AI.

3️⃣ **Organizational Design:** How roles, incentives, and workflows evolve when AI becomes a core operating capability.

4️⃣ **Sustaining Value:** Strategies for continuous iteration, observability, and measuring long-term ROI from AI investments.

**[Register here](https://hubs.ly/Q041vh4c0)**

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980074/1:b041eb34d713fa0181836c2a4400c7d26526c8244d26b4e768fd83cf0f55c267/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Architecture Lecture: Andrew E. Thompson, Preservation NJ
location: AIA NC Center for Architecture and Design
description:
Please join the College of Design for the spring 2026 architecture lecture series. This is the AIA Triangle Design Award Jury Chair Lecture. 

Andrew E. Thompson, Preservation NJ

Serving in his first term on the Preservation New Jersey Board is Andrew E. Thompson, who has been in the field of architecture for 30 years. His education includes City University of New York, the Pratt Institute, and Columbia University.

Andrew’s extensive career includes designing structural components for the Arleigh Burke Class Navy Destroyer vessel; the Port Authority – working on projects in Tower One of the World Trade Center, Newark Airport, and the Air Train for JFK airport; Memorial Sloan-Kettering Cancer Center where he served as the Chief Architect; and the City University of New York, where he was responsible for building their first new community college in 40 years. In 2013, Andrew came to New Jersey taking the position of County Architect for Passaic County.

title: Read Smart Author Event: The Legend of Wyatt Outlaw
description: Join Belle Boggs, professor in the Department of English at NC State, and Sylvester Allen Jr. as they discuss their new book, The Legend of Wyatt Outlaw: From Reconstruction Through Black Lives Matter. Outlaw’s story was one of Black success — he was a Union League leader, business owner and the first Black town constable and commissioner in Graham, a small town in North Carolina’s Alamance County. But in 1870, the Ku Klux Klan murdered Outlaw, setting off a dramatic series of events. Allen and Boggs both sought to discover why Outlaw had been erased from mainstream history books. In The Legend of Wyatt Outlaw, they share what they found in artful detail and connect Outlaw’s story to the violence against Black people in Alamance and throughout the United States, from Reconstruction through Jim Crow, the civil rights era and Black Lives Matter.

The plot unfolds with mistaken identities, romantic entanglements, clever wordplay and humorous social commentary.

The play's director is Danica Jenelle Jackson.

title: Geospatial Forum: Global Data for Local Action
description:
The Geospatial Forum will host a lecture by Ted Wong, a data scientist and research associate on the Urban Analytics team at the World Resources Institute's Ross Center for Sustainable Cities.

The World Resources Institute (WRI) is a large environmental nongovernmental organization that provides geospatial data and analyses to local governments to support sustainable urban planning. This talk will examine how WRI builds and deploys data products supporting city-level planning and decision-making for extreme heat, urban biodiversity and decarbonization. We will discuss theories of change, user research, project-level business models, strategies for mainstreaming, representation of uncertainty and all the technical considerations behind our data products.

title: Celebrating Our Colonial Roots - Online Plant Sale
location: Pick up 5/1/26 at Greenhouses behind Building N
description: The Brunswick County Master Gardeners Volunteer Association will hold an online plant sale starting on April 17, 2026 and running until April 24, 2026 with pick up day on May 1, 2026. This event celebrates the important colonial history of Brunswick County  through education on herbs, flowers, vines, shrubs and small trees which thrive here. Plants featured are those which our Colonial ancestors would have encountered along our coast and the Cape Fear River which are native to our region and support pollinators. Also featured will be plants from seeds which colonists brought with them from Europe and plants which recognize the contributions and life celebrations of the Gullah Geechee people who were enslaved in Africa to work the rice plantations along the Cape Fear River. This online plant sale celebrates the cultural traditions  found in gardening in our coastal region of the State.

title: The Psychology Behind Saving Money and Other Good Financial Habits
description:
Most people can honestly say they believe it’s important to spend within their means and to save money for the future. Unfortunately, good intentions are often at odds with how our mind works. For example, many people want to save for the future but have no motivation to because they have not imagined that future. This course addresses the psychology behind saving money and adopting good financial habits. It will examine recent research related to how and why people save vs. spend and identify strategies for participants to change their financial mindset and develop new habits.

 

Workshop Objectives
At the end of this workshop, you will be able to:
• Understand why some people save for the future and other spend for the moment
• Identify non-productive financial patterns and habits
• Recognize the mental and behavioral habits of people who save
• Create a compelling financial future to motivate behavior change
• Understand how to improve the capacity to delay gratification while increasing enjoyment
• Identify seven simple tools to help change spending habits

title: Let's Go Fishing!
location: Lake James State Park-Catawba River Access
description:
Join us for a relaxing morning fishing program at Lake James State Park – Catawba River Access, perfect for families and first-time anglers alike! Bring your kids, your favorite fishing rods, and any tackle you have—we’ll also have a limited number of rods and tackle available to loan.

We’ll meet at the picnic shelter, then head together to the jetty, where there’s plenty of space to spread out and fish. Spend the morning enjoying the calm water, learning a few fishing tips, and sharing quality time with loved ones while making lasting memories—plus, you just might reel in a fish or two!

Whether you’re teaching your child to fish for the first time or simply enjoying a peaceful morning outdoors, this program is a great way to connect with nature and each other.

All ages welcome. No experience necessary! All NCWRC Laws must be followed such as having a valid NC fishing license.🎣
