title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980082/1:9ed9328480d9be2e4e0f42706c5e63964befa7d17146153229d59e37a2befcfd/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Webinar: 7(a) Manufacturers’ Access to Revolving Credit Loans
description:
Join NC State Industry Expansion Solutions for a webinar that will focus on the Small Business Administration's (SBA) new 7(a) Manufacturers’ Access to Revolving Credit (MARC) Loans.

MARC is SBA’s first-ever loan program dedicated to supporting America’s small manufacturers, which make up 98% of all U.S. manufacturers. The program will offer working capital for small businesses engaged in manufacturing and is specifically designed to provide maximum flexibility and minimal red tape.

MARC complements the SBA’s core 7(a) and 504 loan programs, providing a flexible new line of credit to manufacturers and lenders. MARC loans can be used in combination with SBA and conventional commercial loans, making it a potent new tool in support of SBA's effort to reshore American industrial dominance.

title: Siler City History Club
description:
Join the Chatham County Historical Association for the Siler City History Club on every second Thursday of the month, 10:30am-11:30am at First Baptist Church. The Chatham County Historical Association needs your help in learning about the rich and colorful history of Siler City! They have facts and figures, but they need to hear your own memories of Siler to really understand one of Chatham County’s most important cities. Come share your memories, stories, photos, mementos, and other Siler City treasures. Light refreshments will be served. This event is free, and everyone is welcome! No registration is required.

This event takes place indoors. First Baptist Church in Siler City is ADA-accessible. No pets allowed. Please leave your furry friends at home. Free parking is available next to the church.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events. See more events hosted by The Chatham Historical Association.

title: Taller Mensual de MamásConPoder
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367862/1:01539ee7c6ddaa6ffb98f7838aa85fb4a9d3266fa88041fb2f9291029d721716/

How to join:
Únete a la Reunión en este link: https://momsrising.zoom.us/j/84073901424

Te invitamos a participar en nuestro Taller de Salud Mental este miercoles 29 de Octubre a las 5:30 Hora del Pacifico, impartido por la **Dra. Sandra Martínez**, psicóloga y psicoterapeuta con más de 20 años de experiencia brindando apoyo emocional con un enfoque empático, profesional y humano. Taller de Salud Mental, 

**Este evento es gratuito y lo único que se necesita para entrar es tener una cuenta de Zoom. **

Este espacio está diseñado especialmente para la comunidad latina en EE. UU., con el propósito de ofrecer recursos prácticos y estrategias útiles para mejorar nuestra calidad de vida y afrontar con fortaleza estos tiempos de cambio e incertidumbre.

Durante el taller, hablaremos sobre temas esenciales como:

 - Bienestar emocional: la importancia de cuidar nuestra salud mental.

 - Manejo del estrés, miedo y ansiedad: herramientas y técnicas para recuperar el equilibrio.

 - Creación de espacios seguros: cómo cultivar la salud desde nosotros mismos.

 - Acompañamiento a nuestros hijos y comunidad: cómo, como madres y líderes, podemos apoyar, resistir y manejar el miedo.

 - Educación y apoyo en crisis: cómo acompañar de manera efectiva a quienes están pasando por momentos difíciles.

Además, contaremos con una sesión de preguntas y respuestas con la Dra. Sandra Martínez, donde podrás resolver tus dudas y compartir tus experiencias.

**Regístrate ahora para recibir notificaciones y recordatorios sobre el próximo encuentro.** 

¡Tu bienestar emocional también importa!

**NOTA IMPORTANTE: Tomamos muy en serio tu privacidad y toda tu información compartida por aquí o por cualquier otro medio con MamásConPoder está protegida y no se comparte con nadie más.**

HORA DEL EVENTO de acuerdo a tu zona horaria:

- 5:30 p.m. Hora del Pacifico
- 5:30 p.m. Hora de la Montaña
- 7:30 p.m. Hora del Centro
- 8:30 p.m. Hora del Este

Evento organizado por MamásConPoder para la comunidad.

------------

How to prepare:
Gracias por inscribirte para participar en nuestro evento el miércoles 29 de octubre a las 5:30 p. m. PT / 8:30 p. m. ET

En este Taller de Salud Mental  para padres de McP platicaremos sobre los temas que actualmente nos preocupan a la comunidad latina en EE. UU., con el propósito de ofrecer recursos prácticos y estrategias útiles para mejorar nuestra calidad de vida y afrontar con fortaleza estos tiempos de cambio e incertidumbre.

title: The Media and Democracy Project - What activists can do: Fix Media Now
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47485920/1:f95f303f9a77031229ad21f50c5387361c58810f28a2d14827c5ad468bb9986b/

Join the Media and Democracy Project to better understand and respond to the factors that influence public opinion, voters and democracy. Each session we will activate on a current information-related subject to help Fix Media Now. 

We develop and distribute skills to navigate the Information War on truth and democracy. 

We are a non-partisan all volunteer group that engages in actions to directly improve the quality of public information such as messaging, fighting disinformation, engaging in media accountability and in growing support for local journalism. 

We see journalists and the media as accountable allies essential for an informed democracy, countering dangerous disinformation and propaganda and promoting the common good.

How to prepare:
Bring a friend - send them the link. 
Activate your social networks.

https://www.mobilize.us/mediademocracyproject/event/430177/

title: Connecting Mind and Body for Healthy Living
description:
Your body responds to the way you think, feel and act. This is often called the “mind/body connection.” When you are stressed, anxious or upset, your body tries to tell you that something isn’t right. For example, high blood pressure might develop after a particularly stressful event, such as the death of a loved one. People with good emotional health are aware of their thoughts, feelings and behaviors. They have learned healthy ways to cope with the stress and problems that are a normal part of life. They feel good about themselves and have healthy relationships. Poor emotional health can weaken your body’s immune system, making you more likely to get colds and other infections during emotionally difficult times. Also, when you are feeling stressed, anxious or upset, you may not take care of your health as well as you should. You may not feel like exercising, eating nutritious foods or taking medicine that your doctor prescribes. Abuse of alcohol, tobacco or other drugs may also be a sign of poor emotional health.

 

Workshop Objectives
At the end of this workshop, you will be able to:
Identify the relationship between mind and body
• Describe how the body influences emotional and mental functioning
• Describe how our emotions, beliefs and self talk impact our body
• Identify symptoms of a possible mind/body disconnection
Identify ways to enhance the mind/body connection
• Describe methods for achieving balance in life
• Identify ways to express feelings appropriately
• Describe the importance of taking care of your health
• Describe ways to enhance awareness
Identify your personal action plan for mind/body connection
• Recognize the importance of consulting with your physician
• Describe which mind/body tools need to develop
• List outside resources for further help

 

Click on Carolina Talent registration link and select the “Request” button to automatically enroll.

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980083/1:4ba7b5235024f54744b884574753d1b2440570a4cae520ac58ffdbc327f526cb/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Become a Certified Bike Safety Leader
description:
NC State Transportation is thrilled to host a three-day League Cycling Instructor (LCI) Seminar from June 19 to June 21, led by experienced League Cycling Coach Leta Highsmith. This intensive training empowers prospective educators to master the League of American Bicyclists’ Smart Cycling curriculum, preparing them to teach riders of all ages to navigate traffic confidently, handle their bikes safely and ride legally.

Participants must complete the Smart Cycling Complete class and purchase an individual League of American Bicyclists membership as prerequisites for the class.

Spaces are limited, so please register by May 21 to secure your spot for the seminar. Visit the NC State Transportation webpage for more information or contact Demar Bonnemere at demar_bonnemere@ncsu.edu  with any questions.

title: 18th Century Law and Order in the Historical Park
description: How did North Carolinians keep crime down and conduct legal matters in the backcountry? John Haley was a tax collector and sheriff - what was work like? Join our costumed interpreters as they talk about how 18th century society was policed and how laws were kept. FREE. All ages welcome. Drop-in.

title: Small Arms Demonstration 10:30am & 2:30pm
description:
Learn about one of the small arms (musket, flintlock, or rifle) that a soldier stationed at Fort Macon would have carried and used.

This will include a blank firing demonstration of the weapon.
Meet in the fort parade ground.

 

Photo by Jairus Hill : Living historian demonstrating a musket at Fort Macon State Park.

title: Discover Tryon Palace: Outlander
location: Tryon Palace Historic Sites and Gardens
description:
Step into Tryon Palace and explore Diana Gabaldon’s award-winning Outlander series. Learn about the lives of the fictional Jamie and Claire Fraser, their family and friends, and interactions with historical figures and events in the tumultuous period of the American Revolution.

Tour covers storylines from Outlander books 4 – 8 and the Starz Outlander seasons 4 – 8. (For those not current on the books or show, there will be spoilers for these books and seasons.)

Tour begins at 301 George Street, across from the Tryon Palace Main Gates. Ticket holders should check in at the Waystation ticketing desk no later than 3:30 p.m.

description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47685202/1:8dcd8b332ebbbe5fef16c0dbd954b2182d6e312c15e753dfb42772f070ff5760/

## VoteRiders is seeking dedicated virtual and in-person volunteers to provide free voter ID assistance to eligible voters in key communities where VoteRiders has staff on the ground including *Houston (TX), Columbus (GA), Pittsburgh (PA), Orlando (FL) and Charlotte (NC), , Milwaukee (WI), and Atlanta (GA)*.

This unique opportunity will make an immediate impact and directly help folks secure the ID they need to cast a ballot that counts. 

Are you detail-oriented, reliable, computer savvy, and passionate about people? We need you for this ongoing opportunity!

### How you can help: 

Provide virtual and, if convenient, in-person ID help to people who have requested assistance, through our partners or self-referral. Work one-on-one with voters on your schedule!  (Requirement:  ~3 hours per week).

** Volunteers will need to attend at least one buddy training after this call.  Attend this orientation to receive sign-up information for buddy training.

title: Goldston America 250 Film Series
description:
Join the Chatham Community Library for this Goldston America 250 Film Series, hosted every fourth Tuesday, January-December 2026, 4pm-6pm. America 250’s mission is to honor and commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, marking America’s Semiquincentennial. The Goldston Library will screen the following documentaries and films:

Tue, Jan 27, 4pm-6pm The Congress, Ken Burns.
Tue, Feb 24, 4pm-6pm Becoming Frederick Douglass, Stanley Nelson and Nicole London.
Tue, Mar 24, 4pm-6pm Rachel Carson: The Woman Who Launched the Modern Environmental Movement, Michelle Ferrari.
Tue, Apr 28, 4pm-6pm Thomas Jefferson: A View from the Mountain, Martin Doblmeier.
Tue, May 26, 4pm-6pm The Address, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jun 23, 4pm-6pm The Brooklyn Bridge, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jul 28, 4pm-6pm The Statue of Liberty, Ken Burns.
Tue, Aug 25, 4pm-6pm American Coup: Wilmington 1898, Yoruba Richen and Brad Lichtenstein.
Tue, Sep 22, 4pm-6pm How it Feels to be Free, Yoruba Richen.
Tue, Oct 27, 4pm-6pm 80 Years Later, Celine Parreñas Shimizu.
Tue, Nov 24, 4pm-6pm Gather, Sanjay Rawal.
Tue, Dec 22, 4pm-6pm Women of World War II: The Untold Stories, Eli Brown.

This FREE, family-friendly event is open to the public. No registration is required. The event will take place indoors in the Goldston Library. The Goldston Library is ADA-compliant, including wheelchair-accessible parking, entrances, seating, common spaces, walkways, and restrooms. Free parking is available outside the library. Pets are not allowed - please leave your furry friends at home.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events.

Join us on Zoom at 6pm ET/5pm CT every week as we gather to drive change through digital organizing. Our texting campaigns will give you the opportunity to engage residents across the U.S. while empowering them to make a positive impact in their communities. Last year, we sent over 19 million texts educating residents on local elections, environmental justice, police reform, health care, and so much more! title: Supporting Employee Well-Being: What can you do as a manager?
description:
Organizations are investing in benefits to improve employee wellbeing, and taking on initiatives to foster a culture of wellbeing in the workplace. However, many employees still feel that their job is an obstacle to their wellbeing rather than a help. What can you do as a manager to shift that perception? This session will discuss practical ways to show employees that you and your organization support their wellbeing.

 

Workshop Objectives
This training will help you:
• Describe ways in which a manager can promote employee wellbeing
• Become an advocate for wellbeing resources available to your employees
• Have productive conversations with employees to address concerns that work is negatively impacting their wellbeing
• Lead by example in demonstrating that you place a high priority on your own wellbeing

 

Click on Carolina Talent registration link and select the “Request” button to automatically enroll.

title: Wildflower & Pollinator ID Hike
description:
In honor of National Pollinator Week, join a ranger at Boone Fork Parking Area (milepost 299.9 on the Blue Ridge Parkway) for a wildflower and pollinator ID hike along the Upper Boone Fork Trail. Learn how to ID some common blooming wildflower species of the high country, and some local pollinator species that keep those flowers blooming every year. 

This 1.5 mile easy hike does have some tree roots and rocks to walk over, so please wear appropriate hiking shoes and dress for the weather.

There are no bathrooms at Boone Fork Parking Area.

title: Midday Meander
location: Onsite at Duke Gardens – directions to follow
description:
Join Kavanah Anderson, director of learning and community engagement at Duke Gardens, for a conversational stroll in the garden that deepens your relationship with plants. Swap plant stories, dig into horticultural history, question what you know and practice multisensory observation on a playful amble through the Gardens that delights and disrupts your understanding of what a garden can be. Expect to learn from each other, share what you know and leave with more questions than you started with. 
Fee: Free; registration required. 
Parking: Parking fees apply. 
Location: Onsite at Duke Gardens. Meeting location will be included in a program reminder email sent before the program. 
Accessibility: This will be a small group program that takes place outdoors, and participants will travel on a combination of paved and unpaved paths with some uneven surfaces and moderate inclines.

title: Dr. M.T. Pope House Presents: Evelyn's Book Club
description:
This program honors Evelyn Pope by reading books. 

Evelyn, a college librarian born in 1908 and died in 1995, loved a variety of books from The Adventures of Tom Sawyer to Shakespeare and James Baldwin. The program brings community members together monthly at the museum to discuss books, focusing on works by Black writers or featuring Black subjects, including fiction and non-fiction. Discussions will use questions to facilitate conversation.

"Mama Day" by Gloria Naylor
Wednesday, Feb. 25
 "The Black Angels: The Untold Story of the Nurses Who Helped Cure Tuberculosis" by Maria Smilios
Wednesday, April 22
 "A Visitation of Spirits" by Randall Kenan
Wednesday, June 24
 "Becoming Lunsford Lane: The Lives of an American Aeneas" by Craig Friend
Wednesday, Aug. 26
 "Ring Shout" by P. Dejeli Clark
Thursday, Oct. 29Registration opens Jan. 23.  An optional book bundle will be available to purchase at registration. Books can be picked up at the Dr. M.T. Pope House Museum.

title: Tours of the Moravian Archives Vault--America 250 Theme
description:
Join us for a tour of the Moravian Archives Vault featuring the collection materials from the Revolutionary War and early American History!

Guests will walk through the Archives Vault which contains millions of records from the settlement of the Moravians here in North Carolina in the 1750s on. Highlighted items will include correspondence from George Washington, the Fourth of July Proclamation, maps, congressional records and much, much more!

Learn about Moravian History and the creation of our nation while seeing how such collection materials are preserved for our posterity!

title: Reunión Mensual Comunitaria de MomsRising
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367848/1:cac5d7f5ee4da2616adc8e474a9b229a7f8f668b556c41fee14c34ecb7ab5d62/

REUNIÓN DEL 29 DE ENERO: 

**¿“Mamás unidas y poderosas”? ¡El verdadero poder de las madres en 2026 (¡Tú!). Únete a MomsRising para una reunión comunitaria por Zoom el jueves 29 de enero a las 8:30 p. m. ET / 5:30 p. m. PT.**


Únete a MomsRising para nuestra primera reunión comunitaria de este 2026. Líderes de nuestro movimiento compartirán actualizaciones sobre temas clave como Medicaid e inmigración, así como vías específicas y estratégicas para alzar nuestra voz y tomar acción.  

¡Porque este año hay que estar más presentes que nunca! Acompáñanos para conectar, inspirarnos y planificar la estrategia que necesitamos para 2026.

Por favor, invita a todas las personas que conozcas a unirse a la reunión, incluyendo a quienes estén listas para actuar, construir poder para los tiempos que se avecinan y defender nuestra educación, atención médica, asistencia nutricional y mucho más.

Juntas, podemos crear un futuro más brillante y esperanzador. ¿Te unes a nosotras?

*****Este evento se llevará a cabo en inglés, con traducción simultánea disponible en español.

How to prepare:
Gracias por registrarte en nuestra próxima reunión comunitaria de MomsRising.

Para unirte a la llamada debes tener una cuenta de Zoom.

Nos reunimos el cuarto jueves de cada mes por Zoom a las 5:30 pm PT / 8:30 pm ET para hablar de temas que son importantes para todas las mamás del país. Los eventos son en inglés, con traducción simultánea al español.



**EL DÍA DE LA REUNIÓN PUEDES UNIRTE AQUÍ:**
https://momsrising.zoom.us/j/87977097086

title: Avery County Heritage Festival
description: The Avery County Heritage Festival commemorates the history and heritage of our county including the genealogies of our Avery County families.  Traditional craftsmen will demonstrate pioneer life.  Entertainment is provided by local musicians playing traditional bluegrass and gospel selections.  Food and craft vendors will be onsite also.

title: Pioneer Heritage Festival in Waynesville, NC
description: The Pioneer Heritage Festival will promote Haywood County's early pioneer history.  Heritage demonstrations will be held on the grounds of the Shelton House Museum with artisans showcasing the skills that were needed for daily living in the early 1800s.  Exhibits depicting North Carolina's role in the American Revolution will be on display in the Shelton House barn.  Members of SAR and DAR will be onsite to help visitors learn how to identify ancestors who fought in the American Revolution.  The Festival is free and includes food trucks, live music, activities for kids, and Shelton House Museum tours.

title: Chatham Celebrates America 250: Cumulative Celebration
description:
July 4, 2026, marks 250 years since the signing of the Declaration of Independence. Join Chatham Celebrates America 250 in recognizing the Semiquincentennial during our

July 4, 2026, marks 250 years since the signing of the Declaration of Independence. Join Chatham Celebrates America 250 in recognizing the Semiquincentennial during our Cumulative Celebration on Sat, Jun 27, 2026, 10am-2pm. Admission is free. Together, we’ll remember the events that have shaped our history, reflect on the present, and envision a promising future for the next generation.

Festivities will include live music and performances, food trucks, vendors, displays and demonstrations, activities for children, and much more. If you are interested in participating, volunteering, or getting involved in other ways, please contact Lindsay Ray at lindsay.ray@chathamcountync.gov or 919.545.8302.

This free, family-friendly event takes place indoors at the Chatham County Agriculture & Conference Center (CCACC). Shuttle services will be provided by Chatham Transit. CCACC is an ADA-compliant space, featuring wheelchair-accessible parking, entrances, shared spaces, and walkways. Since most of the festivities will take place indoors, this event will not be pet-friendly. Please leave your furry friends at home. Service animals are welcome.

See more America 250 events. Learn more on the Chatham Celebrates America 250 Microsite. See more events at the Chatham County Agriculture & Conference Center.

America 250 NC is North Carolina's commemoration planning organization responsible for leading the initiative and collaborating with various local, state, and federal partners.

title: NCMA Community Day
description: Celebrate Knowing the West and the America 250 commemoration with a free, family-friendly day of artmaking, performances, and community connection at the North Carolina Museum of Art. Enjoy hands-on art activities inspired by Western North Carolina craft traditions, live music, and creative demonstrations from regional artists. Explore the exhibition Knowing the West through guided tours, family-friendly activities, and opportunities to engage with artists and partners from across the state.

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980085/1:12068bd2fcb1140a221dbca821b62f0153c4777a3a1bf80b38a695cf64eaeadb/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Independence Day Celebrations at Island Farm
description:
Celebrate Independence Day and the nation’s Semiquincentennial with Island Farm! On Saturday, July 4, 2026, from 9 a.m. until 3 p.m., Island Farm will host visitors to celebrate the holiday. Attendees are encouraged to bring a picnic lunch to enjoy on the Farm’s grassy lawn, and continue celebrations of the nation’s birth with musket-fire, traditional games, a reading of the Declaration of Independence, and much more.

A living history farm, Island Farm is a tangible glimpse into coastal history, and the realities of life on Roanoke Island in the mid-1800’s. Admission to the site is $11, and children 3 and under are admitted for free. The site is located at 1140 N US Highway 64, north of Manteo on Roanoke Island.

Island Farm is owned and operated by Outer Banks Conservationists, a 501(c)(3) non-profit organization founded in 1980 to protect important natural, cultural, and historic resources along North Carolina’s Outer Banks through education and conservation.

title: Independence Day
description: Militia muster demonstration on the Courthouse Green at 9:30 Courthouse open for free from 11:00-2:00. Sign your very own copy of the Declaration of Independence.

title: July 4th Capitol Celebration, North Carolina State Capitol
description: The gathering at the N.C. State Capitol will celebrate Independence Day and America’s Semiquincentennial. The free, fun-filled family event will feature musical performances, historic interpreters, hand-on activities and exhibits, a reading of the Declaration of Independence, and a naturalization ceremony, which is held each year at the Capitol. More information on this event will be announced soon.

title: Independence Day Celebration
description:
Celebrate Independence Day with the Museum of the Waxhaws from 12–4 PM as we honor America’s 250th anniversary in true patriotic fashion!

Watch for us in the Town of Waxhaw’s July 4th Parade, then follow us back to the museum for an afternoon filled with living history, special presentations, and hands-on demonstrations. Experience a powerful reading of the Declaration of Independence, enjoy kids’ crafts, and take part in family-friendly activities that bring our nation’s story to life.

There’s no better way to celebrate 250 years of freedom, sacrifice, and independence!

title: Independence Day Celebration
description: Musket firing, living-history demonstrations, and a special reading of the Declaration of Independence will combine to make this a Fourth of July to remember in the historic town of Halifax!  Continue the fun with the Town of Halifax’s celebration that afternoon and enjoy the annual fireworks display, at dark on the Halifax County Courthouse lawn. Free.

title: City of Wilmington 4th of July Celebration
description: City of Wilmington's family friendly event will include music featuring the Wilmington Symphony Orchestra, other entertainment, kids activities, food, fireworks & more!

title: Fourth of July Symphony Pops Concert
description:
CELEBRATING MUSIC THROUGH 250 YEARS AND BEYOND!

Celebrate the Fourth of July with a spectacular family-friendly concert at the Live Oak Bank Pavilion, featuring the Wilmington Symphony Orchestra (WSO) and special guests.

Highlights of the evening include guest appearances, patriotic favorites, pops, classics, and spectacular fireworks display.

Fireworks will start at 9:00 p.m., launched from a barge at the convergence of the Cape Fear and Northeast Cape Fear Rivers, just north of the park. This concert is FREE to attend!

title: Red, White, and Blue Bash
description: Join the City of Sanford for a fun-filled evening at Red, White, and Blue Bash as we celebrate Independence Day in style! Enjoy live performances from incredible bands, indulge in a variety of tasty treats from local food trucks, and wrap up the night with a breathtaking fireworks show. It's an event for all ages, perfect for making memories with family and friends as we come together to honor freedom and enjoy the summer night!

title: Downtown Techies Durham and Raleigh MERGE FOR THE SUMMER!
description:
Downtown Techies: Durham
Hey Techies!

We are so excited to see you all again! Heads up! for the summer months we are merging all of our events into an event once a month on the first Tuesday of every month!

Don't forget to RSVP for first round free!!

We will be resuming Downtown Durham events in September on the third Tuesday of every month. We would love your suggestions on venues! Just ping us.

Questions or suggestions?

Our digital door is always open at www.downtowntechies.com or @downtowntechies. Check out our events in Raleigh as well!

Meet, Drink and be TECHIE!

-Jake and Blake

Looking to get more connected to the local tech scene, opportunity or talent? Email us info@downtowntechies.com and we will make sure you find what you need!

description: The NC Public Librarian Certification Commission was formed in 1936 to set minimum standards for public librarians to protect and maintain public library resources, to assure professional management and administration of library services, to meet the personnel requirements for State Aid administered by the State Library of NC, and to guarantee the best possible public library service to all North Carolinians.

title: NC Public Librarian Certification Commission Meeting
location: North Carolina State Library of Accessible Books and Library Services, Room 308
description:
NC Public Librarian Certification Commission Meeting

The North Carolina Public Librarian Certification Commission was formed in 1936 to set minimum standards for public librarians to protect and maintain public library resources, to assure professional management and administration of library services, to meet the personnel requirements for State Aid administered by the State Library of NC, and to guarantee the best possible public library service to all North Carolinians. Commission meetings are held quarterly to review applications and discuss Certification policies and procedures. 

For information about the NC Public Librarian Certification Commission, please visit https://slnc.info/PLCC.

title: The Media and Democracy Project - What activists can do: Fix Media Now
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47485921/1:621c0f5ac4927795f22b3b0188b4aa4b037ae57b931880d3e6f78e5eba13f991/

Join the Media and Democracy Project to better understand and respond to the factors that influence public opinion, voters and democracy. Each session we will activate on a current information-related subject to help Fix Media Now.

We develop and distribute skills to navigate the Information War on truth and democracy.

We are a non-partisan all volunteer group that engages in actions to directly improve the quality of public information such as messaging, fighting disinformation, engaging in media accountability and in growing support for local journalism.

We see journalists and the media as accountable allies essential for an informed democracy, countering dangerous disinformation and propaganda and promoting the common good.

How to prepare:
Bring a friend - send them the link.
Activate your social networks.

title: Taller Mensual de MamásConPoder
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367863/1:7de02f42c7049dd06ac0881b38c7fe38f3620326fcb8cfaa019ed1fc1b81329d/

How to join:
Únete a la Reunión en este link: https://momsrising.zoom.us/j/84073901424

Te invitamos a participar en nuestro Taller de Salud Mental este miercoles 29 de Octubre a las 5:30 Hora del Pacifico, impartido por la **Dra. Sandra Martínez**, psicóloga y psicoterapeuta con más de 20 años de experiencia brindando apoyo emocional con un enfoque empático, profesional y humano. Taller de Salud Mental, 

**Este evento es gratuito y lo único que se necesita para entrar es tener una cuenta de Zoom. **

Este espacio está diseñado especialmente para la comunidad latina en EE. UU., con el propósito de ofrecer recursos prácticos y estrategias útiles para mejorar nuestra calidad de vida y afrontar con fortaleza estos tiempos de cambio e incertidumbre.

Durante el taller, hablaremos sobre temas esenciales como:

 - Bienestar emocional: la importancia de cuidar nuestra salud mental.

 - Manejo del estrés, miedo y ansiedad: herramientas y técnicas para recuperar el equilibrio.

 - Creación de espacios seguros: cómo cultivar la salud desde nosotros mismos.

 - Acompañamiento a nuestros hijos y comunidad: cómo, como madres y líderes, podemos apoyar, resistir y manejar el miedo.

 - Educación y apoyo en crisis: cómo acompañar de manera efectiva a quienes están pasando por momentos difíciles.

Además, contaremos con una sesión de preguntas y respuestas con la Dra. Sandra Martínez, donde podrás resolver tus dudas y compartir tus experiencias.

**Regístrate ahora para recibir notificaciones y recordatorios sobre el próximo encuentro.** 

¡Tu bienestar emocional también importa!

**NOTA IMPORTANTE: Tomamos muy en serio tu privacidad y toda tu información compartida por aquí o por cualquier otro medio con MamásConPoder está protegida y no se comparte con nadie más.**

HORA DEL EVENTO de acuerdo a tu zona horaria:

- 5:30 p.m. Hora del Pacifico
- 5:30 p.m. Hora de la Montaña
- 7:30 p.m. Hora del Centro
- 8:30 p.m. Hora del Este

Evento organizado por MamásConPoder para la comunidad.

------------

How to prepare:
Gracias por inscribirte para participar en nuestro evento el miércoles 29 de octubre a las 5:30 p. m. PT / 8:30 p. m. ET

En este Taller de Salud Mental  para padres de McP platicaremos sobre los temas que actualmente nos preocupan a la comunidad latina en EE. UU., con el propósito de ofrecer recursos prácticos y estrategias útiles para mejorar nuestra calidad de vida y afrontar con fortaleza estos tiempos de cambio e incertidumbre.

**Aquí puedes checar la HORA DEL EVENTO de acuerdo a tu zona horaria:**

- 5:30 p.m. Hora del Pacifico
- 5:30 p.m. Hora de la Montaña
- 7:30 p.m. Hora del Centro
- 8:30 p.m. Hora del Este


**ÚNETE A LA REUNIÓN DE ZOOM AQUÍ, el día del evento:**

title: Institutional Biosafety Committee Meetings
description:
The Institutional Biosafety Committee (IBC) invites the NC State community to attend our virtual meetings. The IBC provides assurance that the potential impacts associated with the intended use of biological organisms at NC State have been appropriately evaluated.

To receive the meeting link and additional meeting details, please contact Brenda L. Mickens at (919) 515-4190.

title: Ranger Workshop - Stream Ecology
description: Join us as we hike down to Otter Falls (1.2 miles round trip) to learn about how living and non-living things interact in mountain streams. What are all of those insects and other critters swimming and crawling through the creek? We will catch and identify what type of creature they are and what these species could tell us about water quality. Nets will be provided. Any participants wanting to get into the stream should bring clothes and shoes that can get wet and a towel to dry off. Make sure to bring water for the hike! No pets please. This is a Ranger Workshop with The Town of Seven Devils.

title: Greater Triangle Coalition Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48393601/1:ead48ebe1ab5c4be416d49c986ae26cff74c7676a3ebe1812c3c1ae79997cabd/

### Join Our Greater Triangle Regional Coalition Meeting

- 📅 **When**: 3rd Wednesday of every month, 6:00 – 7:00 PM
- 📍 **Where**: Virtual (Online)

Democracy NC’s Greater Triangle region covers **Wake, Harnett, and Johnston counties.**

**We come together to:**

- Build strong grassroots leadership
- Equip people to take action
- Serve “the good of the whole”

Whether you’re brand new or already engaged, this space is for learning, sharing, and organizing for **a movement built of, by, and for the people**, grounded in equity, solidarity, and justice.

**A note from your Greater Triangle Regional Managing Organizer:** 
I’m passionate about building community, amplifying voices, and creating spaces where people can be heard and find their role in supporting civic engagement and protecting our democracy. This work keeps me grounded, and I’m excited to invite you to be part of it.

**Join us, share your voice, and help shape the work ahead!**

How to prepare:
Thank you for signing up to attend our monthly coalition meeting!

Each month, you’ll get a reminder with details on what we’ll be discussing and the actions we can take together. We’re excited to stay connected and work alongside you to make an impact.

Hope to see you online!

— Sugelema Lynch

https://www.mobilize.us/democracync/event/896413/

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980087/1:79fff87a699e1c202fa09ae346f37ee344e988b521229958834d7b28a10dbae7/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

Event Zoom Link: https://us06web.zoom.us/j/84862510973

https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/425528/

title: Succession Planning Webinar
description:
Retirements, skills gaps, and turnover are hitting manufacturing and other industries harder than ever. Join NC State Industry Expansion Solutions for a webinar that will outline for employers how to map critical roles, capture institutional knowledge and build a pipeline of skilled workers. You'll learn practical tools and strategies to protect your operations and prepare your company’s next generation of leaders. You should attend the webinar if you are:

A manufacturing or other employer/ownerAn executiveA plant/training managerAn HR leader who needs to prepare for retirements, turnover and talent shortagesParticipants will learn to:

Identify and develop future leaders within your organizationRetain critical skills and knowledge before they walk out the doorCreate a succession plan that aligns with their business goals and growthAddress unique workforce challenges in manufacturing, including skills gaps and demographic shifts

title: Solar Astronomy
location: Haw River State Park - Iron Ore Belt Access
description: Drop in between 1-3pm at The Iron Ore Belt Access to join Greensboro Astronomy Club members as we observe our planetÕs nearest star using special solar viewing equipment. Please dress for hot, sunny weather! NOTE: Program will be cancelled in the case of cloudy skies/rainy weather. Sign up at the Register link to be emailed in case of cancellation.

title: Discover Tryon Palace: Outlander
location: Tryon Palace Historic Sites and Gardens
description:
Step into Tryon Palace and explore Diana Gabaldon’s award-winning Outlander series. Learn about the lives of the fictional Jamie and Claire Fraser, their family and friends, and interactions with historical figures and events in the tumultuous period of the American Revolution.

Tour covers storylines from Outlander books 4 – 8 and the Starz Outlander seasons 4 – 8. (For those not current on the books or show, there will be spoilers for these books and seasons.)

Tour begins at 301 George Street, across from the Tryon Palace Main Gates. Ticket holders should check in at the Waystation ticketing desk no later than 3:30 p.m.

title: Alexander County Independence Celebration
description: Mark your calendar for the Alexander County Independence Celebration on Saturday, July 18, at 7:00 p.m. with music and fireworks! The Extraordinaires will perform on the Rotary Performance Stage at Alexander County Courthouse Park. Fireworks will follow at approximately 9:30 p.m. at Alexander Central High School. Funded in part by an America 250 NC grant, a program of the NC Department of Natural & Cultural Resources.

title: Dr. M.T. Pope House Presents: Evelyn's Book Club
description:
This program honors Evelyn Pope by reading books. 

Evelyn, a college librarian born in 1908 and died in 1995, loved a variety of books from The Adventures of Tom Sawyer to Shakespeare and James Baldwin. The program brings community members together monthly at the museum to discuss books, focusing on works by Black writers or featuring Black subjects, including fiction and non-fiction. Discussions will use questions to facilitate conversation.

"Mama Day" by Gloria Naylor
Wednesday, Feb. 25
 "The Black Angels: The Untold Story of the Nurses Who Helped Cure Tuberculosis" by Maria Smilios
Wednesday, April 22
 "A Visitation of Spirits" by Randall Kenan
Wednesday, June 24
 "Becoming Lunsford Lane: The Lives of an American Aeneas" by Craig Friend
Wednesday, Aug. 26
 "Ring Shout" by P. Dejeli Clark
Thursday, Oct. 29Registration opens Jan. 23.  An optional book bundle will be available to purchase at registration. Books can be picked up at the Dr. M.T. Pope House Museum.

title: Reunión Mensual Comunitaria de MomsRising
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367849/1:8f61657aaa44c0c069806a05309da11f52329651b1911a99d9373ec0aa4e9ce2/

REUNIÓN DEL 29 DE ENERO: 

**¿“Mamás unidas y poderosas”? ¡El verdadero poder de las madres en 2026 (¡Tú!). Únete a MomsRising para una reunión comunitaria por Zoom el jueves 29 de enero a las 8:30 p. m. ET / 5:30 p. m. PT.**


Únete a MomsRising para nuestra primera reunión comunitaria de este 2026. Líderes de nuestro movimiento compartirán actualizaciones sobre temas clave como Medicaid e inmigración, así como vías específicas y estratégicas para alzar nuestra voz y tomar acción.  

¡Porque este año hay que estar más presentes que nunca! Acompáñanos para conectar, inspirarnos y planificar la estrategia que necesitamos para 2026.

Por favor, invita a todas las personas que conozcas a unirse a la reunión, incluyendo a quienes estén listas para actuar, construir poder para los tiempos que se avecinan y defender nuestra educación, atención médica, asistencia nutricional y mucho más.

Juntas, podemos crear un futuro más brillante y esperanzador. ¿Te unes a nosotras?

*****Este evento se llevará a cabo en inglés, con traducción simultánea disponible en español.

How to prepare:
Gracias por registrarte en nuestra próxima reunión comunitaria de MomsRising.

Para unirte a la llamada debes tener una cuenta de Zoom.

Nos reunimos el cuarto jueves de cada mes por Zoom a las 5:30 pm PT / 8:30 pm ET para hablar de temas que son importantes para todas las mamás del país. Los eventos son en inglés, con traducción simultánea al español.

title: Getting Colonial Clean in the Historical Park
description: How did colonists prepare their households for the seasons? What was bathing and hygiene like? How did soldiers clean their wool uniforms? How were plants and herbs used to freshen things up? Join our costumed interpreters as they demonstrate practices of cleanliness in the 18th and 19th centuries. FREE. All ages welcome. Drop-in.

title: Small Arms Demonstration 10:30am & 2:30pm
description:

This will include a blank firing demonstration of the weapon.
Meet in the fort parade ground.

 

Photo by Jairus Hill : Living historian demonstrating a musket at Fort Macon State Park.

title: DEEP DIVE INTO HISTORY The Root of the Problem: 18th Century Dentistry
location: North Carolina Maritime Museum at Southport
description:
Join us as we delve into the enlightenment age of dentistry!

Without its 18th century foundations, dental chairs, porcelain teeth, modern dentures, and specific tools for dentists, oral care would be much worse! After this presentation, you’ll want to give your dentist and dental assistants a “plaque.”

Find out the Tooth, the Whole Tooth, and nothing by the Tooth on how our founding fathers took care of their oral hygiene and why we should be happy to have the dentists of today.

This event is part of the museum’s Deep Dive Into History series, programs designed to give visitors a deeper understanding of our shared past through costumed interpretation and hands-on learning. Each Deep Dive delves into a specific theme in Lower Cape Fear history. This event is FREE, but donations are always appreciated.

title: The Tory War
description: Have you heard of the Tory War? From 1780-81, Cumberland County and the surrounding region fell into a civil war between Tories and Patriots during the American Revolution. Learn the stories of courage and horror that took place in this formative period in the region's history!

title: Voter ID Assistance Orientation - Help voters get the ID the need to cast a ballot that counts!
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47685203/1:1c536331aed8e630e5410c3ef53e175d8f823fa38ee1d352b639115c9caefad5/

## VoteRiders is seeking dedicated virtual and in-person volunteers to provide free voter ID assistance to eligible voters in key communities where VoteRiders has staff on the ground including *Houston (TX), Columbus (GA), Pittsburgh (PA), Orlando (FL) and Charlotte (NC), , Milwaukee (WI), and Atlanta (GA)*.

This unique opportunity will make an immediate impact and directly help folks secure the ID they need to cast a ballot that counts. 

Are you detail-oriented, reliable, computer savvy, and passionate about people? We need you for this ongoing opportunity!

### How you can help: 

Provide virtual and, if convenient, in-person ID help to people who have requested assistance, through our partners or self-referral. Work one-on-one with voters on your schedule!  (Requirement:  ~3 hours per week).

** Volunteers will need to attend at least one buddy training after this call.  Attend this orientation to receive sign-up information for buddy training.

title: Goldston America 250 Film Series
description:
Join the Chatham Community Library for this Goldston America 250 Film Series, hosted every fourth Tuesday, January-December 2026, 4pm-6pm. America 250’s mission is to honor and commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, marking America’s Semiquincentennial. The Goldston Library will screen the following documentaries and films:

Tue, Jan 27, 4pm-6pm The Congress, Ken Burns.
Tue, Feb 24, 4pm-6pm Becoming Frederick Douglass, Stanley Nelson and Nicole London.
Tue, Mar 24, 4pm-6pm Rachel Carson: The Woman Who Launched the Modern Environmental Movement, Michelle Ferrari.
Tue, Apr 28, 4pm-6pm Thomas Jefferson: A View from the Mountain, Martin Doblmeier.
Tue, May 26, 4pm-6pm The Address, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jun 23, 4pm-6pm The Brooklyn Bridge, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jul 28, 4pm-6pm The Statue of Liberty, Ken Burns.
Tue, Aug 25, 4pm-6pm American Coup: Wilmington 1898, Yoruba Richen and Brad Lichtenstein.
Tue, Sep 22, 4pm-6pm How it Feels to be Free, Yoruba Richen.
Tue, Oct 27, 4pm-6pm 80 Years Later, Celine Parreñas Shimizu.
Tue, Nov 24, 4pm-6pm Gather, Sanjay Rawal.
Tue, Dec 22, 4pm-6pm Women of World War II: The Untold Stories, Eli Brown.

This FREE, family-friendly event is open to the public. No registration is required. The event will take place indoors in the Goldston Library. The Goldston Library is ADA-compliant, including wheelchair-accessible parking, entrances, seating, common spaces, walkways, and restrooms. Free parking is available outside the library. Pets are not allowed - please leave your furry friends at home.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events.

title: Fight Back With Friends! Monthly Call To Action
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980012/1:2cc061f45ae60e05b2eb018ababcf87509f45221226712bb86184a3ab22ff415/

Fight Back With Friends! is a national program designed to help you activate your community! Using the Empower app, the powerful relational organizing app, we’ll equip you with the tools to connect with friends, family, and neighbors who need YOUR voice to get civically engaged. Join us on the fourth Tuesday of every month for a one-hour training session, where we’ll onboard new participants and introduce fresh calls to action that make a real impact.

How to prepare:
Use this link to download the Empower app and access the call to action: https://empowerthefuture.us/Indivisible. Then, use the invite code Indivisible. 

Before joining the meeting, be sure to have your cell phone and laptop or computer ready for the training section of the call if you would like to practice as you follow along.

https://www.mobilize.us/indivisible/event/765804/

title: Dune Ranger Discovery Camp
description:
Calling all future Dune Rangers!  Join Ranger Austin and friends for a fun, educational, and adventurous three-day camp.  Explore maritime forests, paddle on the Roanoke Sound, climb the tallest active dune on the east coast, be creative with cool arts and crafts projects, play goofy games, help with stewardship projects, and make new friends.  At the end of the week, you will be an official Dune Ranger!  Our camp is supported by Friends of Jockey’s Ridge.  

July 29 - July 31, 2026       9:00am to 12:00pm daily

Open to grades 3-6.

Pre-registration is required.  Please email austin.paul@ncparks.gov to signup.  

The mission of the Dune Ranger Discovery Camp will focus on the following elements.

Provide educational opportunities for our youth (both local and visitors),

to promote stewardship and an appreciation for our natural areas,

to provide a safe place for our youth to interact with others and create friendships,

to promote an active lifestyle,

allow for creativity,

and to have fun.

title: 23rd Annual Qualitative Research Summer Intensisve
description: QRSI 2026 (Qualitative Research Summer Intensive) from ResearchTalk is a premier, week-long training program for researchers, co-hosted with UNC's Odum Institute, offering intensive, expert-led courses in qualitative research methods, theory, and data analysis, with options for foundational to advanced topics, including using AI, grounded theory, and developing practical skills for various disciplines.

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980089/1:96d92105c919fc1d9a11d2f3c54bde5de2e56779076bc889c7beda79ab2f89b2/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

Event Zoom Link: https://us06web.zoom.us/j/84862510973

https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/425528/

title: National Ranger Day, Touch-a-Truck!
description:
Come to Lake Norman's Visitor Center to see what our trucks look like! People will have a chance to step inside a Ranger's truck and see all the hats that Rangers wear on a day to day basis and we keep in our truck to protect our visitors and park. 

This event is to help celebrate World Ranger Day. 

 

Photo by J. Davidson: A park ranger's flat hat with a scenic sunrise fall view at Morrow Mountain State Park

title: Ranger Walk & Talk
description:
In honor of World Ranger Day, join a ranger at Boone Fork Parking Area (milepost 299.9 on the Blue Ridge Parkway) for a short stroll along the Tanawha Trail. Learn what rangers do on a day-to-day basis, how we got our job, and feel free to ask us questions! This short, 1/4 mile easy hike ends at a beautiful bridge over the Boone Fork Creek. Feel free to continue your hike onto Grandfather Mountain State Park trails from here, or turn around and walk back with the Ranger. 

Please wear appropriate hiking shoes and dress for the weather.

There are no bathrooms at Boone Fork Parking Area.

title: Independence Comes to NC!
description: Did you know that North Carolina’s first public reading of the Declaration of Independence took place in Halifax on August 1st, 1776?  Join historical interpreters as they reenact events surrounding the joyous news of American independence from Britain. At 12:00pm, see a reenactor portray Cornelius Harnett, President of the Council of Safety, read the Declaration for the first time in North Carolina. View a screening of The American Constitution, a new documentary film on the writing of the United States Constitution, which was partially filmed at Historic Halifax. Free.

description:

title: Downtown Techies Durham and Raleigh MERGE FOR THE SUMMER!
description:
Downtown Techies: Durham
Hey Techies!

We are so excited to see you all again! Heads up! for the summer months we are merging all of our events into an event once a month on the first Tuesday of every month!

Don't forget to RSVP for first round free!!

We will be resuming Downtown Durham events in September on the third Tuesday of every month. We would love your suggestions on venues! Just ping us.

Questions or suggestions?

Our digital door is always open at www.downtowntechies.com or @downtowntechies. Check out our events in Raleigh as well!

Meet, Drink and be TECHIE!

-Jake and Blake

Looking to get more connected to the local tech scene, opportunity or talent? Email us info@downtowntechies.com and we will make sure you find what you need!

title: Black Voters Matter Texting Tuesdays
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48337770/1:ae02fe63a75f07295543c3df0fd63a31c2906064518809cae21762f8ac958ce9/

How to join:
You will need at least a basic Zoom account to register.

Join us on Zoom at 6pm ET/5pm CT every week as we gather to drive change through digital organizing. Our texting campaigns will give you the opportunity to engage residents across the U.S. while empowering them to make a positive impact in their communities. Last year, we sent over 19 million texts educating residents on local elections, environmental justice, police reform, health care, and so much more! During the event we will provide training, discuss our latest texting campaign, and we will take action.

A basic Zoom account is required to register.

How to prepare:
Complete your registration here via Zoom: https://bit.ly/BVMTextingTuesday.   

You will need a basic Zoom account to register. A basic Zoom account is free. 

Fill out the volunteer agreement here - https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=4XYntqm7yEqm7h6bNND82BTiriDtw_dLvWG_BUODEPBUMkgyQ1ZQTENCVVBWNTVNQ0pDWVZaNFFLRC4u

title: Public School Strong Organizing Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46979988/1:833acd21a40456f9de6bb7232f1e41786e50992e3478705d121fc85aa0ebd110/

How to join:
Hi there, 

Thanks for signing up for our Public School Strong Orientation & Organizing Meeting!  

Be sure to register on zoom in advance for this meeting:
https://raceforward-org.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIvcuiurz4pE9BGsfJi-mBBX_lhl2bPG7Ti 

After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting.

**We are thrilled to welcome David Backer, author of As Public As Possible: Radical Finance for America’s Public Schools, as our guest speaker at January’s meeting.** 

Join us for the next Public Schools Strong organizing meeting on Thursday, January 22nd, where parents, family members, educators, and supporters from across the nation gather each month to gain practical tools to promote, protect, and strengthen our public schools. 

*If you have already attended a Public School Strong Orientation, you'll join a breakout room with current members for connection, reflection and training. If you're new, we've got you covered too!*

**Do you want honest, equitable and fully-funded public schools?** Acting locally is key.

### Join public school families, educators and supporters from across the country to learn how to:
1. Form a Public School Strong team in your school district
2. Engage your community to get school boards and district leaders to speak up for equitable and fully funded public schools
3. Connect your local advocacy to others for state and national impact

For more info, please visit publicschoolstrong.org

### Current Public School Strong Members

There is strength in staying connected. After completing Public School Strong (PSS) Orientation, this is your consistent space to reconnect with your peers across the country to:

1. Access helpful tools and practice relevant skills,
2. Connect the dots between what is happening locally and nationally,
3. Learn from mistakes and progress made along the way

Bring a friend!

**Self Directed Orientation Now Available!**

If these live training times don't work for you, you can complete your orientation at your own pace:

1) Create Account in NEA Portal (open to non-NEA members too): 

neapartnera.learnupon.com/users/sign_up

2) Select the PSS orientation by clicking on this link:

bit.ly/SelfDirectedPSS

How to prepare:
### Hi there! Thanks for signing up for a 'Public School Strong Organizing Meeting!' (for new folks we'll also have a new member orientation)

This event is virtual, will provide updates on the federal attacks on our public schools and the immigrant community, actions you can take right now and the tools to support you in doing son. 

For new folks, we'll provide an introduction to our Public School Strong campaign. Check out our organizing platform using this link to find or create a local team: 

- publicschoolstrong.org

More on this powerful tool at your Orientation...

https://www.mobilize.us/raceforward/event/596321/

title: Ranger Workshop - Backyard Birding
description: Join us at The Park at Seven Devils for some backyard birding! Birding is an activity that can be done by anyone at any age. Most birders start out by identifying common birds found at their backyard bird feeders. During this workshop you will learn how to point out key characteristics, such as size, coloration, and activity, that can help you identify common backyard bird species, specifically species that visit bird feeders. By using applications like Audubon and Merlin, along with identification guides, you will be able to use those key characteristics to help you identify common species found here in the High Country. Binoculars are not required, but highly recommended, and there will be a quick lesson on how to properly use binoculars. This is a Ranger Workshop with The Town of Seven Devils.

title: Discover Tryon Palace: Outlander
location: Tryon Palace Historic Sites and Gardens
description:
Step into Tryon Palace and explore Diana Gabaldon’s award-winning Outlander series. Learn about the lives of the fictional Jamie and Claire Fraser, their family and friends, and interactions with historical figures and events in the tumultuous period of the American Revolution.

Tour covers storylines from Outlander books 4 – 8 and the Starz Outlander seasons 4 – 8. (For those not current on the books or show, there will be spoilers for these books and seasons.)

Tour begins at 301 George Street, across from the Tryon Palace Main Gates. Ticket holders should check in at the Waystation ticketing desk no later than 3:30 p.m.

Join the Media and Democracy Project to better understand and respond to the factors that influence public opinion, voters and democracy. Each session we will activate on a current information-related subject to help Fix Media Now.

We develop and distribute skills to navigate the Information War on truth and democracy.

We are a non-partisan all volunteer group that engages in actions to directly improve the quality of public information such as messaging, fighting disinformation, engaging in media accountability and in growing support for local journalism.

We see journalists and the media as accountable allies essential for an informed democracy, countering dangerous disinformation and propaganda and promoting the common good.

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980092/1:0500a30227b84d357b1b8291196dad49a750566256adc38570bec4b5933c4959/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Optimizing Operations: Benefits and Types of Work Measurement
description:
Join NC State Industry Expansion Solutions for a webinar that will explain how to become an expert in work measurements and the latest lean and continuous improvement tools. Work measurement is a foundational practice for improving efficiency and productivity. By systematically analyzing the time and effort required to perform tasks, organizations can achieve a variety of benefits:

Improved planning and scheduling: Accurate time standards allow for better resource allocation, project timelines and production scheduling.Enhanced cost control: By knowing the time it takes to produce a product or deliver a service, businesses can more accurately estimate costs, set prices and manage budgets.Performance evaluation: Work measurement provides a fair and objective basis for evaluating employee or team performance, setting realistic goals and identifying training needs.Process Improvement: By identifying and eliminating non-value-added time, organizations can streamline workflows, reduce waste and increase overall productivity.Informed decision-making: Data from work measurement provides a solid foundation for capital investment decisions, process redesign and outsourcing analyses.Who Should Attend the Webinar?

Anyone responsible for ensuring efficient, consistent plant operationsProduction and operations engineersPlant managersShop supervisorsForemenTechniciansLine workersLean and continuous improvement practitioners

description:

## VoteRiders is seeking dedicated virtual and in-person volunteers to provide free voter ID assistance to eligible voters in key communities where VoteRiders has staff on the ground including *Houston (TX), Columbus (GA), Pittsburgh (PA), Orlando (FL) and Charlotte (NC), , Milwaukee (WI), and Atlanta (GA)*.

This unique opportunity will make an immediate impact and directly help folks secure the ID they need to cast a ballot that counts. 

Are you detail-oriented, reliable, computer savvy, and passionate about people? We need you for this ongoing opportunity!

### How you can help: 

Provide virtual and, if convenient, in-person ID help to people who have requested assistance, through our partners or self-referral. Work one-on-one with voters on your schedule!  (Requirement:  ~3 hours per week).

** Volunteers will need to attend at least one buddy training after this call.  Attend this orientation to receive sign-up information for buddy training.

title: Goldston America 250 Film Series
description:
Join the Chatham Community Library for this Goldston America 250 Film Series, hosted every fourth Tuesday, January-December 2026, 4pm-6pm. America 250’s mission is to honor and commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, marking America’s Semiquincentennial. The Goldston Library will screen the following documentaries and films:

Tue, Jan 27, 4pm-6pm The Congress, Ken Burns.
Tue, Feb 24, 4pm-6pm Becoming Frederick Douglass, Stanley Nelson and Nicole London.
Tue, Mar 24, 4pm-6pm Rachel Carson: The Woman Who Launched the Modern Environmental Movement, Michelle Ferrari.
Tue, Apr 28, 4pm-6pm Thomas Jefferson: A View from the Mountain, Martin Doblmeier.
Tue, May 26, 4pm-6pm The Address, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jun 23, 4pm-6pm The Brooklyn Bridge, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jul 28, 4pm-6pm The Statue of Liberty, Ken Burns.
Tue, Aug 25, 4pm-6pm American Coup: Wilmington 1898, Yoruba Richen and Brad Lichtenstein.
Tue, Sep 22, 4pm-6pm How it Feels to be Free, Yoruba Richen.
Tue, Oct 27, 4pm-6pm 80 Years Later, Celine Parreñas Shimizu.
Tue, Nov 24, 4pm-6pm Gather, Sanjay Rawal.
Tue, Dec 22, 4pm-6pm Women of World War II: The Untold Stories, Eli Brown.

This FREE, family-friendly event is open to the public. No registration is required. The event will take place indoors in the Goldston Library. The Goldston Library is ADA-compliant, including wheelchair-accessible parking, entrances, seating, common spaces, walkways, and restrooms. Free parking is available outside the library. Pets are not allowed - please leave your furry friends at home.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events.

title: Dr. M.T. Pope House Presents: Evelyn's Book Club
description:
This program honors Evelyn Pope by reading books. 

Evelyn, a college librarian born in 1908 and died in 1995, loved a variety of books from The Adventures of Tom Sawyer to Shakespeare and James Baldwin. The program brings community members together monthly at the museum to discuss books, focusing on works by Black writers or featuring Black subjects, including fiction and non-fiction. Discussions will use questions to facilitate conversation.

"Mama Day" by Gloria Naylor
Wednesday, Feb. 25
 "The Black Angels: The Untold Story of the Nurses Who Helped Cure Tuberculosis" by Maria Smilios
Wednesday, April 22
 "A Visitation of Spirits" by Randall Kenan
Wednesday, June 24
 "Becoming Lunsford Lane: The Lives of an American Aeneas" by Craig Friend
Wednesday, Aug. 26
 "Ring Shout" by P. Dejeli Clark
Thursday, Oct. 29Registration opens Jan. 23.  An optional book bundle will be available to purchase at registration. Books can be picked up at the Dr. M.T. Pope House Museum.

title: Anniversary of the New Bern Resolves
description: *Save the Date* More Details Coming Soon! The New Bern Resolves constitute a significant chapter in the history of the American Revolution, reflecting the growing discontent of the colonies with British rule and their bold steps toward self-governance. Adopted on August 27, 1774, by 71 elected NC delegates to the First Provincial Congress in America, the New Bern Resolves represented an early, decisive expression of resistance to British policies and "The Intolerable Acts" (Boston Port/Trade Act, Massachusetts Government Act, Administration of Justice Act, and Quartering Acts) by calling for economic sanctions against Great Britain and its trading partners. As one of the earliest documented calls for solidarity among the colonies, the New Bern Resolves laid the groundwork for broader revolutionary movements.

description:

REUNIÓN DEL 29 DE ENERO: 

**¿“Mamás unidas y poderosas”? ¡El verdadero poder de las madres en 2026 (¡Tú!). Únete a MomsRising para una reunión comunitaria por Zoom el jueves 29 de enero a las 8:30 p. m. ET / 5:30 p. m. PT.**


Únete a MomsRising para nuestra primera reunión comunitaria de este 2026. Líderes de nuestro movimiento compartirán actualizaciones sobre temas clave como Medicaid e inmigración, así como vías específicas y estratégicas para alzar nuestra voz y tomar acción.  

¡Porque este año hay que estar más presentes que nunca! Acompáñanos para conectar, inspirarnos y planificar la estrategia que necesitamos para 2026.

Por favor, invita a todas las personas que conozcas a unirse a la reunión, incluyendo a quienes estén listas para actuar, construir poder para los tiempos que se avecinan y defender nuestra educación, atención médica, asistencia nutricional y mucho más.

Juntas, podemos crear un futuro más brillante y esperanzador. ¿Te unes a nosotras?

*****Este evento se llevará a cabo en inglés, con traducción simultánea disponible en español.

How to prepare:
Gracias por registrarte en nuestra próxima reunión comunitaria de MomsRising.

Para unirte a la llamada debes tener una cuenta de Zoom.

Nos reunimos el cuarto jueves de cada mes por Zoom a las 5:30 pm PT / 8:30 pm ET para hablar de temas que son importantes para todas las mamás del país. Los eventos son en inglés, con traducción simultánea al español.

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980094/1:fa28fd4133ecf7ff637fa3d466aafa83a67e9fa6aa99a998319ffbb9f126902b/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

Event Zoom Link: https://us06web.zoom.us/j/84862510973

title: Small Arms Demonstration 10:30am & 2:30pm
description:

Meet in the fort parade ground.
