title: Public School Strong Organizing Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46979989/1:53a86ad61423ba7e6dd10e758f62a80b7ca3441a7b53aa25fa82a038a6ddd5ab/

How to join:
Hi there, 

Thanks for signing up for our Public School Strong Orientation & Organizing Meeting!  

Be sure to register on zoom in advance for this meeting:
https://raceforward-org.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIvcuiurz4pE9BGsfJi-mBBX_lhl2bPG7Ti 

After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting.

**We are thrilled to welcome David Backer, author of As Public As Possible: Radical Finance for America’s Public Schools, as our guest speaker at January’s meeting.** 

Join us for the next Public Schools Strong organizing meeting on Thursday, January 22nd, where parents, family members, educators, and supporters from across the nation gather each month to gain practical tools to promote, protect, and strengthen our public schools. 

*If you have already attended a Public School Strong Orientation, you'll join a breakout room with current members for connection, reflection and training. If you're new, we've got you covered too!*

**Do you want honest, equitable and fully-funded public schools?** Acting locally is key.

### Join public school families, educators and supporters from across the country to learn how to:
1. Form a Public School Strong team in your school district
2. Engage your community to get school boards and district leaders to speak up for equitable and fully funded public schools
3. Connect your local advocacy to others for state and national impact

For more info, please visit publicschoolstrong.org

### Current Public School Strong Members

There is strength in staying connected. After completing Public School Strong (PSS) Orientation, this is your consistent space to reconnect with your peers across the country to:

1. Access helpful tools and practice relevant skills,
2. Connect the dots between what is happening locally and nationally,
3. Learn from mistakes and progress made along the way

Bring a friend!

**Self Directed Orientation Now Available!**

If these live training times don't work for you, you can complete your orientation at your own pace:

1) Create Account in NEA Portal (open to non-NEA members too): 

neapartnera.learnupon.com/users/sign_up

2) Select the PSS orientation by clicking on this link:

bit.ly/SelfDirectedPSS

How to prepare:
### Hi there! Thanks for signing up for a 'Public School Strong Organizing Meeting!' (for new folks we'll also have a new member orientation)

This event is virtual, will provide updates on the federal attacks on our public schools and the immigrant community, actions you can take right now and the tools to support you in doing son. 

For new folks, we'll provide an introduction to our Public School Strong campaign. Check out our organizing platform using this link to find or create a local team: 

- publicschoolstrong.org

More on this powerful tool at your Orientation...

https://www.mobilize.us/raceforward/event/596321/

title: Temple Theatre Presents: "1776: An American Musical"
description:
Temple Theatre is one of North Carolina’s premier regional theatres, offering Broadway-quality productions in the heart of downtown Sanford. In September, Temple Theatre will present "1776: An American Musical," a stirring musical set in Philadelphia in 1776. 

Tickets range from $25 to $40 with discounts available for active military, Lee County educators and students, and groups of 10 or more. Visit templeshows.org or call 919-774-4512 for more information.

title: Education in the Historical Park
description: Getting an education is more than “book learning” in school. How to build, craft, and care for things to teach others is a form of education too. Come see our costumed interpreters demonstrate some of the educational activities of the 18th and 19th centuries. FREE. All ages welcome. Drop-in.

title: Our Maritime Heritage. Living History at Bath –
description: Celebrate the establishment of the Colonial Navy by the Continental Congress on September 13, 1775, with demonstrations of all things naval: ropemaking, signal flags, maps, naval stores, spinning and weaving, blacksmithing. Learn about Bath and its role in the naval life of the colony. Munitions demonstrations scheduled throughout the day.

title: Girl Scouts Love State Parks!
description: It's year of America's 250th birthday and we are calling all Girl Scouts to celebrate! This year we will be going over different historical games that were popular 250 years ago. Girls will have a chance to play different games and friendly competitions will be had to see who has the best historical games skills!

title: Deep Sky Astronomy
location: Haw River State Park - Iron Ore Belt Accesss
description:
This event is free to attend, but registration is requested. 

Join local astronomers with the Greensboro Astronomy Club as we observe the night skies over the Iron Ore Belt Access. Attendees may expect to see closer-than-typical views of the moon, planets, star clusters, and other celestial objects subject to change with time of year and local sky darkness.

Telescopes and viewing equipment will be set up on site but you may wish to bring your own binoculars or telescope, plus a chair or blanket for seating. Dress for the weather. Flashlights & headlamps should have a red-light option or filter to protect your night vision.

Skywatching sessions may be cancelled at the last minute for weather. Please register at the Register link so you can be emailed in the event of cancellation.

Program equipment and instruction provided by members of the Greensboro Astronomy Club, which meet the third Friday of each month at the Greensboro Science Center. Learn more about their organization here: 

https://greensboroastronomyclub.org/

Star-gazing Etiquette:

Please use a red light at the observing session and keep any light pointed at the ground. As your eyes adapt, a light is not needed at all.Pease do not touch any telescopes or other equipment unless permission is given and form a orderly line behind each telescope. Please do not allow children or pets to run around the observing area. Pets must be on a leash no longer than 6 feet at all times. This is to protect others' safety and the equipment.Stock photo © Pexels / Lucas Pezeta

title: The Media and Democracy Project - What activists can do: Fix Media Now
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47485923/1:f37ea0b0e2e22df73988f13d8c5d62d9c3a3f14159ad4eb2d3bdff38968cc26b/

Join the Media and Democracy Project to better understand and respond to the factors that influence public opinion, voters and democracy. Each session we will activate on a current information-related subject to help Fix Media Now. 

We develop and distribute skills to navigate the Information War on truth and democracy. 

We are a non-partisan all volunteer group that engages in actions to directly improve the quality of public information such as messaging, fighting disinformation, engaging in media accountability and in growing support for local journalism. 

We see journalists and the media as accountable allies essential for an informed democracy, countering dangerous disinformation and propaganda and promoting the common good.

How to prepare:
Bring a friend - send them the link. 
Activate your social networks.

https://www.mobilize.us/mediademocracyproject/event/430177/

title: Greater Triangle Coalition Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48393602/1:446be1031347adc938c44f813c5c5fe917a590a06d5a76ca73f839d1c5dbd3f0/

### Join Our Greater Triangle Regional Coalition Meeting

- 📅 **When**: 3rd Wednesday of every month, 6:00 – 7:00 PM
- 📍 **Where**: Virtual (Online)

Democracy NC’s Greater Triangle region covers **Wake, Harnett, and Johnston counties.**

**We come together to:**

- Build strong grassroots leadership
- Equip people to take action
- Serve “the good of the whole”

Whether you’re brand new or already engaged, this space is for learning, sharing, and organizing for **a movement built of, by, and for the people**, grounded in equity, solidarity, and justice.

**A note from your Greater Triangle Regional Managing Organizer:** 
I’m passionate about building community, amplifying voices, and creating spaces where people can be heard and find their role in supporting civic engagement and protecting our democracy. This work keeps me grounded, and I’m excited to invite you to be part of it.

**Join us, share your voice, and help shape the work ahead!**

How to prepare:
Thank you for signing up to attend our monthly coalition meeting!

Each month, you’ll get a reminder with details on what we’ll be discussing and the actions we can take together. We’re excited to stay connected and work alongside you to make an impact.

Hope to see you online!

— Sugelema Lynch

https://www.mobilize.us/democracync/event/896413/

You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Luminary & Freedom Stories
description:
Harriet Ann Daves recalled just how monumental Civil War surrenders were for her family: "When the surrender came my grandfather came to my mother and told her: 'Well, you are as free as I am.'"

Claiming freedom was not so straightforward for the 331,059 enslaved women, children, and men during the Civil War. Some took enormous risk to reach Union lines before the federal government made any promise of abolishing slavery. Others left their homes and plantations to follow Sherman's army as they marched through the southeast. Some believed home was where they already lived and worked and waited patiently for freedom to extend to them. Others still were lied to by their enslavers or moved away from the advancing U.S. Army so they would remain enslaved. Freedom came in different ways and at different times to enslaved people across the state.

One common thread unites these freedom stories: the defeat of the Confederate military ensured a new birth of freedom across the state and nation.

During this special luminary program, Bennett Place invites you to reflect on the end of the Civil War as a momentous step towards freedom for all Americans and the end of chattel slavery in the United States. Walk among 3,310 luminaries - one for every 1,000 enslaved people in North Carolina - and reflect on experiences of enslavement and freedom. Historians dot the path and share stories of people's pathways to freedom and life after slavery & the Civil War.

title: Voter ID Assistance Orientation - Help voters get the ID the need to cast a ballot that counts!
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47685205/1:90455f218148ac8f948b86db8f9440ddac291322dff174423604326afe146861/

## VoteRiders is seeking dedicated virtual and in-person volunteers to provide free voter ID assistance to eligible voters in key communities where VoteRiders has staff on the ground including *Houston (TX), Columbus (GA), Pittsburgh (PA), Orlando (FL) and Charlotte (NC), , Milwaukee (WI), and Atlanta (GA)*.

This unique opportunity will make an immediate impact and directly help folks secure the ID they need to cast a ballot that counts. 

Are you detail-oriented, reliable, computer savvy, and passionate about people? We need you for this ongoing opportunity!

### How you can help: 

Provide virtual and, if convenient, in-person ID help to people who have requested assistance, through our partners or self-referral. Work one-on-one with voters on your schedule!  (Requirement:  ~3 hours per week).

** Volunteers will need to attend at least one buddy training after this call.  Attend this orientation to receive sign-up information for buddy training.

title: Goldston America 250 Film Series
description:
Join the Chatham Community Library for this Goldston America 250 Film Series, hosted every fourth Tuesday, January-December 2026, 4pm-6pm. America 250’s mission is to honor and commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, marking America’s Semiquincentennial. The Goldston Library will screen the following documentaries and films:

Tue, Jan 27, 4pm-6pm The Congress, Ken Burns.
Tue, Feb 24, 4pm-6pm Becoming Frederick Douglass, Stanley Nelson and Nicole London.
Tue, Mar 24, 4pm-6pm Rachel Carson: The Woman Who Launched the Modern Environmental Movement, Michelle Ferrari.
Tue, Apr 28, 4pm-6pm Thomas Jefferson: A View from the Mountain, Martin Doblmeier.
Tue, May 26, 4pm-6pm The Address, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jun 23, 4pm-6pm The Brooklyn Bridge, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jul 28, 4pm-6pm The Statue of Liberty, Ken Burns.
Tue, Aug 25, 4pm-6pm American Coup: Wilmington 1898, Yoruba Richen and Brad Lichtenstein.
Tue, Sep 22, 4pm-6pm How it Feels to be Free, Yoruba Richen.
Tue, Oct 27, 4pm-6pm 80 Years Later, Celine Parreñas Shimizu.
Tue, Nov 24, 4pm-6pm Gather, Sanjay Rawal.
Tue, Dec 22, 4pm-6pm Women of World War II: The Untold Stories, Eli Brown.

This FREE, family-friendly event is open to the public. No registration is required. The event will take place indoors in the Goldston Library. The Goldston Library is ADA-compliant, including wheelchair-accessible parking, entrances, seating, common spaces, walkways, and restrooms. Free parking is available outside the library. Pets are not allowed - please leave your furry friends at home.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events.

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

title: Dr. M.T. Pope House Presents: Evelyn's Book Club
description:
This program honors Evelyn Pope by reading books. 

Evelyn, a college librarian born in 1908 and died in 1995, loved a variety of books from The Adventures of Tom Sawyer to Shakespeare and James Baldwin. The program brings community members together monthly at the museum to discuss books, focusing on works by Black writers or featuring Black subjects, including fiction and non-fiction. Discussions will use questions to facilitate conversation.

"Mama Day" by Gloria Naylor
Wednesday, Feb. 25
 "The Black Angels: The Untold Story of the Nurses Who Helped Cure Tuberculosis" by Maria Smilios
Wednesday, April 22
 "A Visitation of Spirits" by Randall Kenan
Wednesday, June 24
 "Becoming Lunsford Lane: The Lives of an American Aeneas" by Craig Friend
Wednesday, Aug. 26
 "Ring Shout" by P. Dejeli Clark
Thursday, Oct. 29Registration opens Jan. 23.  An optional book bundle will be available to purchase at registration. Books can be picked up at the Dr. M.T. Pope House Museum.

title: Reunión Mensual Comunitaria de MomsRising
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367851/1:2c53a48828c01209ee22a45f797820983ee625700dfe101eb0225945d2f9658e/

REUNIÓN DEL 29 DE ENERO: 

**¿“Mamás unidas y poderosas”? ¡El verdadero poder de las madres en 2026 (¡Tú!). Únete a MomsRising para una reunión comunitaria por Zoom el jueves 29 de enero a las 8:30 p. m. ET / 5:30 p. m. PT.**


Únete a MomsRising para nuestra primera reunión comunitaria de este 2026. Líderes de nuestro movimiento compartirán actualizaciones sobre temas clave como Medicaid e inmigración, así como vías específicas y estratégicas para alzar nuestra voz y tomar acción.  

¡Porque este año hay que estar más presentes que nunca! Acompáñanos para conectar, inspirarnos y planificar la estrategia que necesitamos para 2026.

Por favor, invita a todas las personas que conozcas a unirse a la reunión, incluyendo a quienes estén listas para actuar, construir poder para los tiempos que se avecinan y defender nuestra educación, atención médica, asistencia nutricional y mucho más.

Juntas, podemos crear un futuro más brillante y esperanzador. ¿Te unes a nosotras?

*****Este evento se llevará a cabo en inglés, con traducción simultánea disponible en español.

How to prepare:
Gracias por registrarte en nuestra próxima reunión comunitaria de MomsRising.

Para unirte a la llamada debes tener una cuenta de Zoom.

Nos reunimos el cuarto jueves de cada mes por Zoom a las 5:30 pm PT / 8:30 pm ET para hablar de temas que son importantes para todas las mamás del país. Los eventos son en inglés, con traducción simultánea al español.

title: Carolina Blues: Indigo on the Cape Fear
location: North Carolina Maritime Museum at Southport
description:
Don’t be blue – we have lots of indigo for you! This subtropical plant was an incredibly important commodity in the Carolinas of the 18th century. We will be exploring the process used by skilled enslaved African labor to extract this valuable dye from the indigo plants - and delve into the science behind dye production. Visitors will have a chance to view and work an active fermentation vat, and dyeing demonstrations will occur throughout the course of the program. T-shirts will also be available for purchase for visitors to try their hand at resist dyeing.

This is a partnered program between Brunswick Town/Fort Anderson State Historic Site and the North Carolina Maritime Museum at Southport. In 2026, this event will be held at the North Carolina Maritime Museum at Southport.

This is an America 250 NC event.

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Downtown Techies Durham and Raleigh MERGE FOR THE SUMMER!
description:
Downtown Techies: Durham
Hey Techies!

We are so excited to see you all again! Heads up! for the summer months we are merging all of our events into an event once a month on the first Tuesday of every month!

Don't forget to RSVP for first round free!!

We will be resuming Downtown Durham events in September on the third Tuesday of every month. We would love your suggestions on venues! Just ping us.

Questions or suggestions?

Our digital door is always open at www.downtowntechies.com or @downtowntechies. Check out our events in Raleigh as well!

Meet, Drink and be TECHIE!

-Jake and Blake

Looking to get more connected to the local tech scene, opportunity or talent? Email us info@downtowntechies.com and we will make sure you find what you need!

title: The Media and Democracy Project - What activists can do: Fix Media Now
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47485924/1:9aec886b0eb7c8ea71cc37babf5169433e9e0920215aa807dff98bc675ba1ae3/

Join the Media and Democracy Project to better understand and respond to the factors that influence public opinion, voters and democracy. Each session we will activate on a current information-related subject to help Fix Media Now.

We develop and distribute skills to navigate the Information War on truth and democracy.

We are a non-partisan all volunteer group that engages in actions to directly improve the quality of public information such as messaging, fighting disinformation, engaging in media accountability and in growing support for local journalism.

We see journalists and the media as accountable allies essential for an informed democracy, countering dangerous disinformation and propaganda and promoting the common good.

How to prepare:
Bring a friend - send them the link.
Activate your social networks.

https://www.mobilize.us/mediademocracyproject/event/430177/

title: Public School Strong Organizing Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46979990/1:84e2f965f1d120cd93fb7dd98f0d0b973c3029e538f77728a4e9c31ba0d1055d/

How to join:
Hi there, 

Thanks for signing up for our Public School Strong Orientation & Organizing Meeting!  

Be sure to register on zoom in advance for this meeting:
https://raceforward-org.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIvcuiurz4pE9BGsfJi-mBBX_lhl2bPG7Ti 

After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting.

**We are thrilled to welcome David Backer, author of As Public As Possible: Radical Finance for America’s Public Schools, as our guest speaker at January’s meeting.** 

Join us for the next Public Schools Strong organizing meeting on Thursday, January 22nd, where parents, family members, educators, and supporters from across the nation gather each month to gain practical tools to promote, protect, and strengthen our public schools. 

*If you have already attended a Public School Strong Orientation, you'll join a breakout room with current members for connection, reflection and training. If you're new, we've got you covered too!*

**Do you want honest, equitable and fully-funded public schools?** Acting locally is key.

### Join public school families, educators and supporters from across the country to learn how to:
1. Form a Public School Strong team in your school district
2. Engage your community to get school boards and district leaders to speak up for equitable and fully funded public schools
3. Connect your local advocacy to others for state and national impact

For more info, please visit publicschoolstrong.org

### Current Public School Strong Members

There is strength in staying connected. After completing Public School Strong (PSS) Orientation, this is your consistent space to reconnect with your peers across the country to:

1. Access helpful tools and practice relevant skills,
2. Connect the dots between what is happening locally and nationally,
3. Learn from mistakes and progress made along the way

Bring a friend!

**Self Directed Orientation Now Available!**

If these live training times don't work for you, you can complete your orientation at your own pace:

1) Create Account in NEA Portal (open to non-NEA members too): 

neapartnera.learnupon.com/users/sign_up

2) Select the PSS orientation by clicking on this link:

bit.ly/SelfDirectedPSS

How to prepare:
### Hi there! Thanks for signing up for a 'Public School Strong Organizing Meeting!' (for new folks we'll also have a new member orientation)

This event is virtual, will provide updates on the federal attacks on our public schools and the immigrant community, actions you can take right now and the tools to support you in doing son. 

For new folks, we'll provide an introduction to our Public School Strong campaign. Check out our organizing platform using this link to find or create a local team: 

- publicschoolstrong.org

More on this powerful tool at your Orientation...

https://www.mobilize.us/raceforward/event/596321/

title: Webinar: Solving the People Problem: Cybersecurity Training 101
description: Does your company have a cybersecurity training program in place? Did you know that the majority of cyber breaches occur due to human error and could be solved with basic cybersecurity awareness training? Sign up for an NC State Industry Expansion Solutions webinar to learn how to put basic cyber training in place today at low or no cost and help solve one of the biggest problems facing small to mid-size businesses today.

title: Port Brunswick Day
description: Join us at Brunswick Town/Fort Anderson State Historic Site for the return of Port Brunswick Day! Living historians will be interpreting various colonial trades and aspects of colonial life, including candle making, blacksmithing, woodworking, and more. Kids will have an opportunity to experience colonial toys and games. This event is free. All ages are welcome.

title: Discover Tryon Palace: Outlander
location: Tryon Palace Historic Sites and Gardens
description:
Step into Tryon Palace and explore Diana Gabaldon’s award-winning Outlander series. Learn about the lives of the fictional Jamie and Claire Fraser, their family and friends, and interactions with historical figures and events in the tumultuous period of the American Revolution.

Tour covers storylines from Outlander books 4 – 8 and the Starz Outlander seasons 4 – 8. (For those not current on the books or show, there will be spoilers for these books and seasons.)

Tour begins at 301 George Street, across from the Tryon Palace Main Gates. Ticket holders should check in at the Waystation ticketing desk no later than 3:30 p.m.

title: Reed Glow Mine
description:
Enjoy a brilliant night time event filled with family fun! 
Activities will include glow panning, hayrides, crafts, and more!  Event may not be suitable for those with light sensitivities. 

Space is limited. Tickets are not available for purchase on site. 
 
Tickets Required for each guest. Tickets on sale September 1. 

6:00pm-8:00pm. $5.00 each.

title: NC State Writes: Celebrating the National Day on Writing
description:
Join the Campus Writing and Speaking Program (CWSP) on Monday, Oct. 20, for NC State Writes, a campuswide celebration of the National Day on Writing. From 9 a.m. to 2 p.m., stop by our pop-up writing stations at the D.H. Hill Jr. Library and Talley Student Union to:

Share your #WhyIWrite storyAdd a line to our collaborative Wolfpack poemTry quick-write prompts to spark creativityPick up free CWSP swagAt 12:30 p.m., join NC State Writes Together for a campuswide writing pause connecting students, faculty and staff through shared reflection and creativity. For 10 minutes, we'll all respond to this question: "What does being part of the Wolfpack mean to you?"

If you can’t join us in person, participate on social media by posting your own #WhyIWrite story with #WolfpackWrites.

All are welcome to participate — because at NC State, we all write.

title: Greater Triangle Coalition Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48393603/1:de31f528fe6629f825338b6f0b779984b9cd9ec5fe85f7e9ba9b4f9a5cdee7e2/

### Join Our Greater Triangle Regional Coalition Meeting

- 📅 **When**: 3rd Wednesday of every month, 6:00 – 7:00 PM
- 📍 **Where**: Virtual (Online)

Democracy NC’s Greater Triangle region covers **Wake, Harnett, and Johnston counties.**

**We come together to:**

- Build strong grassroots leadership
- Equip people to take action
- Serve “the good of the whole”

Whether you’re brand new or already engaged, this space is for learning, sharing, and organizing for **a movement built of, by, and for the people**, grounded in equity, solidarity, and justice.

**A note from your Greater Triangle Regional Managing Organizer:** 
I’m passionate about building community, amplifying voices, and creating spaces where people can be heard and find their role in supporting civic engagement and protecting our democracy. This work keeps me grounded, and I’m excited to invite you to be part of it.

**Join us, share your voice, and help shape the work ahead!**

How to prepare:
Thank you for signing up to attend our monthly coalition meeting!

Each month, you’ll get a reminder with details on what we’ll be discussing and the actions we can take together. We’re excited to stay connected and work alongside you to make an impact.

Hope to see you online!

— Sugelema Lynch

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980101/1:8042c00c946fa76892d4cfc57a4afafffe88c7229dc09474bbb55bb13ecce78b/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Reunión Mensual Comunitaria de MomsRising
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367852/1:1a247a51125d972f31c9b6a39df6efe645d6188fce53fbb3be8dba7a51002a48/

REUNIÓN DEL 29 DE ENERO: 

**¿“Mamás unidas y poderosas”? ¡El verdadero poder de las madres en 2026 (¡Tú!). Únete a MomsRising para una reunión comunitaria por Zoom el jueves 29 de enero a las 8:30 p. m. ET / 5:30 p. m. PT.**


Únete a MomsRising para nuestra primera reunión comunitaria de este 2026. Líderes de nuestro movimiento compartirán actualizaciones sobre temas clave como Medicaid e inmigración, así como vías específicas y estratégicas para alzar nuestra voz y tomar acción.  

¡Porque este año hay que estar más presentes que nunca! Acompáñanos para conectar, inspirarnos y planificar la estrategia que necesitamos para 2026.

Por favor, invita a todas las personas que conozcas a unirse a la reunión, incluyendo a quienes estén listas para actuar, construir poder para los tiempos que se avecinan y defender nuestra educación, atención médica, asistencia nutricional y mucho más.

Juntas, podemos crear un futuro más brillante y esperanzador. ¿Te unes a nosotras?

*****Este evento se llevará a cabo en inglés, con traducción simultánea disponible en español.

How to prepare:
Gracias por registrarte en nuestra próxima reunión comunitaria de MomsRising.

Para unirte a la llamada debes tener una cuenta de Zoom.

Nos reunimos el cuarto jueves de cada mes por Zoom a las 5:30 pm PT / 8:30 pm ET para hablar de temas que son importantes para todas las mamás del país. Los eventos son en inglés, con traducción simultánea al español.



**EL DÍA DE LA REUNIÓN PUEDES UNIRTE AQUÍ:**
https://momsrising.zoom.us/j/87977097086

title: Goldston America 250 Film Series
description:
Join the Chatham Community Library for this Goldston America 250 Film Series, hosted every fourth Tuesday, January-December 2026, 4pm-6pm. America 250’s mission is to honor and commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, marking America’s Semiquincentennial. The Goldston Library will screen the following documentaries and films:

Tue, Jan 27, 4pm-6pm The Congress, Ken Burns.
Tue, Feb 24, 4pm-6pm Becoming Frederick Douglass, Stanley Nelson and Nicole London.
Tue, Mar 24, 4pm-6pm Rachel Carson: The Woman Who Launched the Modern Environmental Movement, Michelle Ferrari.
Tue, Apr 28, 4pm-6pm Thomas Jefferson: A View from the Mountain, Martin Doblmeier.
Tue, May 26, 4pm-6pm The Address, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jun 23, 4pm-6pm The Brooklyn Bridge, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jul 28, 4pm-6pm The Statue of Liberty, Ken Burns.
Tue, Aug 25, 4pm-6pm American Coup: Wilmington 1898, Yoruba Richen and Brad Lichtenstein.
Tue, Sep 22, 4pm-6pm How it Feels to be Free, Yoruba Richen.
Tue, Oct 27, 4pm-6pm 80 Years Later, Celine Parreñas Shimizu.
Tue, Nov 24, 4pm-6pm Gather, Sanjay Rawal.
Tue, Dec 22, 4pm-6pm Women of World War II: The Untold Stories, Eli Brown.

This FREE, family-friendly event is open to the public. No registration is required. The event will take place indoors in the Goldston Library. The Goldston Library is ADA-compliant, including wheelchair-accessible parking, entrances, seating, common spaces, walkways, and restrooms. Free parking is available outside the library. Pets are not allowed - please leave your furry friends at home.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events.

title: Black Voters Matter Texting Tuesdays
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48337781/1:e035b383ece8431b519e5c383c55f435cb1a5ce579a54021d56c9d8b3c85b1f3/

How to join:
You will need at least a basic Zoom account to register.

Join us on Zoom at 6pm ET/5pm CT every week as we gather to drive change through digital organizing. Our texting campaigns will give you the opportunity to engage residents across the U.S. while empowering them to make a positive impact in their communities. Last year, we sent over 19 million texts educating residents on local elections, environmental justice, police reform, health care, and so much more! During the event we will provide training, discuss our latest texting campaign, and we will take action.

A basic Zoom account is required to register.

How to prepare:
Complete your registration here via Zoom: https://bit.ly/BVMTextingTuesday.   

You will need a basic Zoom account to register. A basic Zoom account is free.

Fight Back With Friends! is a national program designed to help you activate your community! Using the Empower app, the powerful relational organizing app, we’ll equip you with the tools to connect with friends, family, and neighbors who need YOUR voice to get civically engaged. Join us on the fourth Tuesday of every month for a one-hour training session, where we’ll onboard new participants and introduce fresh calls to action that make a real impact.

title: Dr. M.T. Pope House Presents: Evelyn's Book Club
description:
This program honors Evelyn Pope by reading books. 

Evelyn, a college librarian born in 1908 and died in 1995, loved a variety of books from The Adventures of Tom Sawyer to Shakespeare and James Baldwin. The program brings community members together monthly at the museum to discuss books, focusing on works by Black writers or featuring Black subjects, including fiction and non-fiction. Discussions will use questions to facilitate conversation.

"Mama Day" by Gloria Naylor
Wednesday, Feb. 25
 "The Black Angels: The Untold Story of the Nurses Who Helped Cure Tuberculosis" by Maria Smilios
Wednesday, April 22
 "A Visitation of Spirits" by Randall Kenan
Wednesday, June 24
 "Becoming Lunsford Lane: The Lives of an American Aeneas" by Craig Friend
Wednesday, Aug. 26
 "Ring Shout" by P. Dejeli Clark
Thursday, Oct. 29Registration opens Jan. 23.  An optional book bundle will be available to purchase at registration. Books can be picked up at the Dr. M.T. Pope House Museum.

title: Downtown Techies Durham and Raleigh MERGE FOR THE SUMMER!
description:
Downtown Techies: Durham
Hey Techies!

We are so excited to see you all again! Heads up! for the summer months we are merging all of our events into an event once a month on the first Tuesday of every month!

Don't forget to RSVP for first round free!!

We will be resuming Downtown Durham events in September on the third Tuesday of every month. We would love your suggestions on venues! Just ping us.

Questions or suggestions?

Our digital door is always open at www.downtowntechies.com or @downtowntechies. Check out our events in Raleigh as well!

Meet, Drink and be TECHIE!

-Jake and Blake

Looking to get more connected to the local tech scene, opportunity or talent? Email us info@downtowntechies.com and we will make sure you find what you need!

title: Homeschool Day at Reed Gold Mine
description:
Dive into a variety of STEAM displays and demonstrations across the site. The highlight of the day is a unique opportunity to step into the shoes of historic miners as you explore 450 feet of restored tunnels, 50 feet below the surface, in our underground mine.

Admission is $4.00 (plus tax) for ages 5 and up and children under 5 are free of charge. Cash and credit card payments inside the Visitor Center (we cannot accept American Express).

Gold panning will be offered, weather permitting, for an additional $4.00 per person (plus tax). 

Registration is not accepted for Homeschool Day.

title: The Great Pumpkin Blowout
description:
Has that jack-o-lantern on your front porch outlived its usefulness? Dispose of him in a unique and rather explosive way by implementing the same technology used to detonate Civil War era torpedoes!
$10 fee per pumpkin and BYOP (bring your own pumpkin). It is free to watch, but YOU get to press the button to detonate if you bring your own! Pumpkins with minimal carving offer the best explosions, but we can work with all types.
Tickets can be purchased at the site on the day of the event.

title: Deep Sky Astronomy
location: Haw River State Park - Iron Ore Belt Access
description:
This event is free to attend, but registration is requested. 

Join local astronomers with the Greensboro Astronomy Club as we observe the night skies over the Iron Ore Belt Access. Attendees may expect to see closer-than-typical views of the moon, planets, star clusters, and other celestial objects subject to change with time of year and local sky darkness.

Telescopes and viewing equipment will be set up on site but you may wish to bring your own binoculars or telescope, plus a chair or blanket for seating. Dress for the weather. Flashlights & headlamps should have a red-light option or filter to protect your night vision.

Skywatching sessions may be cancelled at the last minute for weather. Please register at the Register link so you can be emailed in the event of cancellation.

Program equipment and instruction provided by members of the Greensboro Astronomy Club, which meet the third Friday of each month at the Greensboro Science Center. Learn more about their organization here: 

https://greensboroastronomyclub.org/

Star-gazing Etiquette:

Please use a red light at the observing session and keep any light pointed at the ground. As your eyes adapt, a light is not needed at all.Pease do not touch any telescopes or other equipment unless permission is given and form a orderly line behind each telescope. Please do not allow children or pets to run around the observing area. Pets must be on a leash no longer than 6 feet at all times. This is to protect others' safety and the equipment.Stock photo © Pexels / Lucas Pezeta

title: Public School Strong Organizing Meeting
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46979991/1:00999ed65573c9df5bd32609da02012b4377dd0cd2fcc9ff191926373e81991e/

How to join:
Hi there, 

Thanks for signing up for our Public School Strong Orientation & Organizing Meeting!  

Be sure to register on zoom in advance for this meeting:
https://raceforward-org.zoom.us/meeting/register/tZIvcuiurz4pE9BGsfJi-mBBX_lhl2bPG7Ti 

After registering, you will receive a confirmation email containing information about joining the meeting.

**We are thrilled to welcome David Backer, author of As Public As Possible: Radical Finance for America’s Public Schools, as our guest speaker at January’s meeting.** 

Join us for the next Public Schools Strong organizing meeting on Thursday, January 22nd, where parents, family members, educators, and supporters from across the nation gather each month to gain practical tools to promote, protect, and strengthen our public schools. 

*If you have already attended a Public School Strong Orientation, you'll join a breakout room with current members for connection, reflection and training. If you're new, we've got you covered too!*

**Do you want honest, equitable and fully-funded public schools?** Acting locally is key.

### Join public school families, educators and supporters from across the country to learn how to:
1. Form a Public School Strong team in your school district
2. Engage your community to get school boards and district leaders to speak up for equitable and fully funded public schools
3. Connect your local advocacy to others for state and national impact

For more info, please visit publicschoolstrong.org

### Current Public School Strong Members

There is strength in staying connected. After completing Public School Strong (PSS) Orientation, this is your consistent space to reconnect with your peers across the country to:

1. Access helpful tools and practice relevant skills,
2. Connect the dots between what is happening locally and nationally,
3. Learn from mistakes and progress made along the way

Bring a friend!

**Self Directed Orientation Now Available!**

If these live training times don't work for you, you can complete your orientation at your own pace:

1) Create Account in NEA Portal (open to non-NEA members too): 

neapartnera.learnupon.com/users/sign_up

2) Select the PSS orientation by clicking on this link:

bit.ly/SelfDirectedPSS

How to prepare:
### Hi there! Thanks for signing up for a 'Public School Strong Organizing Meeting!' (for new folks we'll also have a new member orientation)

This event is virtual, will provide updates on the federal attacks on our public schools and the immigrant community, actions you can take right now and the tools to support you in doing son. 

For new folks, we'll provide an introduction to our Public School Strong campaign. Check out our organizing platform using this link to find or create a local team: 

- publicschoolstrong.org

More on this powerful tool at your Orientation...

title: Battleship North Carolina Pearl Harbor Day and Veterans Day Remembrances
description:
Pearl Harbor Day and Veterans Day Remembrances

 

Operating aboard Battleship NORTH CAROLINA, the Azalea Coast Radio Club hosts worldwide HAM radio operator events commemorating:  

The December 7, 1941 attack on Pearl HarborVeterans DayThe Battleship is NI4BK, and HAM operators may contact the ship via Voice, Digital, and Morse Code. NI4BK looks forward to hearing from YOU!

Radio operators making contact will receive a special postcard verifying the contact.

title: Team ENOUGH Welcome Session (For Young People Under 26)
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/46980104/1:ef0f7bb67e7bebdd1c867e0f35e1efbaa3f0f4b382cb638bcec020b63221178a/

How to join:
Please join our virtual welcome session with this link

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

How to prepare:
We are excited to see you soon!

P.S. This is a call for young people from 13-25 years old who are interested in getting engaged with the Team ENOUGH program. If you are over the age of 25 please sign up for the Brady Welcome Session at https://www.mobilize.us/brady/event/274016/

title: Hidden Battleship Tour
description:
Hidden Battleship is for the explorer at heart! Join us for an afternoon on a unique, behind-the-scenes tour of unrestored areas on the Battleship. Organized into small groups, the participants are led to areas in the bow, third deck, and below, and the superstructure, including all the way to the very top! This program requires climbing ladders, crossing high hatches, and going through tight/confined spaces. The tour is limited to ages 16 and older.

2026 Dates

► January 24
► February 28
► March 28
► April 18
► November 14

11 am – 5 pm; $75 (plus tax) per participant. This program always sells out, so get your Hidden Battleship ticket now! This program is for ages 16 and older.

title: Indigenous Cooking in the Historical Park
description: Join us as we celebrate Native American Heritage Month with historic foodways! Our costumed interpreters will cook dishes and discuss historic methods of native cooking. Food samples are unavailable to the public. Demonstration only. FREE. All ages welcome. Drop-in.

title: Honoring Veterans: Lecture & Patriotic Musical
description: The public is invited to “Honoring Veterans – America 250 Patriotic Musical” on Saturday, November 14, at 3:00 p.m. at Bethlehem Baptist Church. Dr. Warren Hollar will discuss “Alexander County History.” Kim Pennell and The Barnes Family will provide entertainment.

title: Wreath Laying in Honor of Governor Richard Caswell and his son General William Caswell
description: The SAR and DAR will sponsor the Wreath Laying and Cemetery Rededication at 11:00 AM, at the Governor Richard Caswell Memorial, 2612 W. Vernon Ave, Kinston, NC 28504. A Living History event will be held by N.C. Historic Sites from 9 AM to 4 PM.

title: The Media and Democracy Project - What activists can do: Fix Media Now
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47485925/1:7fc3022297cfd609c9f89fbe4c673c97de78f91dd25e9b7b74cc60052370d37d/

Join the Media and Democracy Project to better understand and respond to the factors that influence public opinion, voters and democracy. Each session we will activate on a current information-related subject to help Fix Media Now. 

We develop and distribute skills to navigate the Information War on truth and democracy. 

We are a non-partisan all volunteer group that engages in actions to directly improve the quality of public information such as messaging, fighting disinformation, engaging in media accountability and in growing support for local journalism. 

We see journalists and the media as accountable allies essential for an informed democracy, countering dangerous disinformation and propaganda and promoting the common good.

How to prepare:
Bring a friend - send them the link. 
Activate your social networks.

title: Natural Heritage Advisory Committee Meeting _ November 2026
location: Central Piedmont- location to be announced at a later date
description: Committee will consider proposals for dedicated nature preserves and registered heritage areas.  This regular meeting of the Natural Heritage Advisory Committee will be an in-person meeting with a virtual option.  Any interested members of the public who wish to participate may contact natural.heritage@dncr.nc.gov to request a meeting link or directions.  The location and time for meeting will be determined at a later date.

title: Department of Defense Contracting and CMMC Webinar
description:
Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification (CMMC) compliance is required for all Department of Defense contracts. NC State Industry Expansion Solutions will host a webinar on how to become CMMC compliant, how long it takes to become compliant and how much it costs. You should attend the webinar if your company is a Department of Defense contractor or plans to become a contractor. Participants will learn:

CMMC is a law, not an optional standard.CMMC certification is not an overnight project. CMMC certification is not an overnight project. You need to act quickly, or you will risk losing Department of Defense contracts.

description:

title: Open Hearth Cooking Demonstration in the Historical Park
description: Learn how early Americans prepared and cooked for large dinner parties like what most of us experience during the Thanksgiving holiday. Our costumed interpreters will demonstrate how to cook a fall harvest meal over an open hearth in the Hoggatt House. Food samples are unavailable to the public. Demonstration only. FREE. All ages welcome. Drop-in.

title: Discover Tryon Palace: Outlander
location: Tryon Palace Historic Sites and Gardens
description:
Step into Tryon Palace and explore Diana Gabaldon’s award-winning Outlander series. Learn about the lives of the fictional Jamie and Claire Fraser, their family and friends, and interactions with historical figures and events in the tumultuous period of the American Revolution.

Tour covers storylines from Outlander books 4 – 8 and the Starz Outlander seasons 4 – 8. (For those not current on the books or show, there will be spoilers for these books and seasons.)

Tour begins at 301 George Street, across from the Tryon Palace Main Gates. Ticket holders should check in at the Waystation ticketing desk no later than 3:30 p.m.

title: Goldston America 250 Film Series
description:
Join the Chatham Community Library for this Goldston America 250 Film Series, hosted every fourth Tuesday, January-December 2026, 4pm-6pm. America 250’s mission is to honor and commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, marking America’s Semiquincentennial. The Goldston Library will screen the following documentaries and films:

Tue, Jan 27, 4pm-6pm The Congress, Ken Burns.
Tue, Feb 24, 4pm-6pm Becoming Frederick Douglass, Stanley Nelson and Nicole London.
Tue, Mar 24, 4pm-6pm Rachel Carson: The Woman Who Launched the Modern Environmental Movement, Michelle Ferrari.
Tue, Apr 28, 4pm-6pm Thomas Jefferson: A View from the Mountain, Martin Doblmeier.
Tue, May 26, 4pm-6pm The Address, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jun 23, 4pm-6pm The Brooklyn Bridge, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jul 28, 4pm-6pm The Statue of Liberty, Ken Burns.
Tue, Aug 25, 4pm-6pm American Coup: Wilmington 1898, Yoruba Richen and Brad Lichtenstein.
Tue, Sep 22, 4pm-6pm How it Feels to be Free, Yoruba Richen.
Tue, Oct 27, 4pm-6pm 80 Years Later, Celine Parreñas Shimizu.
Tue, Nov 24, 4pm-6pm Gather, Sanjay Rawal.
Tue, Dec 22, 4pm-6pm Women of World War II: The Untold Stories, Eli Brown.

This FREE, family-friendly event is open to the public. No registration is required. The event will take place indoors in the Goldston Library. The Goldston Library is ADA-compliant, including wheelchair-accessible parking, entrances, seating, common spaces, walkways, and restrooms. Free parking is available outside the library. Pets are not allowed - please leave your furry friends at home.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events.

title: Reunión Mensual Comunitaria de MomsRising
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/48367853/1:33f06bfcd9b24f8abde64e8aaeb08666b1c1b90b3206b6aba99c35bb5ed6706f/

REUNIÓN DEL 29 DE ENERO: 

**¿“Mamás unidas y poderosas”? ¡El verdadero poder de las madres en 2026 (¡Tú!). Únete a MomsRising para una reunión comunitaria por Zoom el jueves 29 de enero a las 8:30 p. m. ET / 5:30 p. m. PT.**


Únete a MomsRising para nuestra primera reunión comunitaria de este 2026. Líderes de nuestro movimiento compartirán actualizaciones sobre temas clave como Medicaid e inmigración, así como vías específicas y estratégicas para alzar nuestra voz y tomar acción.  

¡Porque este año hay que estar más presentes que nunca! Acompáñanos para conectar, inspirarnos y planificar la estrategia que necesitamos para 2026.

Por favor, invita a todas las personas que conozcas a unirse a la reunión, incluyendo a quienes estén listas para actuar, construir poder para los tiempos que se avecinan y defender nuestra educación, atención médica, asistencia nutricional y mucho más.

Juntas, podemos crear un futuro más brillante y esperanzador. ¿Te unes a nosotras?

*****Este evento se llevará a cabo en inglés, con traducción simultánea disponible en español.

How to prepare:
Gracias por registrarte en nuestra próxima reunión comunitaria de MomsRising.

Para unirte a la llamada debes tener una cuenta de Zoom.

Nos reunimos el cuarto jueves de cada mes por Zoom a las 5:30 pm PT / 8:30 pm ET para hablar de temas que son importantes para todas las mamás del país. Los eventos son en inglés, con traducción simultánea al español.



**EL DÍA DE LA REUNIÓN PUEDES UNIRTE AQUÍ:**

title: Downtown Techies Durham and Raleigh MERGE FOR THE SUMMER!
description:
Downtown Techies: Durham
Hey Techies!

We are so excited to see you all again! Heads up! for the summer months we are merging all of our events into an event once a month on the first Tuesday of every month!

Don't forget to RSVP for first round free!!

We will be resuming Downtown Durham events in September on the third Tuesday of every month. We would love your suggestions on venues! Just ping us.

Questions or suggestions?

Our digital door is always open at www.downtowntechies.com or @downtowntechies. Check out our events in Raleigh as well!

Meet, Drink and be TECHIE!

Are you a young person between 13 and 25 years old who is interested in taking action around preventing gun violence? Join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session to learn more about the different ways you can engage. 

Team ENOUGH is a youth-led program whose mission is to educate young voices about gun violence and mobilize them to take meaningful action against it. Supported by Brady United, we harness the expertise and experience of today’s youth to advance our intersectional work across the country. 

Led by an Executive Council, comprised of students just like us, we believe that diversity is a strength, and that only when everyone has a seat at the table can we build an effective movement to prevent all gun violence in all communities.

For us, preventing gun violence not only requires regulating access to firearms but also addressing how systemic racism and environmental factors cause this form of bloodshed to run especially rampant in communities of color. You cannot talk about gun violence without talking about police brutality, white supremacy, the broken criminal justice system, poverty, homophobia, access to affordable housing, or the often barriers separating disadvantaged populations from physical and mental healthcare. 

So if you share this belief, join us for our Team ENOUGH welcome session and become part of the movement to end gun violence in our lifetime.

title: Yuletide by Lanternlight
description:
Experience an authentic Christmas of long ago in the warm glow of lantern light.  Enjoy homes, public buildings, and taverns decorated for the season with festive, all-natural décor.  Interactive historical vignettes will bring each building to life.  Enjoy a warm fire in the Tap Room tavern, just as guests did two hundred years ago! Tickets go on sale Nov. 1st.  

 

Fee, ticket required.

title: Santa at the Lake
description: It's the 2nd Annual Santa at the Lake! Bring your Christmas wish lists, little ones, and fury friends for the perfect photo down at the Swim Beach with Santa! We have activities set up for kids and Friends of Lake Norman will be taking donations for sweet treats and warm drinks at our concessions stands.

title: Battleship North Carolina Pearl Harbor Day and Veterans Day Remembrances
description:
Pearl Harbor Day and Veterans Day Remembrances

 

Operating aboard Battleship NORTH CAROLINA, the Azalea Coast Radio Club hosts worldwide HAM radio operator events commemorating:  

The December 7, 1941 attack on Pearl HarborVeterans DayThe Battleship is NI4BK, and HAM operators may contact the ship via Voice, Digital, and Morse Code. NI4BK looks forward to hearing from YOU!

Radio operators making contact will receive a special postcard verifying the contact.

title: Christmas at Historic Bath
description:
Join us as we look at Christmas traditions during the colonial period in the Palmer Marsh House and into the early Victorian period in the Bonner House. Christmas décor of the time periods, vignettes, live music, and light refreshments. 

Use of Palmer-Marsh, Palmer Marsh yard, Bonner House and Kitchen

title: An 18th Century Christmas
description:
Join us once again this holiday season, as we explore how the American colonists celebrated Christmas. During the program, you can enjoy 18th-century-inspired refreshments in the Visitor Center, participate in traditional games, or try your hand at Moravian paper stars and other 18th-century crafts. The highlight of the evening will be an authentic
period candlelit service in the ruins of St. Philip's Anglican Church. The service will begin at 5:00pm and will conclude by 5:30pm. Tickets can be purchased at the gate on the day of.
$5 per person.

title: The Media and Democracy Project - What activists can do: Fix Media Now
description:
Link to join: https://www.mobilize.us/join/47485926/1:688a1547ef61b2763aaa1bfb91bf427569b4103404704185a440f7b9dac91e5a/

Join the Media and Democracy Project to better understand and respond to the factors that influence public opinion, voters and democracy. Each session we will activate on a current information-related subject to help Fix Media Now. 

We develop and distribute skills to navigate the Information War on truth and democracy. 

We are a non-partisan all volunteer group that engages in actions to directly improve the quality of public information such as messaging, fighting disinformation, engaging in media accountability and in growing support for local journalism. 

We see journalists and the media as accountable allies essential for an informed democracy, countering dangerous disinformation and propaganda and promoting the common good.

How to prepare:
Bring a friend - send them the link. 
Activate your social networks.

https://www.mobilize.us/mediademocracyproject/event/430177/

title: Scottish Faire in Historical Park
description: Prepare to celebrate and enjoy festive Scottish traditions for New Year’s Eve (Hogmanay) and the Yule season. We encourage you to dress in your family tartan or plaid! Food not available to the public. FREE. All ages welcome. Drop-in.

title: National Wreaths Across America Day
description: The local observance of National Wreaths Across America Day is planned for Saturday, December 19, at 12:00 noon at the Taylorsville Cemetery. title: Goldston America 250 Film Series
description:
Join the Chatham Community Library for this Goldston America 250 Film Series, hosted every fourth Tuesday, January-December 2026, 4pm-6pm. America 250’s mission is to honor and commemorate the 250th anniversary of the signing of the Declaration of Independence, marking America’s Semiquincentennial. The Goldston Library will screen the following documentaries and films:

Tue, Jan 27, 4pm-6pm The Congress, Ken Burns.
Tue, Feb 24, 4pm-6pm Becoming Frederick Douglass, Stanley Nelson and Nicole London.
Tue, Mar 24, 4pm-6pm Rachel Carson: The Woman Who Launched the Modern Environmental Movement, Michelle Ferrari.
Tue, Apr 28, 4pm-6pm Thomas Jefferson: A View from the Mountain, Martin Doblmeier.
Tue, May 26, 4pm-6pm The Address, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jun 23, 4pm-6pm The Brooklyn Bridge, Ken Burns.
Tue, Jul 28, 4pm-6pm The Statue of Liberty, Ken Burns.
Tue, Aug 25, 4pm-6pm American Coup: Wilmington 1898, Yoruba Richen and Brad Lichtenstein.
Tue, Sep 22, 4pm-6pm How it Feels to be Free, Yoruba Richen.
Tue, Oct 27, 4pm-6pm 80 Years Later, Celine Parreñas Shimizu.
Tue, Nov 24, 4pm-6pm Gather, Sanjay Rawal.
Tue, Dec 22, 4pm-6pm Women of World War II: The Untold Stories, Eli Brown.

This FREE, family-friendly event is open to the public. No registration is required. The event will take place indoors in the Goldston Library. The Goldston Library is ADA-compliant, including wheelchair-accessible parking, entrances, seating, common spaces, walkways, and restrooms. Free parking is available outside the library. Pets are not allowed - please leave your furry friends at home.

This event is part of our Chatham Celebrates America 250 initiative. See more America 250 events.

title: Building an AI powered, global scale messaging system with XMPP &Deep Learning
description:
Triangle AI Meetup
This talk is an in-depth look at XMPP, the open standard for real-time messaging, and how to build a global scale, AI enhanced messaging system using XMPP. We’ll explore EJabberD, the massively scalable, open-source XMPP server that powers in-game chat for Eve Online, and examine how to: combine XMPP with WebRTC for real-time audio/video calling, add AIML to build helpful chatbots, and how to incorporate Deep Learning to categorize messages in real-time. After the talk, you’ll know how to use XMPP and Ejabberd to build anything from a help-desk chatops application, to a globe spanning in-game messaging system.
